observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, pertaining to stale checks and make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to: a.1 Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records in accordance with Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; a.2 Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS as provided in Section 121 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and a.3 Conduct of periodic physical inventory of supplies or properties and reconcile the RPCI to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of Inventory account balances. b. Require the GSO designated to: b.1 Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the original RIS, to the Municipal Accountant as basis of JEV preparation to record the expenditures based on the cost of the supplies and materials issued; and b.2 Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type, prepare the RPCI, and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Require Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on 14 PPE accounts totaling ₱426.595 million and submit an updated RPCPPE; b. Direct the Accounting and Property Units to reconcile their records to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in the PPELCs and the Property Cards (PCs) within ten (10) days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee, to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts; c. Comply with the uniform property identification system and prominently show in the property sticker the PPEs vital information to include: description of the property, model number, serial number, acquisition date/cost, person accountable, space for the validation/signature of the Inventory Committee; and d. Require the Municipal GSO to prepare and maintain the PCs to facilitate the required regular reconciliation of the PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the General Supply Officer to prepare Report on Local Road Network and conduct Physical Count of the Road Network annually; and b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer complete description and cost segregation of road components for road project.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to: 1. Provide the Municipal Accountant with certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index as a basis of recording the RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income; and 2. Intensify the collection of RPT/SET Receivable to include delinquent accounts by providing tax amnesty and post at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the municipal hall pursuant to Section 254 of the RA 7160; b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: 1. Establish and record RPT Receivable and SET Receivable, at the beginning of the year, based on the certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index for the current year provided by the Municipal Treasurer using the following entries: Setting-up of RPT/SET Receivable Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable xxx.xx Deferred Real Property Taxes Income/ Deferred Special Education Tax Income xxx.xx Receipt of Payment Cash in Treasury xxx.xx Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable xxx.xx Recognition of Income and Distribution of Collection (Current Year’s Collection) Deferred Real Property Tax Income/ Deferred Special Education Tax Income xxx.xx Discount on RPT/SET (for prompt payment) xxx.xx Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax xxx.xx Fines and Penalties xxx.xx Due to LGUs xxx.xx

1. The Property and Accounting Units perform the preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking and reconcile their records as set out in COA Circular No. 2020-006.


2. The Inventory Committee prepares and submits to the Audit Team the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory activities and attach property stickers on the counted PPEs.

3. For the reconciling items before CY 2020, the Municipal Accountant investigates and determines the causes and veracity of the reported reconciling items and take the appropriate actions to address each one including making representations with the bank for the furnishing of the original copies of debit and credit advices or bank statements.

9. Retrieve the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to identified Accounts Payable accounts and review if these are valid commitments and obligations of the LGU;


10. For those accounts with no supporting documents including those which have been outstanding for more than 2 years, prepare the following adjusting entry to reverse the non-existing payable balances:
(Dr.) Accounts Payable		xxx
(Cr.) Government Equity		xxx


11. Trace and reconcile the existence of negative balances under the Accounts Payable accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books to correct the balances.

19. The Municipal Assessor reviews the Assessment Roll and correct the above-noted discrepancies concerning properties with duplicate/identical Property Identification Number, properties with zero market value, and properties with zero assessed value.


20. Updated data on real properties within the Municipality be submitted to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation/updating of the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s index card, copy furnished to the Audit Team.

29. The Municipal Accountant issues demand letters to the recipient of fund transfers for the submission of liquidation reports or settlement of receivables.


30. Management discontinues the practice of granting fund transfers to the Implementing Agencies with unliquidated balances and a certification by the accountant be required for succeeding transfers.

31. The local chief executive requires the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.

32. Management submits proof of creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.


33. Further, Management initiates the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.

36. The Municipal Accountant transfers the PPE accounts from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and take up the depreciation of these accounts from prior years’ 2016 to CY 2019 so that the correct balances will be reported in the financial statements.

37. Management directs the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts under Due to GSIS, Pag-ibig and PhilHealth are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these payable accounts.


38. Further, make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to reconcile the negative/abnormal balances of the account.

39. Management requires the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).


40. Management requests approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
41. Management instructs the Municipal Treasurer to speed up the titling of these lands in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants.

42. Management conducts immediate follow-ups to facilitate the issuances of title in its name, otherwise, cancel the transaction and request for the return of the payment from the lot owner.


43. Management requests for an SB Resolution confirming the purchase made.

1. Investigate the cause/s of the non-conduct of the physical count of PPE and undertake measures to eventually prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for evaluation.
2. Prepare and maintain depreciation schedules respectively, for each item of PPE to substantiate the balances presented in the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 1.

3. Reconcile their respective records of Cash in Local Treasury as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and conduct the subsequent reconciliation regularly in order to timely effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments and consistently harmonize the balances at year-end.

4. Reconcile their respective records of Cash in Bank account as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments to present accurate cash in bank balances at year-end.

7. Submit a written explanation on the purchase of reconditioned Boom Truck despite the prohibition stipulated in DBM Budget Circular (BC) No. 2022-1 dated February 11, 2022 and Item 5.3 of DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2022-15 dated August 23, 2022. Thus, this is considered as an irregular expenditure and the objectives of efficiency and economy in the use of government funds were not achieved.
9. Secure the ECC or Certificate of Non-Coverage of the said project and submit it to the Audit Team for verification or a written explanation of why the same could not be submitted.

10. Expedite the implementation of the above-mentioned projects and monitor implementation to ensure effective and efficient project implementation and reporting.
11. Ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.
12. Submit a written explanation on the non-conduct of public/competitive bidding for the procurement of meals for the LGU Budgeting Conference in CY 2023.

14. Establish RPT/SET Receivables in the books at the beginning of the year based on the duly certified list of all taxpayers obtained from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, in order to present reliable and accurate balances in the financial statements.

15. Return the unspent balances to the SA, or Recovery Facility and request approval from the SA to transfer the unexpended balances to the General Fund if the disposition thereof has not been provided in the MOA, Trust Agreement Memorandum of Understanding and close the respective bank account maintained to avoid the incurrence of possible dormancy fees/charges in the ensuing year.

17. Submit its legal basis on the inclusion of lot acquisition under the Build and Design Scheme, for evaluation by the Audit Team, and to appropriately make manifestation with the National Housing Authority on the noted deficiency in the aforementioned Terms of Reference.

22. Intensify the implementation of the projects funded by financial assistance in order to timely achieve the desired benefits.
23. Also, make representation with the respective donors of the financial assistance with unutilized balances and seek authority to use the fund balance to finance other projects/programs of the agency.

25. Coordinate with the daycare centers and conduct inventory of the textbooks and instructional materials and submit the inventory report to the Accounting Office to support the amount of inventory recorded in the books.
26. The Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to comply the documentary requirements for the write off of the Loans Receivable account for verification and evaluation.

27. Establish an Internal Audit Service Office with sufficient support from the top management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO. No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of P.D. 1445.

29. Return to the National Treasury the unutilized /unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.

30. Require the Disposal Committee to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties to the directives of the Municipality contrary to Sections 379, 380 and 381 of RA 7160.
31. Monitor the status of agricultural produce for distribution and breeding stocks and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the review and analysis of the details of the accounts.

32. Impose the bonding requirements of collectors and assign back to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office the Revenue Collection Clerk who was assigned at the Mayor Office’s to maximize her services. Also, stop the practice of assigning the tasks of collecting to personnel hired as casual / job orders.
33. Refund the cost of gasoline issued to privately owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.

34. Require the Municipal Livestock Coordinator, who is in – charge of the dispersal program of the municipality, to monitor the project, prepare and submit a report regularly to the Accounting Division on any increase in the livestock inventory due to returns from recipients as well as the mortality duly supported with an affidavit and witnessed by any of the barangay officials to ensure the reliability, accuracy and validity of the recorded amount in the financial statement.

We recommended and Management agreed to develop and implement a
standardized methodology for measuring impairment allowances to ensure the
consistent application of recognizing allowances for impairment in receivables.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer (i)
publish yearly the notice of delinquency once a week for two consecutive weeks in a
newspaper of general circulation; and (ii) provide information for the reason/s of non-
collection or non-payment in the certified list of all RPT delinquencies submitted to
the Sanggunian.

We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal General
Services Officer facilitate the speedy processing of the titling of lots in the name of the
MGP.

We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal General
Services Officer with the members of the Disposal Committee undertake disposal of
unserviceable properties.

We recommended and Management agreed that the –
a) MBO (i) plan for the adequate funding for earned leave credits; and (ii)
realign the budget for earned leave credits erroneously charged to OPB to TLB;
b) HRMO continue updating the leave balances of officials and employees to
provide a reasonable estimate of LBP for each reporting period and submit this to the
MAO; and
c) MAO use the account “Terminal Leave Benefits” pertaining to
payments and accrual of accumulated leave credits of government
officials and employees.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
adjust the affected accounts to reflect the correct classification of the unspent DRRM
funds received from the Makati LGU, as follows:
Account Title Debit Credit
Due to LGUs ₱500,000.00
Trust Liabilities - DRRMF ₱500,000.00

We recommended that Management –
a) post quarterly reports on fund utilization and the status of program/project
implementation using the prescribed format (Annex C) on the LGU's website
within twenty (20) calendar days from the end of each quarter as prescribed
in Part 5.4 of LBC No. 151 dated March 30, 2023;
b) advise the MPDO and/or MEO to (i) conduct a thorough assessment of the
project site to ensure alignment with the project’s primary objectives; (ii)
future site selections must comply with program guidelines and include proper
planning to maximize public fund efficiency and socioeconomic benefits; and
(iii) coordinate with the DILG and relevant agencies to resolve right-of-way
issues promptly and prevent similar delays;
c) instruct the BAC and BAC-TWG to strictly comply with the updated IRR of
RA 9184 by thoroughly verifying, validating, and documenting all technical,
financial, and legal requirements during post-qualification. Use only the latest
prescribed forms and ensure assessments or checklists are comprehensive to
prevent awarding contracts to unqualified bidders and to uphold the integrity
of the procurement process; and
d) require the immediate submission of the DOLE-approved CSHP prior to
project implementation.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue its compliance with
DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No.1 on the appropriation and utilization of funds.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Engineer
issue a Report of Final Inspection and Certificate of Final Acceptance with date
indicated therein and for future implementation, require the contractor to post
performance security with validity until issuance of the Certificate of Final
Acceptance of the project.
We also recommended that the MEO establish a monitoring tool and
documentation to support the Report of Final Inspection and Certificate of
Completion and Acceptance.

We recommended and Management agreed through the HRMO:
a) Undertake appropriate measures to review and align the daily wage rates of
job order and contractual workers with the applicable minimum standards set
by DBM;
b) Improve the terms of reference and future consultancy contracts by specifying
clearly defined outputs that are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and
time-bound to ensure objective performance monitoring and evaluation;
c) Conduct a thorough assessment of the manpower needs (the number of
personnel per office, both permanent, casual, contractual, job orders, and
consultants, if any) of the MGP, to determine the appropriate balance between
plantilla positions, casual, contractual, job orders, and consultants, if any; and
d) Evaluate financial sustainability of hiring casual, contractual, job orders, and
consultants versus creating plantilla positions.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Require that future contracts explicitly include provisions for waste disposal,
such as designated sites, necessary permits, and documentation, in accordance
with Section 17.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
b. Through the Municipal Engineer strengthen the monitoring and
documentation system by ensuring that the desilting projects, including
manpower and equipment utilization and desilting output (e.g., volume of silt
extracted), are properly recorded and supported with verifiable documents.
c. Through the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer conduct a physical inspection or validation for future
and attach the inspection reports supported with complete documentation,
including the volume of extracted silt in the river in the disbursement voucher.

We recommended Management agreed that the–
a) BPLO (i) ensure the accuracy of the marginal tax brackets and allowable
deductions as prescribed in the Revised Municipal Revenue Code; and (ii)
pending the full implementation of the business tax payment system, ensure that
the personnel involved in tax assessments are provided guidance on the correct
application of revised tax brackets, applicable threshold deductions, and
assessment procedures under the current Municipal Revenue Code; and
b) Municipal Treasury Office (MTO) conduct examinations of business taxpayers’
books of accounts in accordance with the Revised Municipal Revenue Code and
undertake the necessary procedures to rectify any over or underassessment
made, ensuring proper documentation of all adjustments.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BPLO to –
a) strictly enforce the timely submission of certified copies of ITRs, Audited
FS, or equivalent documents from all business permit holders on or before
April 30 of each year, as mandated by the Revised Municipal Revenue
Code. Furthermore, these documents should be promptly forwarded to the
MTO for proper review and verification in support of accurate business
tax assessment and collection; and
b) properly maintain copies of proof of income submitted by business permit
holders to ensure the completeness and integrity of tax records.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (i)
review the SLs for discrepancies and reconcile them with the pertinent records of the
MTO or BPLO showing the paid business taxes; (ii) implement controls to prevent
data loss and errors; and (iii) regularly coordinate with the MTO to verify collections
and correct discrepancies.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS-TWG: (a) require
the use of HGDG tool by including it in the official memorandum for GPB
preparation; and b) integrate compliance monitoring of HGDG tool usage into the GPB
review process.

We commended the Management for its continuous strict adherence with
existing revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR. We
recommended that it sustain continuous compliance with existing revenue regulations
to avoid penalties which may be imposed for the violation thereof.

We commended the Management for its continued strict compliance with RA
No. 8291 in the prompt remittance of the premiums deducted from the salaries of its
employees and the government share. We recommended that Management continue
to being compliant with the requirements of the GSIS Act.

We commended the Management specially the MAO and MTO for its
continued compliance with the HDMF Law in the prompt remittance of contributions
and other amount due to Pag-IBIG. We recommended that Management continue its
regular compliance to the HDMF regulations.

We commended the Management on the prompt remittance of the amount due
to PhilHealth. We recommended that Management sustain its compliance to the
PhilHealth regulations.

We recommended that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the
settlement of the disallowances.

Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed, to continue the conduct of the physical inventory of PPE and submit the RPCPPE within the prescribed period and reconcile it with the accounting office’s record.

Nevertheless, we recommended and Management agreed to continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the MGP’s funds while limiting expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid cash deficiency.
Nonetheless, we recommended and Management agreed to adjust the stale checks and continue to follow strictly the existing rules and regulations regarding cash management.

We commended Management for their remarkable determination in incessantly ensuring full settlement/liquidation of the CAs granted.  Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to continuously follow strictly the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of the CAs.

However, we recommend that Management:
Continue to retain copies of MOA or any trust agreements and furnish the AT for proper monitoring of the utilization and evaluation of compliance with its provisions; and
Direct the responsible Department/Office to expedite the implementation of the unimplemented PPAs to optimally utilize the readily available funds received from SAs within the agreed timeframe, thus ensuring timely delivery of the intended benefits to the constituents.

Nonetheless, we recommend that Management:
Continue to adopt strategies to achieve full implementation of the targeted PPAs and maximized utilization of the 20% DF for the substantial achievement of the purpose for which said fund was established/received; and
Consider the additional requirements in prioritizing the implementation of PPAs so that delays may be prevented and the same will be completed within the planned time frames.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MDRRMO to continue to monitor closely the implementation of the targeted PPAs and the utilization of the LDRRMF to facilitate timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization.

Nevertheless, we recommended that the GFPS and GFP:
Coordinate closely with the Municipal Budget Officer and other concerned Department Heads in monitoring the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure correctness of the reported GAD accomplishments and fund utilization; and
Submit a revised or supplemental GPB to the concerned DILG office in case additional GAD PPAs were identified and/or implemented, or supplemental funds were appropriated.
We also recommended that the Department Heads monitor the implementation of their targeted PPAs to facilitate the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the allotted fund, if needed, to other prioritized PPAs to maximize the utilization thereof.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to continuously submit the FS within the reglementary period of submission of the same.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to continue to remit all withheld taxes within the prescribed period as set by the BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of GSIS contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of RA No. 8291.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the MHRMO to submit to the MAO the lacking HDMF numbers of the job order personnel with unremitted contributions.  Otherwise, require the job order employees to apply for Pag-IBIG MID and inform immediately the MAO of the MID for remittance purposes; and
Comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on deductions and remittance of HDMF contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679
Management commented that the MHRMO will forward to the Municipal Accounting Office the MID of job order personnel and will include PAG IBIG MID as one of the requirements in the hiring process.  Also, Management promised to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on deductions and remittance of HDMF contributions

We recommended and Management agreed to comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 7875 as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606.
We recommended and Management agreed to conduct an inventory of supplies and materials and submit the RPCI to COA as prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Property Office to maintain the SPC and the RegSPI and the MAO to prepare and maintain the SPLC in accordance with the provisions of COA Circulars Nos. 2022-004 and 2024-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Call the attention of the Disposal and Appraisal Committee to handle the procedures in the disposal of unserviceable properties;
Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the provisions of the Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties and other issuances related thereto; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to assist the Disposal and Appraisal Committee in preparing the Inventory and IIRUP by providing necessary information.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The accrued interest payable and the accrued interest expense as at year end be recognized in the FS;
The amount of interest expense recognized in CY 2025 that pertains to December 18 to 31, 2024, be adjusted to correct the total interest expense for the year 2025; and
The accounting and budget records reconcile the discrepancy between the total loan payments and the amount obligated.

Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed that:
The MAO to make the necessary adjustment to correct the entry; and
Utilize the savings or balance from the implementation of CSS NC II based on the instruction of TESDA that the fund be utilized for training-related purposes.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to Sections 22 and 23 of the amendment to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292 per CSC MC No. 41, s. 1998 and CSC MC No. 16 s. 2002 on the guidelines on Monetization of Leave Credits.
We also recommended that the HRMO:
Exercise prudence in recommending approval for the grant of leave monetization;
Ensure that officials and employees availing of monetization of VL credits have accumulated no less than 15 days, in which case they can monetize no more than 10 to 30 days, and that five days of VL credits will remain after monetization which shall be availed only once a year;
Strictly follow the conditions prescribed in Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave in case officials and employees choose to avail of the special monetization; and
Stop allowing the practice of monetizing SL or a combination of vacation and sick leave credit balances.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the validity and completeness of the supporting documents before processing any payments for leave monetization.

1.7. We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Establish RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year using
the duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and
collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer's Index Cards
submitted by the Municipal Treasurer;
b. Prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the
correct balances of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2024;
c. Maintain and regularly update Subsidiary Ledgers for RPT/SET
Receivables to support the balances reported in the financial
statements, in accordance with sound accounting practices and
applicable regulations.

1.8. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-012 dated June 18, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

2.10. We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a.1 Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records in
accordance with Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs, Volume 1;
a.2 Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their
appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense
only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI
together with the original copy of the RIS as provided in
Section 121 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1;
and
a.3 Conduct of periodic physical inventory of supplies or
properties and reconcile the RPCI to ascertain the accuracy
and reliability of Inventory account balances.
b. Require the GSO designate to:
b.1 Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials
were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along
with the original RIS, to the Municipal Accountant as basis of
JEV preparation to record the expenditures based on the cost
of the supplies and materials issued; and
b.2 Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by
type, prepare the RPCI, and submit to the Auditor concerned
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the
first and second semesters, respectively.

2.11. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-006 dated June 11, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

3.13. We recommended and the Management agreed to require the
Inventory Committee to:
a. Prepare a PIP in coordination with the GSO and Accounting
Office, and submit the PIP approved by the Municipal Mayor to
the Audit Team at least 10 CD before the scheduled start of
inventory taking activities;
b. Conduct a comprehensive and timely physical inventory of all PPE
items in accordance with the approved PIP, enlisting support from
technical staff and concerned offices to ensure completeness,
accuracy and proper documentation;
c. Reconcile the results of the physical count with the property and
accounting records immediately after the completion of inventory
activities; and
d. Prepare and submit a complete and reconciled RPCPPE based on
the results of the actual physical count, to uphold the integrity of
the Municipality's property records, in compliance with Section
124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, not later
than January 31 of each year.

3.14. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-009 dated June 17, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

4.10. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-013 dated June 19, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

5.7. We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record advance payments
for Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET)
under the Deferred Real Property Tax and Deferred Special
Education Tax accounts respectively, and recognize income only
when the revenue is earned;
b. Recognize the corresponding discounts granted for advance
payments under the Discount on Advance Payments account and
credit the account when the discount is applied;
c. Record all collections of fines and penalties arising from late
payment of RPT and SET under the designated account Tax
Revenue – Fines and Penalties – Property Taxes account; and
d. To avoid similar errors in the future, direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to ensure proper segregation and classification of all
components of real property tax collections, including current
dues, advance payments, discounts, and penalties at the point of
recording.

5.8. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-014 dated June 23, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

6.9. We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Immediately remit to the GSIS, PhilHealth and BIR the
outstanding balances of ₱1.265 million, ₱0.494 million and ₱0.115,
respectively, as of December 31, 2024;
b. Ensure the timely and complete remittance of all future
mandatory contributions and taxes withheld, in accordance with
RA No. 8291, the Revised IRR of the NHIA, and BIR RMC No. 23-
2012;
c. Strengthen internal controls and monitoring mechanisms by
conducting regular reconciliation of withheld and remitted
amounts, to avoid under-remittances.

6.10. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-010 dated June 17, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

7.7. We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Immediately stop the practice of remitting HDMF contributions
prior to the actual withholding from employees’ salaries;
b. Ensure that all remittances to HDMF correspond strictly to the
actual amounts withheld, and are made only after the completion
of the salary processing period;
c. Strengthen internal controls by conducting regular reconciliation
of withheld versus remitted HDMF contributions and loan
payments, to prevent over-remittances and ensure accurate
accounting of inter-agency payables.

7.8. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-010 dated June 17, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

8.5. We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to revert the ₱3.286 million unutilized
LDRRMF balance beyond the five-year prescriptive period to the
General Fund’s unappropriated surplus.

8.6. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-004 dated March 10, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

9.12. The Audit Team issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
No. 2025-011 dated June 18, 2025 for the foregoing observations and
recommendations.

10.7. We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the MDRRMO to utilize the LDRRMF for the programs,
projects, and activities (PPAs) in accordance with Section 5 of
NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and stop the
incurrence of non-allowable administrative and operating charges
to the LDRRMF; and
b. Direct the MDRRMO to coordinate closely with the Municipal
DRRMC in the formulation of priority DRRM PPAs and to
include only in the LDRRMP those PPAs that directly support the
four thematic areas of the DRRM.

No. 2025-003 dated February 25, 2025 for the foregoing observations and

We recommended and Management agreed to revise future Program of Works for infrastructure projects to be implemented by administration to exclude rental costs of construction equipment that are available in the Municipality.

Require the submission of the sworn declaration from professionals before January 15 of each year to avail of the 5% withholding income tax rate.

Committee, to conduct annual physical count and submit the Report on
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) on or before
January 31 of each year. We also recommended and Management agreed
to require the concerned Offices to maintain the Property Cards and PPE
Ledger Cards and fast track the reconciliation of the previous year’s
RPCPPE with the Accounting records.

Accountant agreed to the following:
a. Assign a designated personnel to trace the differences between the
cashbook and accounting records for all funds in order to resolve this
recurring audit observation; and
b. Conduct monthly reconciliation of the records in the cash books and
accounting records.

Accountant, agreed to thoroughly investigate, identify the nature of these
variances, and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries once the
cause of the variance is identified.

accounts from the source documents and effect the appropriate
adjustments to correct and reconcile these accounts; and
b. Prepare subsidiary ledgers of each account mentioned above for easy
and fast reconciliation.

MOA between the Municipality and JO personnel that will clearly state the
necessity and propriety of hiring their services as well as the specific
duties of work or details on the expected outputs of the JO workers.

8.5 We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Engineer,
agreed to implement the projects/programs/activities funded by NGAs and
LGUs in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the
implementation of those PPAs.
8.6 We also recommended and the Management, through the Municipal
Accountant, agreed to initiate timely liquidation and return the
unexpended balances to the source agency after the completion of the
intended PPAs in compliance with the afore-cited regulation.

9.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to revert the unexpended
balances of the appropriations of the projects that were already completed
and settled for the current year and in the previous years to the
unappropriated surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year so
that the Municipality can utilize these funds for other projects as well.

10.6 We recommended and the Management, through the Local Chief
Executive and other concerned officials, agreed to the following:
a. Expedite the implementation of the abovementioned projects and
monitor implementation to ensure effective and efficient project
implementation and reporting; and
b. Ensure that the development projects funded out of the 20% DF are
well planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

11.6 We recommended and the BAC agreed to submit an
explanation/justification to controvert the findings and discontinue the
practice of splitting purchase requisitions or purchase orders/contracts in
order to promote effective competition and transparency in the
procurement process and obtain an equitable contract for the best interest
of the government.

12.11 We recommended and Management, through the Local Chief Executive
and other concerned officials, agreed to the following:
a. Consider the special circumstances for contract time extension
provided in the Procurement Manual for LGUs, Volume III and
approve the request of the contractor after due investigation and
finding the request justified;
b. Take note of the rules on contract suspension and the grounds for
suspension of work by the LGU and by the contractor provided in the
Procurement Manual for LGUs, Volume III;
c. Observe the provisions in Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184
on the Contract Implementation Guidelines for the Procurement of
55
Infrastructure Projects and other related issuances particularly Item
4.2 of GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 to ensure the timely implementation
of infrastructure projects and effective management of the
performance of contractors; and
d. Coordinate with the NHA and discuss possible actions to fast-track the
implementation of the project.

adjustment on the affected transactions if the same have not been paid
and record the retention amount under the “Guaranty/Security Deposits
Payable” account on future transactions.

2.17. We recommended that the:
2.17.1. Municipal General Services Officer:
a. Prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station and submit it to the
Accounting Office for recording in the books;
b. Prepare a List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs for items that
were recorded in the books but not found during the
inventory count following the format in Annex C of COA
Circular No. 2020-006 and follow the procedures for the
disposition of these items provided under Item 7 of the
circular;
c. Prepare IIRUP for all the PPEs found unserviceable,
obsolete and/or no longer needed;
d. Forward to the Accounting Office, the records of disposal
for all disposed items to date;
e. Expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties in
accordance with COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-01
dated January 30, 2024; and
f. Use the unit value in the subsidiary ledgers for
articles/items counted, if available.
2.17.2. Municipal Accountant:
a. Take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize
PPEs found at station and prepare/maintain corresponding
subsidiary ledgers based on the List of PPEs Found at
Station prepared by the GSO;
b. Update the subsidiary ledgers of PPEs as necessary and
take up the necessary accounting entries to derecognize
disposed PPEs based on original copies of IIRUP with
complete supporting documentation;
c. Take up the necessary accounting entries to derecognize
from the books of accounts the amounts lumped under
“Unreconciled SL”, “Reconciling SL” or the like; and
d. Ensure that the total balance of PPE subsidiary ledgers tally
with the GSO records.

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Apply for write-off for write-off for those accounts which collectability
could no longer be ascertained, provided that the conditions in COA
Circular No. 2016-005 are present; and
b. Furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of demand letter duly received
by the Accountable Officers.

4.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough
analysis and verification of non-moving payable accounts so that proper
action and/or disposition could be done to ensure that the validity and
propriety of the account balances are maintained.

5.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts by reclassifying the CIP accounts
to its appropriate PPE accounts, and for the subsequent recording of
completed projects, reclassify the CIP account as soon as the project is
completed as prescribed in Section 50 of the MNGAS for LGUs.

6.9. We recommended that the HRMO:
a. Discuss the terms and conditions stipulated in the Job Order to the
hired JO personnel, require them to acknowledge the same and
furnish them with a copy; and
b. Require them to execute a Notice of Assumption as additional control
to substantiate the effectivity date of their service.

7.9. We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer require
warranty security upon acceptance of goods procured through public
bidding and for the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments are duly
supported with such warranty.

8.9. We recommended that the Municipal HRMO proceed with the hiring of the
one mandatory position and coordinate with the Budget Officer in the
creation of the lacking required positions as set in the NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM-CS JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended/unobligated QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE to a special trust fund using the following Journal Entries: a. For General Fund Books: Dr. Subsidy to other Funds xx Cr. Cash in Bank xx; b. For Trust Fund Books: Dr. Cash in Bank xx Cr. Trust Liability – DRRMF xx (with SL for the source and year of transfer).

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all projects implemented by the LGU “by Administration” are listed among the approved projects or in an updated Annual Procurement Plan (APP); b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor all ongoing and future projects of the LGU and ensure that all approved projects are directly under his supervision and responsibility; and c. Direct the BAC Chairman and Acting Municipal Planning & Development Coordinator to indicate the mode of procurement in the APP and PPMP.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documentary requirements such as certificate of completion, Inspection & Acceptance Report (IAR), pictures before, during and after construction and properly accomplished Inspection & Acceptance Report as indicated in Annex K; b. Direct the Municipal Engineer and other focal persons to submit certificate of completion, pictures and necessary supporting documents with the approval of proper/authorized officials; and c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to check and verify the completeness of supporting documents on various claims before signing to establish its validity/propriety, otherwise, the transaction shall be suspended/disallowed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the documents including MOA on fund transfers from NGAs on the prescribed period; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the receipts, documentary requirements and utilization of the fund transfers; c. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and PESO Focal person to submit the required/relevant documents in the implementation of DOLE Projects and other project of the Municipality to establish validity of claim; and d. Return any unutilized balances to the source agency.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of fund and assess its implementation to immediately address any concerns/problems that may arise; b. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management (MDRRM) Council to prioritize PPAs that are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation taking into consideration the thrust set by the LGU; and c. Direct the MDRRM Council to identify the projects and activities to be funded on the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF, and incorporate in the updated LDRRMIP and in the revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to be duly approved by the Local Sanggunian and the Local Chief Executive (LCE).

We recommended and the Management agreed to open a separate depository account for the special trust fund for LDRRMF in compliance with Section 21, Paragraph 3 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 92 – 382 to account the LDRRMF balance.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documentary requirements such as Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, Inspection & Acceptance Report and Monthly Report of Official Travels as indicated in Annex N; and b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation and adequate information to establish the validity of transactions and activities, and to stop the practice of processing payment for claims not sufficiently documented.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to install signboards in the prescribed project for every infrastructure project; and b. Comply with the transparency requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA Circular 2013-004, in all its infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the GSO Designate to comply with the markings “For Official Use Only” on all agency motor vehicles of the agency measuring three inches; and b. Secure government plates for the 19 motor vehicles.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Require all Accountable Officers/Collectors to observe daily remittance of their collections intact as prescribed by Section 29 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I to lessen the risk of misuse and loss of funds; and b. Direct Municipal Treasurer to monitor and control the issuance of Accountable Forms to collectors without sacrificing efficiency.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory semestrally; b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Accountable Forms Inventory; and c. Direct the Liquidating Officer to check the CRAAF as to receipts, issuances and to verify the ending balances on hand.

12. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Schedule of Dormant Accounts as of December 31, 2023 and on the next quarters on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month.


13.  Submit a request for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts following COA Circular No. 2023-008.

15. Management monitors its cash position against its current and continuing obligations.


16. Management files a request for the approval of COA to write-off/derecognize from the books the dormant accounts to be filed pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 to ascertain that the long outstanding Due to NGAs no longer exist and that there is no potential cash deficit.

17. The Disbursing Officer stops accommodating loan payments for other employees and stop granting cash advances from the petty cash fund. The funds of the DO should be directly given to the payee named in the payroll unless there is written authorization to an immediate family member to collect the wages due to the payee.
18. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at the end of each quarter so that errors can be rectified at the earliest possible time.

21. Management places appropriate controls to ensure that project signboards are installed at the project site like requiring the attachment of photos of the installed project signboards upon payment of mobilization costs, and every progress billing of a certain project.
22. Management releases the barangay shares from RPT and community tax within five (5) days after the end of each quarter according to Section 271(d) of Republic Act 7160.

23. Management requires the personnel who are assigned motor vehicles to leave the motor vehicles in the motor pool or in a designated garage for motor vehicles after office hours and weekends to prevent the improper and unauthorized use of the motor vehicle.
28. Management submits written justification/comment on the excess of ABC and total contract price versus the COA Cost Estimate.

34. The Municipal Accountant obtains the necessary documents on the turn-over of the projects including barangays’ acceptance and prepare the following adjusting journal entries:

5. Submit a written valid justification and discontinue the practice of splitting purchase requisitions/purchase orders/contracts in order to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain an equitable contract for the best interest of the government.
6. Ensure that RFQs prepared are in compliance with Section 18 of the IRR which provides that specifications for the procurement of Goods shall be based on relevant characteristics, functionality and/or performance requirements and does not include brand names.
8. Submit a written explanation as to the reason for non-compliance with the above-stated provisions of R.A. 9184.

16. Submit an acceptable explanation/justification for the noted deficiency, for the Audit Team’s evaluation to avoid audit disallowance.
18. Secure from NTC the required Permits and submit to the Audit Team to consider the procurement of communication equipment valid and legitimate expenditures.

19. Submit explanation for the non-implementation of these projects to be funded out of the other Local Government Units.
20. Immediate implementation of these programs/projects in order not to delay further the benefits expected to be derived therefrom.

21. Direct the end-user unit and the Bids and Awards Committee, Technical Working Group to ensure that the preparation of the technical specifications of the goods/items to be procured shall strictly observe the prohibition against making reference to brand name so as not to defeat competition and to give every qualified supplier the chance to participate in the bidding.

24. Copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services to be submitted within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.
