observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to instruct:
a) Municipal Accountant (a) to set up the RPT/SET Receivables in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in support of the RPT and SET Receivable balances so that reconciliation be made with the MTO; and (b) to effect the necessary adjustment to fully record the RPT/SET Receivables based on the Certified List of Taxpayers/Actual List of Delinquent Tax Payers in order to reflect accurate and reliable receivable and the related deferred income balances.
b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) institute more effective collection efforts to improve tax collection efficiency of the municipality; (ii) on a timely basis prepare an accurate and updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Taxes Due (CLTTD) and submit the same to the Accounting Office as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET Receivables in the beginning of the year; and (iii) strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as provided in Section 256 of RA no. 7160.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman and its TWG to a) ensure strict compliance with the requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR; and b) issue Certificate of Final Acceptance with date indicated therein and for future implementation, require the contractor to post performance security and retention bond with validity until issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance of the project.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to:
(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to reconcile accounting and property records for the correction of verified discrepancies;
(b) assign a focal person who will focus on the requirements needed to enable the immediate transfer of title of the subject lands under the name of the Municipality; and
(c) instruct the OIC-General Services Officer to a) direct the personnel assigned in maintenance of property cards to properly and completely fill out the Property Cards and to use the prescribed LRNPC for the Road Networks; and c) conduct further investigation on the stolen items and request for relief from accountability, otherwise, the money value of the said stolen items will be paid by the accountable person.
(d) instruct the Disposal Committee to dispose obsolete/unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration of the property and consequent depreciation to its value.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the OIC Municipal Accountant to evaluate outstanding checks aging above six months which are considered stale per Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2022 and make necessary adjustments and prepare JEV to record the transactions as Accounts Payable in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Vol. I; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check become stale to inform him/her of the existence of the outstanding check, pursuant to Section 59 of NGAS for LGU Vol. I.

We recommended that Management exert diligent efforts to locate the documents supporting the reconciling items, make representation with the bank for the furnishing of the original copies of debit and credit advices, record the reconciling items and stale checks in the books and if warranted, prepare journal entries to adjust the cash in bank balance of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, Management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in the same Circular.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant expedite the reconciliation of long outstanding/dormant accounts and negative balances.
We recommended that Management concerned monitor and facilitate liquidation of fund transfers from source agency, especially those long outstanding/ dormant accounts.
We recommended that Management submit written justification/comment on the excess of ABC and total contract price versus the COA Cost Estimate.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the recipient of fund transfers for the submission of liquidation reports or settlement of receivables.

We further recommended that Management discontinue the practice of granting fund transfers to the Implementing Agencies with unliquidated balances and a certification by the Accountant be required for succeeding transfers.

We recommended that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.
We recommended that Management submit proof of creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommended that the Management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.

We recommended that the municipal accountant obtain the necessary documents on the turn-over of the projects including barangays’ acceptance and prepare the following adjusting journal entries:

Adjusting Entry No. 1

Debit: Buildings  ₱5,034,502.21  
Credit: Construction in Progress   ₱5,034,502.21 
To reclassify completed projects from CIP to PPE -AOM No. 2020-005(19)

Adjusting Entry No. 2

Debit: Prior Period Adjustments  ₱6,514,791.40  
Debit: Accumulated Depreciation  35,609.14  
Credit: Buildings  ₱6,550,400.54 
To drop the cost of the BEA 1-6 projects from the LGU books and transfer it to the BLGU books
We also recommended that the municipal accountant ensure that the individual books of the affected barangays be adjusted accordingly to record these assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the PPE accounts from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and take up the depreciation of these accounts from prior years’ 2016 to CY 2019 so that the correct balances will be reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts under Due to GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these payable accounts.
Further, make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to reconcile the negative/abnormal balances of the account.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).

We further recommended that Management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to speed up the titling of these lands in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly.

We recommended that Management conduct immediate follow-ups to facilitate the issuances of title in its name, otherwise, cancel the transaction and request for the return of the payment from the lot owner.
We further recommended that Management request for an SB Resolution confirming the purchase made.

Establish RPT/SET Receivables in the books at the beginning of the year based on the duly certified list of all taxpayers obtained from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, in order to present reliable and accurate balances in the financial statements.

Given the above conditions and predicaments, we recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Office, Municipal Budget Office, Municipal Engineering Office as well as other concerned Offices such as the end-users to collectively prepare a holistic and comprehensive yet realistic catch-up plan to expedite the project implementation of these funded projects which are still pending. With this, project backlogs will be appropriately reduced which will then allow the Municipal Engineering Office and other involved offices to focus working on all the preparatory activities needed to make the next proposed projects to be funded under the Municipality’s LDF in the ensuing budget year.

Return the unspent balances to the SA, or request approval from the SA to transfer the unexpended balances to the General Fund if the disposition thereof has not been provided in the MOA, Trust Agreement or Memorandum of Understanding and close the respective bank account maintained to avoid the incurrence of possible dormancy fees/charges in the ensuing year.
Submit an acceptable explanation/ justification for the noted deficiency, for the Audit Team’s evaluation to avoid audit disallowance.

Submit its legal basis on the inclusion of lot acquisition under the Build and Design Scheme, for evaluation by the Audit Team, and to appropriately make manifestation with the National Housing Authority on the noted deficiency in the aforementioned Terms of Reference.
Secure from NTC the required Permits and submit to the Audit Team to consider the herein procurement of communication equipment valid and legitimate expenditures.
Submit explanation for the non-implementation of these projects to be funded out of the financial assistance from other Local Government Units.
Immediate implementation of these programs/projects in order not to delay further the benefits expected to be derived therefrom.

Direct the end-user unit and the Bids and Awards Committee, Technical Working Group to ensure that the preparation of the technical specifications of the goods/items to be procured shall strictly observe the prohibition against making reference to brand name so as not to defeat competition and to give every qualified supplier the chance to participate in the bidding.
Intensify the implementation of the projects funded by financial assistance in order to timely achieve the desired benefits.
Also, make representation with the respective donors of the financial assistance with unutilized balances and seek authority to use the fund balance to finance other projects/programs of the agency.

Copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services to be submitted within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

Coordinate with the daycare centers and conduct inventory of the textbooks and instructional materials and submit the inventory report to the Accounting Office to support the amount of inventory recorded in the books.
The Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to comply the documentary requirements for the write off of the Loans Receivable account for verification and evaluation.

Establish an Internal Audit Service Office with sufficient support from the top management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.

Submit copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.
Return to the National Treasury the unutilized /unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.
Require the Disposal Committee to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties to the directives of Sections 379, 380 and 381 of RA 7160.
Monitor the status of the agricultural produce for distribution and breeding stocks and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the review and analysis of the details of the accounts.

Impose the bonding requirements of collectors and assign back to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office the Revenue Collection Clerk who was assigned at the Mayor Office’s to maximize her services. Also, stop the practice of assigning the tasks of collecting to personnel hired as casual / job orders.
Refund the cost of gasoline issued to privately owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.

Require the Municipal Livestock Coordinator, who is in – charge of the dispersal program of the municipality, to monitor the project, prepare and submit a report regularly to the Accounting Division on any increase in the livestock inventory due to returns from recipients as well as the mortality duly supported with an affidavit and witnessed by any of the barangay officials to ensure the reliability, accuracy and validity of the recorded amount in the financial statement.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the concerned Inventory Committee to undertake the complete physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality to comply with the One-time Cleansing of PPE balances and agreed to request for authority to derecognize non- existing/ missing PPEs to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or the Supervising Auditor (SA) as provided in COA Circular No. 2020- 006 dated January 31, 2020, if any; and the Accounting Office agreed to consistently prepare and update the PPE Ledger Cards and to establish internal control to ensure accuracy of depreciation; and also the General Service Officer-Designate agreed to prepare Property Cards for each type of asset in accordance with the prescribed format pursuant to NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and designated head of the General Services Office reconciled and is still reconciling the records on PPE as much as what allows them. This observation was reiterated under Finding No. 5 in page 46 of this AAR.
observations.reason: The reason for non- implementation is man power.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer,
the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Property Officer to locate
records of road networks in their offices where the necessary data,
specifications and locations of the Road Networks may be sourced to
facilitate the preparation and submission of the aforementioned required
reports and the proper recognition of Road Networks and their
corresponding depreciation in the accounting books.

1.7 We also recommended that Management ensure preparation and
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and its components and the
35
disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and Management agreed that FSs, DVs and ORs be
submitted within the prescribed period to enable timely audit of account
balances and transactions.

3.5 We recommended and Management agreed to immediately require the
concerned personnel to cooperate in the preparation and submission of
AAPSI in order for the audit team to monitor the status of implementation of
audit recommendations and validate the actions taken, if any.

4.10 We recommended and Management agreed:
• That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers
are properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall a
disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting documents; and
• That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately submitted
to COA for evaluation and review.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed to submit list of beneficiaries of
the farm inputs that were supposedly procured and distributed specifying
therein their names, addresses, quantity received and their signatures for
further verification.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the coverage of
possible uncorrected discovered defects and third-party liabilities; and abide
with the rules and regulations on the procurement of infrastructure projects
and ensure that the surety bonds submitted by contractors/suppliers are
properly reviewed before any payment is made.

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the recoupment of the
cited outstanding balance of advance payment; and abide with the rules and
regulations on the procurement of infrastructure projects and ensure that the
surety bonds submitted by contractors/suppliers are properly reviewed
before any payment is made.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit justification as to why
reimbursements method was resorted instead of alternative mode of
procurement prescribed by the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, for
evaluation.

9.7 We recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Auditor copy of the
purchase orders and contract documents not later than five working days
from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with
duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and
evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

10.8 We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required
documents mentioned above. Henceforth, comply with the rules and
regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

1.4. We reiterated prior years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the assets/items subject for disposal so as not to misled
users thereof;
b) assign a focal person who will focus on the requirements needed to
enable the immediate transfer of title of the subject lands under the
name of the Municipality; and
c) instruct the Disposal Committee to dispose obsolete/unserviceable
properties in accordance with prescribed procedures to avoid further
deterioration and depreciation thereof.

2.2. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct:
a) Municipal Accountant to (i) justify the adjustment made and provide
supporting documents; and (ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers/Actual List
of Delinquent Tax Payers in order to reflect accurate and reliable receivable and
the related deferred income balances; and
b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) institute more effective collection efforts to improve
tax collection efficiency of the municipality; and (ii) strictly enforce the remedies
for the collection of delinquent RPT as provided in Section 256 of RA No. 7160.

3.7. Meanwhile, we recommended that Management:
a) Draw the following adjusting entries:
Particulars: To record the remaining medicines from DOE funds
Account Title Debit Credit
Due to NGAs ₱419,860.00
Account Title Debit Credit
Other Deferred Credits ₱419,860.00
Particulars: To transfer the funds received from PCSO from Due to NGAs
to Due to GOCCs
Account Title Debit Credit
Due to NGAs ₱1,600,946.34
Due to GOCCs ₱1,600,946.34
b) Conduct a comprehensive review of allocations for funds received from PCSO,
ensuring alignment with community needs and priorities specific for the health
programs; and
c) Maximize the utilization of the funds received from PCSO.

4.15. Furthermore, we recommended and Management agreed to ensure thorough
inspection of proposed project locations and immediately conduct detailed
engineering assessments to identify and address the possible constraints in project
implementation.

5.18. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Municipal Engineer
agreed issue Certificate of Final Acceptance with date indicated therein and for future
implementation, require the contractor to post performance security and retention
bond with validity until issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance of the project.

5.19. We also recommended that:
a) The Planning and Programming Division of the Municipal Engineer’s
Office adhere to Department of Public Works and Highways Department
Order No. 03 series of 2010 dated January 04, 2010 which provides the
guidelines on the acquisition of motor vehicles for use in infrastructure
project supervision; and
b) The Municipal Engineering Office and the Municipal Accounting office
ensure that provision for service vehicle for engineers covers rental
agreement and supported with Official Receipts from car companies and
their authorized dealers.

6.8. Meanwhile, we recommended and Management agreed to (i) ensure the
optimal utilization of the LDRRMF which includes the Quick Response Fund (QRF)
especially when there is a declaration of State of Calamity within the Municipality;
and (ii) to fully implement the planned Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs)
identified in the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) that will benefit the
constituents in times of disaster.

7.7. We also recommended and Management agreed that the MSWMB continue
its supervision and assistance as an oversight body in identifying solutions and actions
on key issues/challenges on Solid Waste Management facing the Municipality as a
whole and the barangays particularly on those without established Barangay
Materials Recovery System.

8.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive –
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary
Ledgers for business taxpayers;
b. direct the BPLO to –
i. ensure the timely submission of proof of annual gross
receipts/income and monitor as well the submission of copies of ITRs
on or before April 30 of each year
ii. consider hiring personnel to assist in monitoring ITR submissions to
ensure accurate assessment and collection of annual business taxes;
iii. coordinate with the BPLTAS system provider for a review of
business tax rates before transitioning to new software; and
iv. ensure the accuracy of rates in the upcoming system software for
business permit and licensing;
c. advise the Acting Municipal Treasurer to –
i. assign an inspector to every application for business termination,
with the responsibility of verifying the operational status by visiting
the business address; and
ii. conduct examination of books of accounts of businessmen after
attendance to the relative seminar; and
d. ensure that the Revenue Code is regularly updated, adhering to the
provisions of Section 191 of RA 7160, with updates conducted no more
frequently than once every five years.

9.16. We recommended that Management agreed to instruct the GFPS (a) to ensure
that PPAs are in alignment with the GAD objectives of addressing gender issues
towards gender equality and women empowerment; and (b) attribute only a portion
of the Municipal Government of Pilar’s programs to the GAD budget by subjecting
the program to gender analysis using the HGDG tool to reflect the correct amount
pertaining to GAD Accomplishment.

10.4. We commended Management for its continuous strict adherence with existing
revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR. We
recommended that it sustain continuous compliance with existing revenue regulations
to avoid penalties which may be imposed for the violation thereof.

11.6. We commended Management for its continued strict compliance with RA No.
8291 in the prompt remittance of the premiums deducted from the salaries of its
employees and the government share. We recommended that Management continue
to being compliant with the requirements of the GSIS Act.

12.6. We commended Management specially the Accounting Office and Treasury
Offices for its continued compliance with the HDMF Law in the prompt remittance
of contributions and other amount due to Pag-IBIG. We recommended that
Management continue its regular compliance to the HDMF regulations.

13.7. We commended Management on the prompt remittance of the amount due to
PhilHealth. We recommended that Management sustain its compliance to the
PhilHealth regulations.

14.3. We recommended that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the
settlement of the disallowances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, pertaining to stale checks and make proper action to affect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record all purchases under the account “Construction Materials Inventory” and observe compliance with the perpetual inventory system in the recording of the supplies and materials; and
Require Municipal GSO Designate to submit a summary of the utilized Construction Materials Inventory monthly to the Accounting Office based on issuances recorded as they take place.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended/unobligated QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱4.020 million to a special trust fund using the following Journal Entries:
For General Fund Books:
Dr.         Subsidy to other Funds                  xx
Cr.         Cash in Bank                                           xx
For Trust Fund Books
Dr.   Cash in Bank                                         xx
Cr     Trust Liability- DRRMF                                    xx
(with SL for the source and year of transfer)

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records in accordance with Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1;
Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS as provided in Section 121 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and
Conduct of periodic physical inventory of supplies or properties and reconcile the RPCI to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of Inventory account balances.
Require the GSO designate to:
Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the original RIS, to the Municipal Accountant as basis of JEV preparation to record the expenditures based on the cost of the supplies and materials issued; and
Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type, prepare the RPCI, and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all projects implemented by the LGU “by Administration” are listed among the approved projects or in an updated Annual Procurement Plan (APP);
Direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor all ongoing and future projects of the LGU and ensure that all approved projects are directly under his supervision and responsibility; and
Direct the BAC Chairman and Acting Municipal Planning & Development Coordinator to indicate the mode of procurement in the APP and PPMP.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documentary requirements such as certificate of completion, pictures before, during and after construction and properly accomplished Inspection & Acceptance Report (IAR), as indicated in Annex K;
Direct the Municipal Engineer and other focal persons to submit certificate of completion, pictures and necessary supporting documents with the approval of proper/authorized officials; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to check and verify the completeness of supporting documents on various claims before signing to establish its validity/propriety, otherwise, the transaction shall be suspended/ disallowed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of fund and assess its implementation to immediately address any concerns/problems that may arise; and
Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management (MDRRM) Council to prioritize PPAs that are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation taking into consideration the thrust set by the LGU; and
Direct the MDRRM Council to identify the projects and activities to be funded on the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF, and incorporate in the updated LDRRMIP and in the revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to be duly approved by the Local Sanggunian and the Local Chief Executive (LCE).

We recommended and the Management agreed to open a separate depository account for the special trust fund for LDRRMF in compliance with Section 21, Paragraph 3 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 92 – 382 to account the LDRRMF balance.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documentary requirements such as Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, Inspection and Acceptance Report and Monthly Report of Official Travels were not properly accomplished, as indicated in Annex N; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation and adequate information to establish the validity of transactions and activities, and to stop the practice of processing payment for claims not sufficiently documented.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to install signboards in the prescribed project for every infrastructure project; and
Comply with the transparency requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA Circular 2013-004, in all its infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the GSO Designate to comply with the required markings “For Official Use Only” on all motor vehicles of the agency measuring three inches; and
Secure government plates for the 19 motor vehicles

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory for supplies semestrally;
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Accountable Forms Inventory; and
Direct the Liquidating Officer to check the CRAAF as to receipts, issuances and to verify the ending balances on hand.

We recommended that the Property and Accounting Units perform the preliminary activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking and reconcile their records as set out in  COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We also recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory activities and attach property stickers on the counted PPEs.

We recommended that for the reconciling items before CY 2020, the Municipal Accountant investigate and determine the causes and veracity of the reported reconciling items and take the appropriate actions to address each one including making representations with the bank for the furnishing of the original copies of debit and credit advices or bank statements.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Retrieve the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to identified Accounts Payable accounts and review if these are valid commitments and obligations of the LGU;
For those accounts with no supporting documents including those which have been outstanding for more than 2 years, prepare the following adjusting entry to reverse the non-existing payable balances:
Accounts Payable			xxx
Government Equity			xxx
Trace and reconcile the existence of negative balances under the Accounts Payable accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books to correct the balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Schedule of Dormant Accounts as of December 31, 2023 and on the next quarters on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month.
Submit a request for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts following COA Circular No. 2023-008
We recommended and management agreed to secure APMVs from the Secretary of the Interior and Local Government on the purchase of motor vehicles for the succeeding procurements.

We recommended that Management monitor its cash position against its current and continuing obligations.
We also recommended that Management file a request for the approval of COA to write-off/derecognize from the books the dormant accounts to be filed pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 to ascertain that the long outstanding Due to NGAs no longer exist and that there is no potential cash deficit.

We recommended and management agreed that the Disbursing Officer stop accommodating loan payments for other employees and stop granting cash advances from the petty cash fund. The funds of the DO should be directly given to the payee named in the payroll unless there is written authorization to an immediate family member to collect the wages due to the payee.
We recommended and management agreed to reconcile their records at the end of each quarter so that errors can be rectified at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor review the Assessment Roll and correct the above-noted discrepancies concerning properties with duplicate/identical Property Identification Number, properties with zero market value, and properties with zero assessed value.
We further recommended that updated data on real properties within the Municipality be submitted to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation/updating of the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, copy furnished to the Audit Team.

We recommended and management agreed to place appropriate controls to ensure that project signboards are installed at the project site like requiring the attachment of photos of the installed project signboards upon payment of mobilization costs, and every progress billing of a certain project.
We recommended and the management agreed to release the barangay shares from RPT and community tax within five (5) days after the end of each quarter according to Section 271(d) of Republic Act 7160.

We recommended and management agreed to require the personnel who are assigned motor vehicles to leave the motor vehicles in the motor pool or in a designated garage for motor vehicles after office hours and weekends to prevent the improper and unauthorized use of the motor vehicle.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee investigate the cause/s of the non-conduct of the physical count of PPE and undertake measures to eventually prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for evaluation; and
Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain depreciation schedules and PPE ledger cards, respectively, for each item of PPE to substantiate the balances presented in the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant agreed to reconcile their respective records of Cash in Local Treasury as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and conduct the subsequent reconciliation regularly in order to timely effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments and consistently harmonize the balances at year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant agreed to reconcile their respective records of Cash in Bank account as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments to present accurate cash in bank balances at year-end.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit a written valid justification and discontinue the practice of splitting purchase requisitions/purchase orders/contracts in order to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain an equitable contract for the best interest of the government; and
Ensure that RFQs prepared are in compliance with Section 18 of the IRR of R.A. 9184 which provides that specifications for the procurement of Goods shall be based on relevant characteristics, functionality and/or performance requirements and does not include brand names.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit a written explanation on the purchase of reconditioned Boom Truck despite the prohibition stipulated in the DBM and DILG circulars.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit a written explanation as to the reason for non-compliance with the above-stated provisions of R.A. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to secure the ECC or Certificate of Non-Coverage of the said project and submit it to the Audit Team for verification or a written explanation of why the same could not be submitted.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Expedite the implementation of the above-mentioned projects and monitor implementation to ensure effective and efficient project implementation and reporting; and
Ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials agreed to submit a written explanation on the non-conduct of public/competitive bidding for the procurement of meals for the LGU Budgeting Conference in CY 2023.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials agreed to submit a written explanation/justification as to the necessity of conducting the LGU Budget Conference outside the island of Camotes.

1.7. We recommended and the designated head of the General Services Office
agreed, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to hasten the
process of disposing the unserviceable properties so that the same may
be appropriately dropped from the books of accounts which in turn will
provide for a more accurate balance of the PPE accounts.

2.12. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that
cash advance granted for the release of Confidential Fund is recorded
under the “Advances to Special Disbursing Officer” account and
liquidation of prior year expenditures are taken up in the books of
accounts through the “Prior Period Adjustment” account in order to
prevent the understatement of the surplus for the period.

3.11. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly adhere
to the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units and the
provisions of IPSAS 23 when recording liquidations involving fund transfers
from National Government Agencies, and prepare the following adjusting
entries and submit the Journal Entry Vouchers to the Audit Team:
Due to NGAs 6,380.00
Due to GOCCs 6,380.00
To adjust misclassified Due to GOCCs.
Due to NGAs 114,337.35
Trust Liabilities - DRRMF 114,337.35
To adjust misclassified cash donations for DRRMF.
Due to NGAs 9,994,000.02
Government Equity 9,994,000.02
To record liquidation of completed projects.

3.12. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue
with the reconciliation and clarification of details pertaining to the
subsidiary accounts with insufficient information in order to guarantee
that these accounts are properly substantiated.

4.10. We recommend and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly use the
accounts prescribed in the chart of accounts promulgated under COA
Circular No. 2015-009 and prepare the following adjusting journal entry to
correct the misclassified amount of ₱6,935,436.20:
Terminal Benefits 6,935,436.20
Leave Benefits Payable 6,935,436.20
To adjust the misclassified earned leave credits.

5.9. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an
Inventory Committee whose main priority is to implement the procedures
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing
of PPE account balances in order to address the issues that are
preventing the reconciliation of records between the concerned offices
and to establish accurate balances of the PPE accounts that are to be
presented in the financial statements.

6.7. We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to ensure that the amount of cash advance granted
for the release of Confidential Fund adheres to the three-month
requirements limitation while its disbursement voucher, together with the
required transmittal letter and supporting documents, are submitted to
the COA Audit Team Leader within seven calendar days after the release
of the related check pursuant to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-
DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

7.6. We recommended and the MDRRM Officer agreed to revise the LDRRMFIP in the
event of any changes in the budget whether due to the discontinuance or
creation of PPAs, and/or budget augmentation thereof and to furnish the Office
of the Auditor, Office of the Civil Defense and the Department of Interior and
Local Government – Local Government Operations Office a copy of the
revised LDRRMFIP.

8.9. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to terminate the
subject contract by following the procedures embodied in Item IV under
Annex “I” of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

Management implement
the following measures to
assure the reliability of
balances of PPE
accounts:

fixed assets shall be
made at least once a
year;

inventory reports of

• Reconciliation of the
inventory report with
the balances reflected
in the financial
statements,
identification of
persons accountable
for loss of property,
and proper adjustment
in the financial
statements, if any.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management cause the refund of the amount of ₱14,850.00 due to the failure to present photos to substantiate the undertaking of the labor activity on August 2, 3, and 4, 2022 resulting to its irregularity.
Likewise, we reiterate prior year’s recommendation that the Management, through the LDRRMO to –
• monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMIP to ensure the full implementation of the planned activities; and
• properly plan the schedule for each planned PPAs for its timely implementation.
Moreover, we also recommend that the –
a. Municipal General Services Officer to religiously submit the SSMI together with RISs to the Municipal Accounting Office to properly account the issued supplies and materials; and
b. MDRRMO to –
• submit the distribution list or equivalent document to prove the existence of distribution of the handbooks or manuals; and
• together with the MEO, to submit additional documents and pictures taken during the desilting of Masuaje River to substantiate the work done.

We recommend that the GFPS (a) adhere with the guidelines of reporting to the DILG of the changes on indorsed/approved GPB prior to the disbursement of funds and (b) strive for the full implementation of the identified and approved PPAs by utilizing judiciously the fund set aside for said purposes.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the –
Municipal Health Officer to –
• strengthen the LGU's campaign and widen the scope of its information drive to generate more demand for the vaccine and continue its vaccination roll out until the desired local herd immunity is attained;
• render a monthly report to the MGSO and MAO on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources for the proper monitoring and recording thereof; and
• Municipal General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office in coordination with the MHO to determine and recognize in the reports and books the future receipts and issuances of the vaccines and ancillary supplies for fair presentation of the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal General Services Office to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies, prepare, and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, in compliance with the set guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the GSO Designate to ascertain the roads with unknown locations, prepare Report on Local Road Network and conduct Physical Count of the Road Network annually;


Direct Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Voucher Entry to record the depreciation expense and determine the unbooked accumulated depreciation for restatement of the equity accounts and adjustments to Prior Years Adjustment, beginning balance of the local road network, its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; and

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer complete description and cost segregation of road components for road project.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to account for the difference of 2,708 doses and to secure duly filled-out copies of Requisition and Issue Slips from PHO every time goods are received/transferred from other agencies and reconcile all RIS at year end;


Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to submit the data to the Municipal Accountant for recording of donated vaccines; 

Direct Municipal Rural Health Officer to prepare and submit Inventory Report of Vaccines as prescribed in Section 6.3 COA Memorandum No. 2021-015 as follows:

dates of receipts and expiration dates.
quantity and cost of vaccines or supplies issued or distributed for inoculation, wastage or spoilage, and remaining balance of vaccines and supplies.
dates of issuance and inoculation.

Direct the Municipal Health Unit to conduct regularly inventory of drugs and medicines to account the number of vaccines received and issued to establish its existence and condition.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular verification and reconciliation of unexpended balances of fund transfer and cause the return of balances to the Source Agency.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Provide the Municipal Accountant with certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index as a bases of recording the RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred RPT /Deferred SET Income; and	
	
Intensify the collection of RPT/SET Receivable to include delinquent accounts by providing tax amnesty and post at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the municipal hall pursuant to Section 254 of the RA 7160;

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

1.   Establish and record RPT Receivable and SET Receivable, at the beginning of the year, based on the certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index for the current year provided by the Municipal Treasurer using the following entries:

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to submit the Program of Activities on the above-mentioned projects;

Direct the MDRRMO Head to submit the monthly and annual report on the utilization of the LDRRMF to the Regional Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (RDRRMC) through the Regional Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Regional Officer of the DILG and DBM; and

Observe compliance with the Fundamental Principle that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to remit immediately the withheld taxes totaling ₱250,246.34 as of December 31, 2022 to the BIR. Penalties to be imposed by the BIR shall be the liability of the concerned Withholding Agent; and

Monitor and remit monthly the taxes withheld from suppliers and contractors within 10 days after the end of each month to avoid the incurrence of penalties in compliance with the aforementioned provisions.

We recommended that Management make representation to the PHO-Bohol for the preparation of Memorandum of Agreement to determine the responsibilities and accountabilities in the distribution/recording of COVID-19 vaccines. Otherwise, donated vaccines are to be recorded in the books of the Municipality.
We recommended that Management submit immediately copies of any variation orders, time extension and suspension/resumption order of the projects, for further verification.

We also recommended that Management require the contractor to fast track the implementation and submit sufficient justification in the delay of the full implementation of the infrastructure projects and if warranted, rescind the contracts, forfeit the contractors’ performance security and takeover the prosecution of the projects, in accordance with Item 9, Annex E of RA 9184.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute and deduct liquidated damages from the retention money pursuant to Item 9 Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office prepare an updated data on real properties within the municipality and submit it to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation/updating of the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, and copy furnish the Municipal Accountant for the adjustment of the recorded RPT & SET Receivables.
We also recommended that the Management concerned develop an effective tax collection campaign to enhance the collection of RPT and SET receivables.
We recommended that Management explain why the projects were provided in generic terms and does not contain sufficient details despite previous audit findings in CY 2021.

We also recommended that Management submit to the Audit Team a copy of the authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan for the implementation of specific projects in CY 2022 taken from the lump-sum appropriation of the 20% Development Fund. Otherwise, charges thereto will be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure first an authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to implementation of PPAs funded out of the lumpsum appropriation. Moreover, in the formulation of subsequent annual budgets, PPAs needs to be procurement-and-implementation-ready.
We recommended that Management submit written justification on the deficiencies noted above.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Office review the supporting documents of the Obligation Request to determine whether these are proper charges against the LDRRM fund.
We further recommended that trip tickets and all other supporting documents be reviewed by the Municipal Accountant for completeness and propriety to avoid irregular and illegal transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the collections and direct all collecting officers to turn over all collections intact daily, pursuant to Section 69, P.D. No. 1445 and Section 29, NGAS for LGU, Volume I.

We recommended that Management submit justifications in utilizing the 20% Development Fund for expenditures that are not allowed to be charged. Otherwise, irregular or illegal transactions shall be disallowed in audit.

We further recommended that Management refrain from charging wages of job order workers that are not associated with a specific priority project to 20% Development Fund and adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

We recommended that Management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/ activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic Act 10121.

We further recommended that Management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the preparation of ABC, in accordance with the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR. Otherwise, irregular/illegal expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management make written representation to the BIR in order to (a) ascertain whether the activities are taxable or not, (b) apply for an Authority to Print (ATP) for receipts or invoices and pay the Annual Registration Fee, if necessary, (c) determine the taxes due, if applicable.

We recommended that Management submit written explanation on the non-deduction of liquidated damages from the contractor of the 3 BEA projects, and if after validation warrants the imposition of the same, demand from the contractor the liquidated damages not deducted from the final payment.
Further, strictly follow with the provision of Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 with regard to the computation of liquidated damages.

Further, the following journal entries have to be taken up in the books:

We recommended that the Management require the Property Custodian or the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS and request for computation to ensure that properties are adequately covered with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We recommended that Management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.

We recommended that Management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP meeting.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer to not accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.

We recommended that Management require coordination between the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in the identification of all completed public infrastructure projects based on the transfers recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the registries of public infrastructures in preparation of the five-year transitional application for the recognition of the Public Infrastructures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) application.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that funds are really available when he signs the certification.
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit immediately the complete set of financial statements and other financial reports as of December 31, 2022.
Expedite the hiring of a qualified Municipal Accountant so that the preparation and submission of required financial reports can be immediately facilitated.

Revert the unexpended balances of the appropriations of the projects that were already completed and settled for the current year and in the previous years to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year so that the Municipality can utilize these funds for other projects as well. Henceforth, compliance with Section 322 of RA 7160 is hereby enjoined.
Submit explanation / justification on the low utilization of the LDRRMF specifically those which pertain to 70 per cent allocation for preparation and mitigation.
Ensure that all PPAs included in the LDRRM Plan are implemented and LDRRMF utilization is maximized to attain its goals for the benefit of its constituents.

Instruct the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization on or before the 15th day after the end of each month and include in the report all information required in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to enable the COA and other offices concerned to conduct the evaluation of the LDRRM Fund utilization on time.

Fast track the hiring of a qualified Municipal Accountant to fill in the position, and in the meantime, submit a request for exemption to the CSC on the designation of an employee, currently holding a first-level position to carry out the second-level position. Thereafter, furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the exemption duly approved by the CSC.

Submit an acceptable explanation or justification for the non-imposition of liquidated damages on the delayed completion of the project. Henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of Annex “E” of the RIRR of R.A. 9184 and demand or deduct liquidated damages before payment of final billing and release of retention money to the supplier.
Stop the practice of having this kind of transaction and properly plan any activities that are conducted outside the vicinity of the LGU.
Stop the practice of granting cash advances to any elected officials for purposes other than that stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Demand liquidation of the cash advances and henceforth follow the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Fill in the vacant mandatory positions in order to attain the full implementation of the Municipality’s programs, projects and activities for the optimal benefit of the constituents. If possible, coordinate with the Civil Service Commission to boost the pool of qualified candidates in the selection process.

Establish clear-cut guidelines on the grant of AICS, such as the qualification of indigent, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements, and the like, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, to provide proper control in the disbursement of public funds.

Procurement of meals and snacks or catering services for its various activities will be done through competitive bidding or any alternative mode when justified by conditions, following the guidelines prescribed in RA 9184, its IRR and related issuances.

Comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide an updated Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies to the Accountant annually before the close of accounting books. Further agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to perform periodic reconciliation of the RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: Implemented based on the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the property accounts to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for necessary adjustment to reclassify the account from Investment Property to Property, Plant and Equipment including its accumulated depreciations and provide sufficient disclosures of the transfer in the Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed by IPSAS 16 and IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: Implemented based on the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to ensure compliant with the policy and procedure provided by law in the enactment of an appropriation ordinance for the use of savings and Augmentation and the use of savings thru Supplemental Budget.
observations.managementAction: Implemented based on the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to adhere with pertinent provision of the laws in time of changes of the annual budget be done through supplemental budget provided that there shall be funds actually available, if covered by new revenue sources and in time of public calamity. Further, agreed in the use of augmentation in any items of the budget shall be from savings in other items within the same expenses class of their respective appropriation supported with an ordinance.
observations.managementAction: Implemented based on the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare, in the prescribed format, and certify correct the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the LDRRM personnel submit the same report to COA auditor, copy furnish the OCD and the DILG-LGOO along with the copy of the LDRRMFIP. Further, the management agreed that the LDRRMC identify DRRM related projects and activities to be charged against prior years’ LDRRMF unexpended balances and include the same in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption.
observations.managementAction: Implemented based on the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management issue demand letter to the retired MT for the immediate deposit of the amount of ₱1.9 million otherwise institute appropriate charges against the erring accountable officer.
observations.managementAction: Management will issue demand letter to compel the retired MT to settle immediately her accountability.
observations.reason: The retired MT refuses to pay her obligation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to –
	set up the RPT/SET Receivables in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in support of the RPT and SET Receivable balances so that reconciliation be made with the MTO; and
	effect the necessary adjustment to fully record the RPT/SET Receivables based on the Certified List of Taxpayers/Actual List of Delinquent Tax Payers in order to reflect accurate and reliable receivable and the related deferred income balances; and
Municipal Treasurer to –
	institute more effective collection efforts to improve tax collection efficiency of the Municipality;
	on a timely basis, prepare an accurate and updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Taxes Due (CLTTD) and submit the same to the Accounting Office as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET Receivables in the beginning of the year; and
	strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as provided in Section 256 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal General Services Office to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies, prepare, and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, in compliance with the set guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman and its TWG to –
ensure strict compliance with the requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR; and
issue Certificate of Final Acceptance with date indicated therein and for future implementation, require the contractor to post performance security and retention bond with validity until issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance of the project.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the –
Municipal Health Officer to –
	strengthen its campaign and widen the scope of its information drive to generate more demand for the vaccine and continue its vaccination roll out until the desired local herd immunity is attained;
	render a monthly report to the MGSO and MAO on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources for the proper monitoring and recording thereof; and
Municipal General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office in coordination with the MHO to determine and recognize in the reports and books the succeeding receipts and issuances of the vaccines and ancillary supplies for fair presentation of the financial statements of the Municipality.

We reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that, through the LDRRMO to –
monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMIP to ensure the full implementation of the planned activities; and
properly plan the schedule for each planned PPAs for its timely implementation.
Moreover, we also recommended that the –
Municipal General Services Officer to religiously submit the SSMI together with RISs to the Municipal Accounting Office to properly account the issued supplies and materials; and
MDRRMO, together with the MEO, to submit additional documents and pictures taken during the desilting of Masuaje River to substantiate the work done.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS –
adhere with the guidelines of reporting to the DILG of the changes on indorsed/approved GPB prior to the disbursement of funds; and
strive for the full implementation of the identified and approved PPAs by utilizing judiciously the fund set aside for said purposes.

We commended the Management for its continuous strict adherence with existing revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR. We recommended that it sustain continuous compliance with existing revenue regulations to avoid penalties which may be imposed for the violation thereof.

We commended the Management for its continued strict compliance with RA No. 8291 in the prompt remittance of the premiums deducted from the salaries of its employees and the government share. We recommended that Management continue to being compliant with the requirements of the GSIS Act.

We commended the Management specially the Accounting Office and Treasury Offices for its continued compliance with the HDMF Law in the prompt remittance of contributions and other amount due to Pag-IBIG. We recommended that Management continue its regular compliance to the HDMF regulations.
We commended the Management on the prompt remittance of the amount due to PhilHealth. We recommended that Management sustain its compliance to the PhilHealth regulations.
We recommended that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the settlement of the disallowances.
Nonetheless, we recommended that Management continue to follow strictly the existing rules and regulations with regards to cash management.

Nonetheless, we recommend that Management continue to comply strictly with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and all related rules and regulations with regard to the grant, utilization and liquidation of CAs.

Nevertheless, we recommended that the Municipal Development Council and all other concerned offices of the MGP implement fully all PPAs targeted during the year within the timeframe to ensure that anticipated local developments will be realized.

However, we recommended that the OIC-MBO retain funds for capital outlay projects not yet completed as at year-end as part of the continuing appropriations in compliance with Section 322 of RA No. 7160.
Nonetheless, we recommended that the MGP, thru its OMA, continue their commitment in achieving accuracy, reliability and transparency in financial reporting.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that Management comply strictly with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure to retain copies of MOA, even the nonnotarized ones or after signing by the LCE, as basis for the utilization of these fund transfers.  Further, furnish the AT for proper evaluation of the implementation and timely verification of the veracity and completeness of the disbursements made in relation to these trust accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Remit immediately shares of collections to the concerned agency;
Return to the concerned SA the unutilized balances of the fund transfers wherein the agreement requires the return thereof.  Otherwise, transfer as income to the general fund if the agreement does not mention of the disposition of the unused balance, subject to the approval of the grantor; and
Make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balance of the Due to NGAs and other affected accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the Contractors to post additional performance security to cover the entire Defects Liability Period of the abovementioned projects.  To ensure posting thereof, Management shall not issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance, pending compliance by the Contractors.
Henceforth, in future infrastructure projects, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to require the Contractors to post performance securities to cover the period until the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance as a condition prior to the signing of the contracts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the BAC, BAC Secretariat and its TWG to:
Conduct detailed review of actual procurement practice and exert effort to align the same with the ideal procurement timelines set forth under Annex “C” of the amended IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Adopt an operational manual applicable within the authorized functions of the BAC.

We recommended that Management require the RHU Pilar to:
Submit the PTRs and duly accomplished supporting documents to the SPO and OMA to facilitate evaluation of the receipt and issuances/utilization of COVID-19 vaccines, ancillary supplies and rice incentives and the recording of the remaining balances of unused inventories in the books and property records;
Coordinate with the PGS to secure the documents that contain the costs of the items donated as needed for the recording thereof; and
Ensure to align the inventory procedures with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management require the MHO and RHU Pilar to plan properly the utilization and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines, especially those nearly expiring to prevent wastage thereof.  For this matter, we also recommended that the DOH and its procurement committees ensure that the vaccines are of good quality and hasten the distribution thereof to target LGUs to preserve their efficacy.

We recommended that Management continue with their strategies of encouraging residents to be vaccinated in their present and future campaigns of battling the COVID-19 or other viruses for their own protection and the public at large.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the required certifications signed by the LCE or any authorized approving officials to support the travel expenses paid to JO personnel in CY 2022 amounting to ₱93,999.00, lest these will be suspended in audit.
Henceforth, the LCE direct the department heads, as immediate supervisors of JO workers, to stop the practice of allowing these personnel to attend conferences, trainings and other travel-related tasks, unless strictly in compliance with the criteria set forth in Section 3(a) of EO No. 77, series of 2019.  Also, issue certifications signed by the LCE or any authorized approving officials as required under paragraph B(5) of COA Resolution No. 2021-044 and attached the same to support future travel claims.

We recommended that Management ensure the efficient and effective implementation of LDRRM projects alongside with optimum utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen the resiliency of the community and enhance the disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the MGP.  Also, we recommended that the MBO and the MDRRMO reconcile their respective RSU of LDRRMF to report the correct information.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GFPS and GFP to:
Submit revised or supplemental GPB to the DILG Provincial Office in case of changes in GAD-related PPAs were identified and/or implemented for information and monitoring of the revisions made; and
Ensure proper monitoring of planned GAD PPAs, as well as full implementation and utilization of the GAD funds.
