observations.recommendation: We recommended that all offices and personnel concerned continue to adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines with regards to PPEs in order to safeguard the Municipality’s largest asset classification.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines with regards to PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) continue to:

Issue demand letters compelling the Accountable Officers (AO) or employees to immediately settle in full their outstanding CAs, either by liquidation, by payment/refund or through salary deductions; and 
 
Comply strictly with the requirements of Section 89 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and all related rules and regulations pertaining to CAs.
observations.managementAction:
Management continued to require concerned officers and employees to liquidate all CAs within the timeframe.

Management complied strictly to rules and regulations regarding CAs.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant (MA) and his staff deserved full recognition and appreciation for submitting the CY 2021 year-end Financial Statements of the Municipal Government of Pilar (MGP) within the legal deadline.
observations.managementAction: We recommended that the MA and his staff continue to maintain the same efficiency and diligence towards making accurate, reliable and relevant financial reports.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: The MGP implemented programs aligned with the submitted 10-year Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan while awaiting approval from the National SWM Council observing Republic Act No. 9003 promoting protection of the environment and beneficial to the general welfare of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office to intensify efforts to require the barangays for the utilization and full functionality of the barangay Material Recovery Facilities (MRF).
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for duly incorporating all COVID-19-related PPAs in the approved AIP/LDRRMFIP/supplemental AIPs in compliance with rules and regulations governing COVID-19 fund receipts and utilization.  Moreover, we recommended that Management continue to comply with necessary documentation required for fund allocation and disbursement for COVID-19-related PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to comply with the required documentation with regards to COVID-19 fund allocation and utilization.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for the substantial implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF and recommended that the Municipal Development Council and all other implementing offices of the MGP continue to maximize the utilization of the fund and implement the programs and projects within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to achieve optimum utilization of the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: Various PPAs funded by National Government Agencies (NGA) were implemented as at year-end demonstrating timely implementation and efficient fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: We commended the efforts exerted by Management in ensuring prompt implementation of the aforementioned PPAs indicating efficient and timely utilization of fund transfers received from various NGAs intended for the welfare of its constituents.  We recommended, however, that Management require the MAO to return/refund the remaining balance of the fully implemented PPAs to the Source Agencies (SA).
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Gender and Development (GAD) mechanisms and processes of the MGP were maintained and complied substantially to pursue objectively and sustain effectively GAD mainstreaming within the locality.
observations.managementAction: We commended the Municipality’s continuous effort to adhere to the mechanisms and process in accordance with the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women-Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM)-National Economic and Development Authority Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 to provide the mechanics for the development of PPAs to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of women at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres. We also recommended that Management pursue to mainstream GAD in all the Local Government Unit’s activities and operations to achieve 100 per cent gender-responsiveness.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: To protect the interest of the government, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), with the recommendation of the Municipal Treasurer (MT), direct the AO to increase the amount of her bond sufficient to cover her maximum cash accountability for a given period in accordance with the Schedule of Bonds provided by the Bureau of Treasury (BTr).
observations.managementAction: The MT shall coordinate with the BTr for the increase in the amount of bond upon renewal of Fidelity Bond.
observations.reason: Renewal of bond is scheduled on the 1st month of 2023.

observations.recommendation: Reports of Collections and Deposits (RCD) prepared by the AO were not properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: We recommended that the MT instruct all AOs under her department to accomplish properly the RCD.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that LCE require the:

Municipal Engineer to provide the MA and the designated Property Officer the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as basis for recording in their respective subsidiary records;

Property Officer to keep and maintain complete and updated LRNPCs using the prescribed format for checking the accuracy of the LRNLCs maintained by the MA; and


Property Officer and the MA to conduct regular reconciliation their records, at least on quarterly basis, to ensure that their records are consistent with each other.
observations.managementAction:
Instructed the Municipal Engineer to provide complete roads component to the MA.

Instructed the property officer to maintain the LRNPC’s and to conduct regular reconciliation of records.

Instructed the Property Officer and the MA to periodically reconcile the records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the MAO to exert more effort in tracing the concerned transactions for validation and possible collection thereof.  Otherwise, determine those deemed uncollectible and make the necessary request for write-off to COA, following the guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The MAO made necessary adjustments in the books upon validation of the related transactions.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management comply strictly with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the availability of the MOA as basis in the utilization and reporting of necessary transactions relative to fund transfers.  Also, furnish the Audit Team (AT) with the necessary MOA to be used in verifying whether the purposes of the trust funds are met.
observations.managementAction: Instructed all concerned agencies to secure a copy of MOA upon receipt of funds.
observations.reason: Failed to follow up signed and notarized MOA

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal DRRM Council to:

monitor properly the utilization of LDDRM Fund;


include only eligible PPAs in the LDRRMFIP; and


use the unexpended and excess balances of the LDRRMF by incorporating these in the LDRRMFIP and submit the same, including supplemental Investment Programs, if any, to the AT within the prescribed period.

We also recommended that the LCE instruct the LDRRM Officer (LDRRMO) and the MAO to coordinate properly so that accurate figures will be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction:
Instructed the LDRRMO to prepare monthly utilization report.

Management adhered to the rule of excluding ineligible PPAs.

Management prepared and submitted the LDRRMFIP.


Instructed the LDRRMO to coordinate properly to the MAO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the efficient implementation and optimum utilization of the LDRRMF, including savings from continuing appropriations and prior years’ unexpended balances, on programs and activities to promote a more disaster resilient community and help reduce disaster risks.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the LDRRMO to implement all PPAs in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

MT to provide the LSB with the correct income estimate of Special Education Tax (SET) in the preparation of annual SEF Budget;

LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program supported by approved School Improvement Plans (SIP) and other information from Department of Education (DepEd) Regional Office, which supposedly were the basis for the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget, and to furnish the AT for reference and review purposes; and


Strictly comply with the requirement of DepEd-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2017 in identifying the PPAs that reflect the equitable distribution of mandated priority projects/programs.
observations.managementAction:
Required the MT to provide correct income estimate of SET.

Management required the LSB to prepare 3-year plan taking into consideration the recommendation of DepEd, Public Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) and SDS.

To require Four Districts Supervisors to comply and adhere to JMC No. 1, series of 2017 for effective implementation of the projects.
observations.reason:
Late submission of the Consolidated SIPs. 




Noncompliance of the requirements due to late submission of SIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned offices to submit complete supporting documents on purchased goods for distribution particularly the list of targeted beneficiaries and distribution list duly signed by recipients for items actually received.  Accordingly, we recommended that the LCE direct the MAO to check the completeness of documentation before submitting these to COA.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the concerned offices to submit complete supporting documents and to prepare distribution list to all beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require thru the offices-in-charge to:

Revisit operations to determine the bottleneck and the reasons why the said grants were not yet implemented and utilized as to date;


Identify measures that would address the reasons and act accordingly; and


Ensure timely and efficient utilization of funds, more importantly those coming from the NGAs as required in the MOA, existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Directed the Municipal Engineer to monitor strictly the timeliness of project implementation.

Prioritization of PPAs to be implemented.

Instructed the MAO to return all balances /unitized fund.
observations.reason: Still in the process of ensuring the utilization of funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:


The Local Finance Committee to conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets; and 


The MT’s Office and other departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to perform efficiently in the revenue administration by considering conducting a periodic review to ascertain whether collection targets are attained and in case of shortfall in collections, to analyze the reasons and to look into alternative actions to avoid incurrence of lapses in projection in the future.
observations.managementAction:
Instructed the Local Finance Committee to consider the historical data and latest trends in forecasting revenue

Management will formulate a comprehensive collection strategy.
observations.reason: Lack of strategies to improve revenue generation and lack of collectors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Initiate measures to collect the delinquencies from market rentals amounting to almost ₱2.0 million and employ strategies to intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and


Ensure that the market stall holders have valid and subsisting “Contract of Lease”.


We also recommended that the Local Sangguniang Bayan enact a new or revised Market Code to adjust the rates to current levels.
observations.managementAction:
Management will intensify collection of delinquent rentals.


Management will require all stall holders to renew the Contract of Lease.

Management will update regularly the Market Code.
observations.reason:
Vacant Market Stalls are being rented out but its viability is still under evaluation.


Some stall holders are in financial distress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current designated Accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018 | On-going reconciliation for the Due from NGAs account
observations.reason: Accounts were not yet traced.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make written representation to the BIR in order to (a) ascertain whether the activities are taxable or not, (b) apply for an Authority to Print (ATP) for receipts or invoices and pay the Annual Registration Fee, if necessary, (c) determine the taxes due, if applicable.
observations.reason: No supporting documents submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit written justification on the procurement of medical supplies with prices exceeding the price ceiling provided by the DOH. Otherwise, require the supplier to refund the excess amount computed based on the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0144A and 2020-0250A.  

We further recommend that management observe the DPRI and other related issuances of the DOH in the succeeding procurement of medicines and medical supplies pursuant to Republic Act No. 7581 and DOH Administrative Order No. 2014-0001.
observations.reason: Excessive medical supplies were noted in CY 2022 (Reiterated in Finding No. 3)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). 

We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department Circular 001-2015.
observations.reason: To draft a letter to request approval from BLGF to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require coordination between the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in the identification of all completed public infrastructure projects based on the transfers recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the registries of public infrastructures in preparation of the five-year transitional application for the recognition of the Public Infrastructures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) application.
observations.reason: Management had difficulty in tracing projects since the current Accountant has no data of the Public Infrastructure from the very beginning.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Finance Committee make representation to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) on the mandatory posting requirements of the accumulated reports of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities (BGCM) Funds in the absence of the LGU’s official website.

We also recommended that the LGU provide or create its own official website in order to comply the posting requirements of the DBM and enhance its transparency reporting.
observations.managementAction: Management is planning to upgrade the Internet speed of the LGU to comply the posting requirements of the DBM.
observations.reason: Due to slow internet connection, management still not able to comply with the posting requirements of the DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.
observations.managementAction: Management is planning to upgrade the Internet speed of the LGU to comply the posting requirements of the DBM.
observations.reason: Due to slow internet connection, management still not able to comply the posting requirements of the DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the day care centers and conduct inventory of the textbooks and instructional materials and submit the inventory report to the Accounting Office to support the amount of inventory recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to comply the documentary requirements for the write off of the Loans Receivable account for verification and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management is still in the process of securing the related documentary requirements.
observations.reason: The related account still exist as of Dec. 31, 2021

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish an Internal Audit Service Office with sufficient support from the top management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management is planning the creation of Internal Audit Office but constrained with the additional PS may entail.
observations.reason: The LGU has not created the Internal Audit Office as of CY 2021 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials or offices responsible to submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management still failed to comply the submission of the said documents.
observations.reason: In CY 2021 audit, the Audit Team still did not receive the said documents within specified time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management return to the National Treasury the unutilized / unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.
observations.managementAction: The management still did not return the said fund.
observations.reason: In CY 2021 Audit, the said amount still exists.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Disposal Committee to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties to the directives of Sections 379, 380 and 381 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management already conducted physical count of PPE but still unable to disposed the Unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: As of CY 2021 audit, the LGU have not yet able to disposed the Unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit proof of submission of the APP to the GPPB as well the explanation as to the non-submission of the PMR.

observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned personnel exert effort to monitor the status of the agricultural produce for distribution and breeding stocks and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the review and analysis of the details of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly impose the bonding requirements of collectors and assign back to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office the Revenue Collection Clerk who was assigned at the Mayor’ Office to maximize her services.  Also, stop the practice of assigning the tasks of collecting to personnel hired as casual / job orders.
observations.managementAction: No appropriation in the Annual Budget.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply the existing regulations for the establishment of the LDDRM Office and fill up the mandatory positions to perform the functions of administration & training, research & planning and operations & warning.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth the concerned municipal officials adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 7160 in the computation of PS limitation to avoid excess expenditures for Personal Services.
observations.managementAction: Continued implementation of the Salary Standardization Law (4th Tranche)
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management require the concerned Municipal employees to refund the cost of gasoline issued to privately owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that contract documents for all procurement of infrastructure projects contain all the general and special conditions required of a contract and to provide protection to the rights of the LGU when a matter relating to the poor or non-performance by the contractor occurs. 

We recommended further, that the municipality pursue forfeiture of performance bond in favor of the LGU in case the contractor falls short in performing its contractual obligations to the LGU for the construction of these projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should require the Municipal Livestock Coordinator, who is in – charge of the dispersal program of the municipality, to monitor the project, prepare and submit a report regularly to the Accounting Division any increase in the livestock inventory due to returns from recipients as well as the mortality duly supported with an affidavit and witnessed by any of the barangay officials to ensure the reliability, accuracy and validity of  the recorded amount in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Reports were not prepared and the livestock were not monitored regularly.

Ensure that rules and regulations with regard to the procurement of medicines are observed at all times.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above noted deficiencies, we recommended and Management agreed to properly program all the trust fund for DRRM from prior years unutilized balance as required under Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 7.

observations.recommendation:
1.13	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct regular monthly and quarterly reconciliation of their books of accounts and prepare report of reconciliation and copy furnish the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days of the ensuing month. Regarding deposit, we recommended further that the Municipal Treasurer record in the cashbook upon obtaining validated deposit slip, otherwise defer the recording to the date of bank validation and furnish the same to Municipal Accountant. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant furnish the Municipal Treasurer adjustments made in the books so that these could be recorded in the cashbook.
1.14	During exit conference, the management was well taken the recommendation and agreed to take appropriate actions.

2.8	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis to set up beginning of the year. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET receivable at the beginning of the year using the certified list of taxpayers. Also, require the Municipal Assessor to update the list of taxpayers duly certified indicating the amount and collectible for the year 2021 and thereafter and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer.
2.9	During exit conference, the management was well taken the recommendation and agreed to take appropriate actions.

3.16	We recommended that Management direct the concerned Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of inventory and reconcile with the Accounting Office, of the records and report on physical count of PPE in order that appropriate adjustments of the accounts. Likewise, direct the GSO to update the property cards and require the accounting office to prepare and maintain PPE ledger cards.
3.17	During exit conference, the management was well taken the recommendation and agreed to take appropriate actions.

4.11	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the concerned Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate and render account for their long overdue cash advances or refund in favor of the government the unliquidated amount, if applicable.
4.12	During exit conference, the management was well taken the recommendation and agreed to take appropriate actions.

5.8	We recommended that Management direct the BAC officials to submit explanations/justifications on why the transaction should not be considered illegal. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant will not process the disbursement without complete supporting documents.
5.9	During exit conference, the management was well taken the recommendation and agreed to take appropriate actions.

6.11	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation for each bank account and submit the same together with the supporting documents within 10 days of the ensuing month and thereafter, to the Office of the Auditor.
6.12	During exit conference, the management was well taken the recommendation and agreed to take appropriate actions.

7.6	We recommended that Management strictly adhere in the application of savings for augmentation and supplemental budget by way of realignment, is supported with ordinance, consistent with the provision cited above. Further, we recommended that the said reprogrammed projects for the new PPAs should be supported with supplemental Investment Program and the certificate of funds available for appropriations duly under oath by the local treasurer and local accountant and attested by the local chief executive.
7.7	During exit conference, the management explained that the low utilization and implementation of the project was due to pandemic crisis where the immediate implementation is uncertain as to its planned. However, the recommendation was well taken by the Management and agreed to take appropriate actions.

Further, we recommended that all offices and personnel concerned continue to adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines with regards to PPEs in order to safeguard the Municipality’s largest asset classification.

Likewise, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the MAO continue to:
Issue demand letters compelling the AOs or employees to immediately settle in full their outstanding CAs, either by liquidation, by payment/refund or through salary deductions; and
Comply strictly with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and all related rules and regulations pertaining to CAs.
We also recommended that the MA and his staff continue to maintain the same efficiency and diligence towards making accurate, reliable and relevant financial reports.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management instruct the MENRO to intensify efforts to require the barangays for the utilization and full functionality of the barangay MRFs.

We commended Management for duly incorporating all COVID-19-related PPAs in the approved AIP/LDRRMFIP/supplemental AIPs in compliance with rules and regulations governing COVID-19 fund receipts and utilization.  Moreover, we recommended that Management continue to comply with necessary documentation required for fund allocation and disbursement for COVID-19-related PPAs.

Management Comment:
Management assures full implementation/utilization of PPAs under 20% DF.
We recommended, however, that Management require the MAO to return/refund the remaining balance of the fully implemented PPAs to the SAs.

Management Comment:
The Office of the GAD is committed to maintain all the positive and commendable findings.

We recommended that the LCE, with the recommendation of the Municipal Treasurer, direct the AO to increase the amount of her bond sufficient to cover her maximum cash accountability for a given period in accordance with the Schedule of Bonds provided by the Bureau of Treasury.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct all AOs under her department to accomplish properly the RCD.

We recommended that LCE require the:
Municipal Engineer to provide the MA and the designated Property Officer the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as basis for recording in their respective subsidiary records;
Property Officer to keep and maintain complete and updated LRNPCs using the prescribed format for checking the accuracy of the LRNLCs maintained by the MA; and
Property Officer and the MA to conduct regular reconciliation their records, at least on quarterly basis, to ensure that their records are consistent with each other.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the MAO to exert more effort in tracing the concerned transactions for validation and possible collection thereof.  Otherwise, determine those deemed uncollectible and make the necessary request for write-off to COA, following the guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management comply strictly with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the availability of the MOA as basis in the utilization and reporting of necessary transactions relative to fund transfers.  Also, furnish the AT with the necessary MOA to be used in verifying whether the purposes of the trust funds are met.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MDRRM Council to:
monitor properly the utilization of LDDRM Fund;
include only eligible PPAs in the LDRRMFIP; and
use the unexpended and excess balances of the LDRRMF by incorporating these in the LDRRMFIP and submit the same, including supplemental Investment Programs, if any, to the AT within the prescribed period.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the LDRRMO and the MAO to coordinate properly so that accurate figures will be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that Management ensure the efficient implementation and optimum utilization of the LDRRMF, including savings from continuing appropriations and PYs unexpended balances, on programs and activities to promote a more disaster resilient community and help reduce disaster risks.

We recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Treasurer to provide the LSB with the correct income estimate of SET in the preparation of annual SEF Budget;
LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program supported by approved SIPs and other information from DepEd Regional Office, which supposedly were the basis for the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget, and to furnish the AT for reference and review purposes; and
Strictly comply with the requirement of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 in identifying the PPAs that reflect the equitable distribution of mandated priority projects/programs.

We recommended that Management require the concerned offices to submit complete supporting documents on purchased goods for distribution particularly the list of targeted beneficiaries and distribution list duly signed by recipients for items actually received.  Accordingly, we recommended that the LCE direct the MAO to check the completeness of documentation before submitting these to COA.
We recommended that Management require the job order employees to apply for Pag-IBIG MID and immediately inform the MAO for remittance purposes.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct Acting Municipal Treasurer to monitor the issuance of Accountable
Forms to collectors and refrain from issuing additional Official Receipts to
collectors unless remittance of collections are made; and
b. Require all Accountable Officers/Collectors to observe daily remittance of their
collections and intact as prescribed to lessen the risk of misuse and loss
of fund and strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 29 of the Manual on
NGAS Vol. I.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to keep track of documents relative to
Properties of the Municipality;
b. Come up with proposed budget for litigation, titling, relocation and make
representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the appropriation of
funds necessary to pursue the claim and transfer of Torrens Title in its name of
the Municipality; and
c. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entry to Land account
and Other Prepayments.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 5% of the CY’s annual budget;
b. Direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
to properly take up the difference in the mandatory allocation of 5% in the
General Fund amounting to P296,888.20;
c. Direct the MDRRMO to implement PPAs in the Annual Investment Plan for
LDRRMF and cause the preparation of a supplemental or updated LDRRMIP
as necessary to include other activities not originally covered by the
appropriation and changes in priorities; and
d. Cause the timely submission of Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF.
Henceforth, the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
should strictly adhere with RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommended and management agreed to include only items and
expenditures that are chargeable to LDRRMF. Submit written justification on the
purpose why Administrative and operating expenditures were charged to LDRRMF
instead of Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) or Capital Outlay
(CO) of the General Fund.
Henceforth, Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
should strictly include only activities that adhere to guidelines in the preparation
and utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply documentary
requirements in every government expenditure transaction; and
b. Direct OIC-Municipal Accountant to reclassify the accounts and to adhere to
provisions and descriptions as provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 Chart of
Accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel to
furnished the Municpal Accountant, the documents necessary to record the
reciept and distribution of vaccines and Inventory Reports or its equivalent in
relation to the vaccination program; and
b. Direct the Municpal Accountant to record using the perpetual inventory
system, the vaccines received and its distribution as reported by the LGU to
DOH-CHD and draw journal entry voucher for the applicable inventory
accounts to record the remaining vaccines in stock at December 31, 2021.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of contracts
within the prescribed period to facilitate timely auditorial review, technical
evaluation and inspection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
The Management commented that copies of contracts and purchase orders will be
submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period in CY 2022.

We recommended and management agreed that OIC - Municipal Accountant to
submit immediately the accounts and reports listed which were already overdue;
and
Henceforth, strictly comply with that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of
P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly
complied with in a timely manner.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Require the GSO to prepare and submit to the OIC-Municipal Accountant the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original
copies of the RIS and cause the timely submission within five days from
month-end, to facilitate the proper recording of transaction and ensure
propriety and accuracy of inventory balances and expenses accounts;
b. Require GSO and other supplies/inventory custodians to undertake the actual
physical inventory at least once a year and to submit RPCI to the Office of the
Auditor not later than January 31 each year; and
c. Enjoin GSO/all inventory custodians and Municipal Accountant to reconcile of
the inventory balances in the books of accounts.
(This is reiterated from last year’s Audit Observation).

We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents and written justification on the deficiencies noted above.
Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds, specifically on the grant of COVID-19 AHDP and COA Circular 2012-001, among other pertinent provisions.

We recommend that the management submit written justification and the lacking supporting documents.
We also recommend that concerned personnel provide sufficient details in the Purchase Request, Purchase Order, and Inspection and Acceptance Report.
Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations specifically on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, among other pertinent provisions.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the procurement of medical supplies with prices exceeding the price ceiling provided by the DOH. Otherwise, require the supplier to refund the excess amount computed based on the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0413 and 2021-0223, and DOH Department Circular No. 2021-0291.
We further recommend that management observe the DPRI and other related issuances of the DOH in the succeeding procurement of medicines and medical supplies pursuant to Republic Act No. 7581 and DOH Administrative Order No. 2014-0001.

We recommend the management to strictly implement the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of its inventory of PPE thru the creation of an Inventory Committee who shall:
Prepare, in coordination with Property Supply Office, and Accounting Office, and submit the PIP for approval to the Head of the Agency to ensure that the conduct of inventory is properly and adequately planned;
Submit a Copy of the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities; and
Complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that management exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the reconciling items, make representation with the bank for the furnishing of the original copies of debit and credit advices, and record the reconciling items and stale checks in the books.
Further, require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant expedite the reconciliation and tracing of long outstanding/dormant accounts. It is further recommended that adjusting entries be made on errors and misclassification in order to present a reliable balance of the Due to LGUs.
We also recommend that the municipal accountant monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the required adjusting entries to correct the erroneous transactions to its appropriate accounts.
We further recommend that in the succeeding years, record the transactions to its appropriate accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009, in order to reflect accurate account balances in the financial statements.

We recommend that management demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.
We further recommend that management file for request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts if it meets the criteria stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommend that management submit the written justification/s on the above-noted findings including the lacking supporting documents.
We further recommend that management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds specifically on DPWH Department Order 197 and RA 9184 and its Revised IRR, among other pertinent provisions. Otherwise, irregular/illegal expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management submit the written justification/s on the above-noted findings including the lacking supporting documents.
We further recommend that management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds specifically on DPWH Department Order 197 and RA 9184 and its Revised IRR, among other pertinent provisions. Otherwise, irregular/illegal expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management explain why the projects were provided in generic terms and do not contain sufficient details and cost of each project. Also, require from the Local Development Council a list of duly identified projects in the formulation of future annual budgets.
We further recommend that the Municipal Mayor secure first approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to implementation of programs/projects/activities funded out of the lumpsum appropriation under the 20% DF pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2010-014.

We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund.  Further, ensure that identified projects are implemented within the year in order to deliver the intended benefits and to get the maximum value of the fund considering the increasing prices of materials and labor costs.

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic Act 10121.

We recommend that management submit written justification/comment on the excess of ABC and total contract price versus the COA Cost Estimate.
We further recommend that management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the preparation of ABC, in accordance with the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR. Otherwise, irregular/illegal expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that Management require the concerned municipal officials/employee to immediately submit the deficient financial reports and ensure that the prescribed periods in the submission of reports are observed. Failure to submit these reports on time shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries pursuant to Sections 122 of PD 1445.
We recommended and Management agreed and already prepared the adjusting journal entries to reconcile the said accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed and already prepared the necessary adjusting entry to correct the cash in bank account by debiting the Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account and crediting the Accounts Payable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the refund of the amount of penalty or bank service charges for falling below the required maintaining balance and further instruct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor the cash in bank balance to avoid incurrence of service charges/penalty.

We recommended that Management, through the HRMO, exert more effort in filling in the vacant mandatory positions in order to attain the full implementation of the Municipality’s programs, projects and activities for the optimal benefit of the constituents. If possible, coordinate with the Civil Service Commission to boost the pool of qualified candidates in the selection process.

We recommended and Management agreed that they will establish clear cut guidelines on the grant of AICS, such as the qualification of indigent, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements, and the like, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, to provide proper control in the disbursement of public funds.

We recommended that Management submit a valid explanation on why there was no competitive/public bidding was made in procuring the services of a Media and Public Information Consultant. We further recommended that the Management, through the Bids and Awards Committee, ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 by determining the appropriate mode of procurement to be used based on the condition of each procurement.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the refund of the amount paid to the Private Secretary and stop the practice of paying travelling expenses that are personal in nature and adhere to Section 3 of EO No. 77 on paying the claims for travelling expenses.

We recommended and Management agreed that the procurement of meals and snacks or catering services for its various activities will be done through competitive bidding or any alternative mode, when justified by conditions, following the guidelines prescribed in RA 9184, its IRR and related issuances.

We recommended and Management agreed that copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods and services will be submitted within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant request for snap shots from the bank every end of the month. Designate a focal person who would be coordinating and responsible in monitoring the status of project and/or programs implementation of funds transferred from SAs, be it from NGA, GOCC or LGU.
observations.managementAction:
Coordinated with the PGS 

Ensured MOA in all grants and/or fund transfers

Assigned concerned officer in monitoring the status of PPAs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE inform the ATL within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:

Strictly comply to regularly prepare and submit an approved PMR in accordance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and in the format prescribed in GPPB Circular No. 03-2017; and

Prepare the APP based on the prescribed format as provided in GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and ensure that all Project Procurement and Management Plan are properly consolidated and procurement are entirely incorporated to reflect the transparency of procurement activities within the Municipality.

Informed the BAC to prepare and submit APP based on the consolidated PPMP

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and all other implementing offices of the Municipality maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 pandemic

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent DF re-alignments be used appropriately for its intended projects/programs/activities and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the applicable guidelines and/or regulations.
observations.managementAction: Strictly adhered to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2020-01

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to use the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to incorporate the PPAs to be funded as well as current appropriations, including realignments pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to boost the attainment of a disaster resilient community.

Also, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to submit to the Auditor the LDRRMFIP and the supplemental investment program, if any, within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
Only eligible PPAs were included in the LDRRMFIP

Require the MDRRMO to submit to the auditor the LDRRMFIP

observations.recommendation:

Optimally use or utilize the LDRRMF through the full implementation of the   projects and activities allowed in order to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks; and

Ensure that realignments are properly approved by the local sanggunian and subsequently furnish the Auditor a copy of the approved resolution with its corresponding documents, as necessary.
observations.managementAction:
Implementing fully the PPAs stipulated in the LDRRMF to be able to optimally use the LDRRMF

Complying strictly with the process of the realignments
observations.reason:
Still in the process to fully implement PPAs

Still in the process of complying the needed process for realignments

Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review of the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payment on claims; and

Employ alternative ways such as designation of alternate person-in-charge in case of work arrangements to avoid deficiencies and strictly adhere to the existing rules and regulations as to documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant adheres to the rules and laws applicable to the financial transactions and to conduct thorough review of the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payments on claim

Moreover, we recommended that the SEF Budget be specifically presented by PPAs, by allotment class and per school.

LSB adhere to the format presented in Form 1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC. No. 1, s. 2017, in the proper computation and presentation of the SEF Budget.

Municipal Treasurer prepared the correct SET income estimates

LSB strictly observed the prescribed format

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the Bayanihan Grant allocations be used appropriately for its intended PPAs and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of LBC No. 125.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) continue updating and maintaining the GAD Database.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

The Local Finance Committee to conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets; and 

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and other departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to perform efficiently in the revenue administration by considering conducting a periodic review to ascertain whether collection targets are attained and in case of shortfall in collections, to analyze the reasons and to look into alternative actions to avoid incurrence of lapses in projection in the future.
observations.managementAction:
Give more time to the Local Finance Committee in conducting revenue forecasting to come up with more realistic forecast.

Enjoined all revenue generating offices to be more efficient in revenue generation and administration, to schedule periodic evaluation & its performance so that corrective action can be effected in the event of shortfall in the target.
observations.reason:
Still in the process of availing remedies in enforcing payments particularly in tax delinquency

Still in the process of identifying strategies to improve revenue generation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Initiate measures to collect the delinquencies from market rentals amounting to almost ₱2.0 million and employ strategies to intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and

Ensure that the market stall holders have a valid and subsisting “Contract of Lease”.

We also recommended that the Local Sangguniang Bayan enact a new or revised Market Code to adjust the rates to current levels.
observations.managementAction:
Measures were being implemented to collect delinquencies from market rental based on the ordinance passed by the SB giving tenants long term in paying their arrears.

Contract of Lease of stall holders were inspected to check existence and validity.

Revised Market Code was already undergone four public hearing in the SB and the fifth public hearing was scheduled within the month.
observations.reason:
Payment of delinquencies of stall holder are in installment basis.

Contract of Lease of some stall holders were not yet reviewed because of their inability to pay at least ¼ of their arrears.

Market Code was in the process of revision.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineering Office to expedite the preparation of Initial Environmental Examination so that upon issuance of Environmental Compliance Certificate, the construction of the Sanitary Landfill could already be initiated and the cost to provide services for the disposal of solid waste could be reduced or eliminated.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office already prepared the Initial Environmental Examination.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. Also, require the MT/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant, which shall be used as basis in recording issuance.

We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or GSO as the case may be, conduct physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies and the Municipal accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2015, 2016, 2017 | In CY 2021, the report for the physical count of inventory was submitted to the COA Audit Team. The Accounting Office also maintained the supplies inventory schedule.
observations.reason: Perpetual inventory method was used in costing of the supplies inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize the asset as Construction in Progress and record back the liability account. Upon submission of full liquidation to the grantor, the assets in the Trust Fund be closed to equity or Due to LGUs and transferred to the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity.

We further recommend that succeeding expenditures in the Trust Fund be made in accordance with Sections 97 and 104 of the NGAS Volume 1, to avoid misstatements in the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | All CY 2019 Trust Fund transactions were recorded using the appropriate asset/ expense account and closed to the due/payable account; and transferred to General Fund upon liquidation.
observations.reason: JEV has been submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit written justification on the non-inclusion of warranty clause in the contracts/purchase orders. Further, strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 pertaining to warranty clause in order to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Written justification has been submitted by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted within 10 days from receipt of Bank Statements. The failure to submit required reports may warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, | Bank Reconciliation Statements were prepared and submitted to COA. Adjustments were also made.
observations.reason: Some documents to support the preparation of the necessary journal entries were not available/furnished to the current Accountant. There were still reconciling items carried over from previous years.  (Reiterated in Finding No. 5)

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpay er’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year. 

We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2020 | The Treasurer furnished a soft copy of the list of taxpayers with the assessed value of the real properties. Also, there were adjustments made last October 2018.

Further, strictly follow with the provision of Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 with regard to the computation of liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Also, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.
