observations.recommendation:
1.6 We have recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to locate records of road
networks in their offices where the necessary data, specifications and
locations of the Road Networks may be sourced to facilitate the
preparation and submission of the aforementioned required reports and
36
the proper recognition of Road Networks and their corresponding
depreciation in the accounting books.

1.7 We have also recommended and Management agreed to ensure
preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and its
components and the disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial
Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

2.9 We have recommended and Management agreed to see to it that the
DRRMF unexpended/unobligated balances are duly transferred to a
special trust fund account to avoid misuse of funds and to effect proper
accounting entries in compliance with the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

3.7 We have recommended and Management agreed that FSs, DVs and ORs
should be submitted within prescribed period to enable timely audit of
account balances and transactions.

4.11 We have recommended and Management agreed:
• That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement
vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation. In no
case shall a disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting
documents; and
• That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately
submitted to COA for evaluation and review.

We have recommended that Management submit justification as to why
reimbursements method was resorted instead of alternative mode of
procurement prescribed by the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, for
evaluation.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit
the lacking documents and ensure that in future transactions all
required documents are attached and reviewed before processing the
disbursement vouchers and making payment thereof. Ensure that rules
and regulations with regard to the procurement of medicines are
observed at all times.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2020 to COA for
audit purposes, if any; and
• Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis
guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA for
audit purposes.

We have recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Auditor a
copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five
working days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of
contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for
technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No.
2009-001.

We have recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to
submit the required documents mentioned above. Henceforth, comply
with the rules and regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA
9184.

11.4 We have recommended and Management agreed to utilize the
unexpended balance of allocated funds for the intended purposes after
due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns taking into
consideration current developments and to accordingly re-appropriate
the funds of PPA which have become irrelevant or not responsive to
current priority undertakings.

12.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to limit the utilization of
the 20% Development Fund on programs, projects and activities that will
contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes that shall partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures that will yield greater benefit to the
people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2017-1.

13.7 We have recommended and Management agreed to take necessary action
to follow up or inquire the status of the approval of the CLUP at the
Sangguniang Panlalawigan in order for the Municipality to have a more
improved planning and implementation of long-term development goals.

14.10 We have recommended and Management agreed to:
• Comply with the provisions that require the allocation to GAD
activities of a minimum of 5% of the Municipality’s annual total
budget; and
• Submit the required supporting documents for further verification of
the submitted GAD Accomplishment Report.

15.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD
Focal Point System to:
• Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for
gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation:
• Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender
issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are
responsive to the said issues; and
• Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for
particular gender issues.

We therefore recommended and Management agreed to reimburse the BGCM, through the General Fund, of inappropriate expenses charged to the fund prior to its receipt totaling ₱3,341,290.82. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer meticulously examine transactions and ensure that there is an existing appropriation and funds are available before entering into contract. We also recommended that the:
Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Budget Officer ensures that PPAs indicated in the approved Supplemental Annual Investment Program are followed to prevent incurrence of an overdraft and implementation of PPAs;
BAC, thru its Secretariat,
post the -
NOA, Contract/PO and NTP in the PhiGEPS website and Agency’s website for procurements undertaken under “Negotiated Procurement-Emergency Cases” of Section 53.2 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184;
NOA, Contract/PO and NTP for procurements undertaken under “Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act”, if any, and Approved revised Annual Investment Plan for CY 2020 in the GPPB Online Portal (www.gppbgovph.com)
ensure compliance in the posting requirements of all procurements in accordance with the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184, GPPB Circular No. 04-2016 dated December 20, 2016, and Item 9.0 of GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020; and
Municipal Accountant to –
immediately submit all the documents enumerated in Item Number 6 of this AOM; and
meticulously review and ensure that all DVs are supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular 2012-001 and in compliance with relevant rules and regulations before making any payment to provide reasonable basis for establishing the validity of claims.

We recommended and Management agreed  –
through the concerned personnel, to refund the excess payment made to them totaling to ₱35,750.00;
that the Human Resource Management Officer submit the office orders or the approved work schedule and the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking supporting documents and the unsubmitted liquidation voucher for evaluation of the Audit Team, without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Suspension, if warranted by evidence; and
that the Municipal Accountant check and ensure the completeness of the supporting documents before affixing her signature in the vouchers.

In view of the above noted deficiencies, we recommended and Management agreed to –
stop using the DRRMF to expenses not chargeable thereto;
reimburse the calamity fund from the general fund the amounts improperly charged to the fund; and
properly program all the trust fund for DRRM from prior years unutilized balance as required under Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We likewise commended the Management for its responsive actions against COVID-19 and ASF as guided by the rules and regulations laid down by the National Government.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive –

instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the updated Real Property Assessment Roll generated by the PATAS in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I, including the amount of taxes due and collectible from them;
require the Municipal Treasurer to continue to update the list of real property units and the related status of payments; and
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in support of the RPT and SET Receivable balances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the ---
Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory of the real properties and report their actual conditions through the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), which shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year;
Accounting Office effect the necessary adjusting entry to present fairly the Land account; and
Management include in the succeeding year’s budget the cost of titling of all untitled/titled lots.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant –
provide a Schedule of valid claimants and the complete documentation of the obligated expenses at year-end; and
ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents, otherwise, these should not be taken up in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct MSWMB, through the MENRO, to –
coordinate with the barangay officials, specifically with the BSWMB, regarding the waste segregation at source, re-use and recycling of wastes so as to enable them to implement diversion of wastes; and
conduct monthly validation/monitoring of the barangay compliance on Solid Waste Management in order to measure the status of the implementation of the program;
Likewise, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation through the MSWB-
fully comply with its mandated functions as enumerated in R.A. No. 9003 and its IRR, revisit and modify priorities, if applicable, as the plan is being implemented, and pursue its plan, as contained in its MSWMP; and
establish a long-term institutionalized process aimed at having law abiding citizens through the implementation of laws, rules and regulation and develop on enormous number of people who have the love and care for the environment.

We reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that –
Municipal Planning and Development Office ensure that the fund is appropriated and utilized in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017;
Municipal Engineer properly schedule and monitor the implementation of the projects so that early achievement of its objective would be attained; and
strictly ensure compliance with the requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point gather and analyze sex-disaggregated data for integration to the CBMS to provide a more complete understanding of GAD issues and concerns, thus formulate PPAs addressing thereof.

We commended the Management for its continuous strict adherence with existing revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR. We recommended that it sustain continuous compliance with existing revenue regulations to avoid penalties which may be imposed for the violation thereof.

We commended the Management for its continued strict compliance with RA No. 8291 in the prompt remittance of the premiums deducted from the salaries of its employees and the government share. We recommended that Management continue to being compliant with the requirements of the GSIS Act.

We commended the Management specially the Accounting Office and Treasury Offices for its continued compliance with the HDMF Law in the prompt remittance of contributions and other amount due to Pag-IBIG. We recommended that Management continue its regular compliance to the HDMF regulations.
We commended the Management on the prompt remittance of the amount due to PhilHealth. We recommended that Management sustain its compliance to the PhilHealth regulations.
We recommended that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the disallowances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant adopt the perpetual inventory method in purchase of inventories and to create control procedures on its receipt and issuances.
We also recommend that the Property Custodian submit the actual inventory of the supplies at year-end and that the corresponding asset and expense account be adjusted by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that the management submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted above, and submit other documents to support its claims.
Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-003, among other pertinent provisions, to safeguard government funds.

We recommend that the management submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted above.
Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-003, among other pertinent provisions, to safeguard government funds.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the procurement of medical supplies with prices exceeding the price ceiling provided by the DOH. Otherwise, require the supplier to refund the excess amount computed based on the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0144A and 2020-0250A.
We further recommend that management observe the DPRI and other related issuances of the DOH in the succeeding procurement of medicines and medical supplies pursuant to Republic Act No. 7581 and DOH Administrative Order No. 2014-0001.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the deficiencies noted above.
We further recommend that management strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of public funds specifically on COA Circular No. 94-013 and Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020, among other pertinent provisions, to safeguard government funds.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the non-inclusion of warranty clause in the contracts/purchase orders. Further, strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 pertaining to warranty clause in order to protect the interest of the government.

We recommend that management require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

We recommend that the Property Officer initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for all property, plant and equipment of the municipality.
We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the results of the physical inventory count and make adjustments, if any. Furthermore, submit to the Auditor the reconciliation report and adjustments made for verification.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the recipient of fund transfers for the submission of liquidations reports or settlement of receivables.
We further recommend that management discontinue the practice of granting fund transfers to the Implementing Agencies with unliquidated balances and a certification by the Accountant be required for succeeding transfers.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit justification on the low collection ratio on RPT and SET; and in the difference of the balances of the two accounts.
We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We recommend that management submit written explanation on the non-deduction of liquidated damages from the contractor of the 3 BEA projects, and if after validation warrants the imposition of the same, demand from the contractor the liquidated damages not deducted from the final payment.
Further, strictly follow with the provision of Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 with regard to the computation of liquidated damages.

We recommend that management make written representation to the BIR in order to (a) ascertain whether the activities are taxable or not, (b) apply for an Authority to Print (ATP) for receipts or invoices and pay the Annual Registration Fee, if necessary, (c) determine the taxes due, if applicable.

We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7.1 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended that Management observed the guidelines issued by the DOH in terms of the Price freeze of essential medicines and devices and explain on the excess in the price of the non-thermal scanner purchased.

We recommended that Management submit distribution list for the relief goods distributed to constituents and ensure that control procedures and complete documentation are established for claims against government funds.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee make representation to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) on the mandatory posting requirements of the accumulated reports of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities (BGCM) Funds in the absence of the LGU’s official website.

We also recommended that the LGU provide or create its own official website in order to comply the posting requirements of the DBM and enhance its transparency reporting.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.

We recommended that Management submit the approved payrolls and submit justification for the processing of unapproved payrolls. Henceforth, Management practice processing payrolls only when it bears the certification and approval from proper officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that an appropriation ordinance is passed authorizing the said augmentation. Henceforth, we recommend strict observance of Article 454 of the IRR of RA 7160 implementing Section 336 of RA 7160.
We recommended that Management submit justification on the utilization of the appropriation other than its intended purpose to establish validity of disbursements and avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Agency prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement in a timely manner in compliance with the rules and regulations to properly substantiate the Cash in Bank balance reflected in the accounting records.
We recommended that Management stop the practice of reprogramming of savings from Personal Services to Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses items in the Supplemental Budget.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the immediate implementation of identified priority programs/projects/activities funded from the 20% Development Fund.
Likewise, implementation of programs and projects related to COVID - 19 response be given utmost priority to address the imminent threat to public health and improving recovery efforts during the pandemic.

We recommended and Management agreed the following:
a. Direct the Acting GSO to submit to the Disposal Committee the IIRUP to facilitate disposal of all unserviceable property of the Municipality.
b. Direct the Acting GSO to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) excluding IIRUP on condemned property which are already approved by the Head of Office upon recommendation of the Property Inspector and/or the Appraisal Committee and cancel the corresponding AREs.
c. Direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to drop the net book value with approved IIRUP from the books of accounts only after disposal was made.

We recommended and Management agreed the following:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor and liquidate cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was granted have been served and
Secure prior to preparation of DVs for subsequent cash advances certification from the Accountant that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office continue to:
Issue demand letters compelling the AO or employees to immediately settle in full their outstanding cash advances, either by liquidation, by payment/refund or through salary deductions; and
Strictly comply with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and all related rules and regulations pertaining to cash advances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and his staff continue to maintain the same efficiency and best efforts towards making quality and reliable financial reports.
We recommended that Management instruct the MENRO to ensure the functionality of the barangay MRFs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Coordinate with the Depository Banks for the prompt delivery of the monthly BS with the supporting documents, but in cases where the BS are not yet available, secure snapshot copy of the bank transactions to facilitate the timely reconciliation of its bank accounts; and/or
Enroll in online banking services of the depository banks.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Continue the preparation and submission of BRS within five days upon receipt of the BS; and
Immediately prepare correcting/adjusting entries for discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Coordinate with the Depository Banks as regards the supporting documents of the BS;
Effect adjustment in the books of accounts for the noted reconciling items to assure the correctness of the Cash in Bank account in the FS; and
Effect adjusting entries reverting the two stale checks to the Cash in Bank account of the LGU and restore the corresponding payable accounts thereof after considering the other needed procedures in the Manual.
Henceforth, prepare the BRS within reasonable time so that reconciling items and stale checks are monitored and appropriate actions are timely considered.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consistently monitor the release of checks issued by the Municipality and inform the payees of the existence of checks due to them before the said checks become stale.  Likewise, adjust the cashbook records in the GF for the stale checks identified.

We recommended that the:
Property Custodian keep a prescribed, complete and updated LRNPCs to serve as a means of checking the accuracy of the Local Road Network account maintained by the Municipal Accountant;
Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant conduct regular reconciliation of the balances of their records, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure that their records are consistent with each other; and
Municipal Engineer be required to furnish the Municipal Accountant and the Designated GSO with complete description and cost segregation of the various road components for completed road projects as basis for recording in their respective subsidiary records.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Unit to exert more effort in tracing the transactions, determine those deemed uncollectible and make the necessary request for write-off to COA, following the guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure the availability of the MOA in order to have a basis in the utilization and reporting of necessary transactions relative to fund transfers and in order for the Audit Team to verify whether the specific purpose for which the funds received are met.

We recommended that the Management:
a.	Require concerned implementing offices to reconcile their records with the records of SA as to the unliquidated, unrefunded and/or unremitted balances as to date;
b.	Exert extra effort to secure the availability of significant documents relative thereto; and
c.	Ensure prompt liquidation, remittance and/or refund of balances to the source or intended agency after the completion of each project, program or activity.

We recommended that Management require thru the offices-in-charge to:
Revisit operations to determine the bottleneck and the reasons why the said grants were not yet implemented and utilized as to date;
Identify measures that would address the reasons and act accordingly; and
Ensure timely and efficient utilization of funds, more importantly those coming from the NGAs as required in the MOA, existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
Coordinate with the PGS as regards the terms and conditions of the unutilized grants;
Ensure that all grants or fund transfers, be it from NGAs, GOCCs or LGUs shall have MOA in order to give basis in the utilization and reporting of necessary transactions relative to fund transfers and in order for the Audit Team to verify the terms and conditions and the timeline for the implementation of the fund transfers, the treatment of any unused balance upon completion of the purpose and the handling of unutilized/unexpended grants/fund transfers due to non-implementation of PPAs; and
Designate a focal person who would be coordinating and responsible in monitoring the status of project and/or programs implementation of funds transferred from SAs, be it from NGA, GOCC or LGU.

We recommended that the LCE inform the ATL within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Audit Team at the beginning of the year, a list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs during the year with detailed information prescribed under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to:
Strictly comply to regularly prepare and submit an approved PMR in accordance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and in the format prescribed in GPPB Circular No. 03-2017; and
Prepare the APP based on the prescribed format as provided in GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and ensure that all PPMPs are properly consolidated and procurement are entirely incorporated to reflect the transparency of procurement activities within the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and all other implementing offices of the Municipality maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent DF re-alignments be used appropriately for its intended projects/programs/activities and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the applicable guidelines and/or regulations.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management direct the MDRRM Council to use the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to incorporate the PPAs to be funded as well as current appropriations, including realignments pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to boost the attainment of a disaster resilient community.
Also, we recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to submit to the Auditor the LDRRMFIP and the supplemental investment program, if any, within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management:
a.	Optimally use or utilize the LDRRMF through the full implementation of the   projects and activities allowed in order to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks; and
b.	 Ensure that realignments are properly approved by the local sanggunian and   subsequently furnish the Auditor a copy of the approved resolution with its corresponding documents, as necessary.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review of the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payment on claims; and
Employ alternative ways such as designation of alternate person-in-charge in case of work arrangements to avoid deficiencies and strictly adhere to the existing rules and regulations as to documentary requirements.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and DEDPs and other information from DepEd Regional Office, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Moreover, we recommended that the SEF Budget be specifically presented by PPAs, by allotment class and per school.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
Municipal Treasurer to provide the LSB with correct income estimate of SET in the preparation of annual SEF Budget; and
LSB adhere to the format presented in Form 1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC. No. 1, s. 2017, in the proper computation and presentation of the SEF Budget.

We recommended that Management ensure that the Bayanihan Grant allocations be used appropriately for its intended PPAs and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of LBC No. 125.
We recommended that the GFPS continue updating and maintaining the GAD Database.
We recommended that Management require the job order employees to apply for Pag-IBIG MID.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant request for snap shots from the bank every end of the month. These documents could be used in lieu of the bank statements to facilitate timely preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements to support Management’s assertion on the existence and accuracy of the reported valuation of Cash in Bank – LCCA in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management adhere to the policies and procedures prescribed for effective control and accounting of government properties and:
(a)	The Municipal Accountant record as expense only consumed supplies and materials, maintain an inventory account using perpetual inventory system, prepare supplies ledger card per inventory item and prepare the necessary adjusting entry below to record the unconsumed inventories at the end of the year:
(b)	The GSO maintain supplies and materials stock cards per stock number and prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes; and
(c)	The Inventory Committee conduct the annual physical count at least annually to ensure proper accounting of the inventories on hand and help the Municipal Accountant establish the ending inventory to be reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management strengthen the installation of an effective inventory management system to make sound analysis on the receipt, distribution and/ or utilization of in-kind donations by adhering COA Circular No. 2014-002 as amended by COA Circular No. 2020-009. Require the concerned offices for the receipt and distribution to submit immediately a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and the Accounting Office and the GSO to record timely the in-kind donations in the books or in the registries.

We recommended that:
(a)	The Municipal Engineer prepares the Quarterly Report on GPPAs funded out from the 20% DF, in addition to the report he prepares for the Trust Fund, since most projects and programs are infrastructure related. If to be delegated to the Municipal Budget Officer, the latter can coordinate with the former to ensure that details, figures and amounts reflected on the report is true and correct;
(b)	The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the cost incurred to date so that the amount reflected on the quarterly report can be relied upon. Likewise, ensure that the amount actually disbursed is duly reflected on the SCBAA; and
(c)	Further, ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 for the timely submission of quarterly and consolidated reports.
We recommended that the BAC regularly prepare the PMR in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and in the format prescribed by the GPPB.

We recommended that:
(a)	Management optimally utilize the 20% DF in projects and programs that could contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality in adherence to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 1, dated March 27, 2020;
(b)	The Municipal Accountant send demand letters to barangays with unliquidated balances so that funds appropriated under 20% DF as financial assistance for allowable projects and programs will be released and implemented by respective BLGU in a timely manner. Likewise, require the BLGU to liquidate as soon as the programs or projects are completed; and,
(c)	The Municipal Engineer implement the CY 2020 projects and programs immediately as respective funds were not re-aligned for Covid-19 response.

We recommended that management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU, after which, the assessed value should be recorded by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has recorded land with the amount ₱35,097,290.00 and already on the process of acquiring TCT to establish ownership of the said lots.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all the necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF in the financial statements. Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure requirements on LDRRMF as provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.managementAction: Necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF were disclosed in CY 2020 Notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
• That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall a disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting documents; and
• That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately submitted to COA for evaluation and review.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
• Ensure the recoupment of the cited outstanding advance payment; and
• Henceforth, abide with the rules and regulations on the procurement of infrastructure projects and ensure that the surety bonds submitted by contractors/suppliers are properly reviewed before any payment is made.
observations.managementAction: Cited Advances to Contractors were already recouped.

observations.managementAction: Management submitted copies of the cited Retention Money Bonds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
• Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2019 to COA for audit purposes, if any; and
• Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA for audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted CY 2019 APP but incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the required documents mentioned above. Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted some of the required documents such as the minutes of pre-procurement meetings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management maintain a separate depository account for the share from tobacco excise tax to facilitate proper monitoring of the implementation and utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management created and maintained separate depository account for the share from tobacco excise tax through SB Resolution No. 24 Series of 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
• Henceforth, prepare SWMP and all relevant costs for the implementation of the Solid Waste Management programs are integrated to the Annual Budget as well as to the Annual Investment Plan. Management Coordination among the different offices is hereby advised; and
• Revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/monitoring mechanism to facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs indicated in the SWMP.
observations.managementAction: Management furnished the COA a copy of the Ecological Solid Waste Plant for CYs 2017-2027.

observations.recommendation: Those schedules to support the beginning balance of Due to LGUs account be prepared and submitted. Prepare the schedule of necessary disbursement voucher and effect payment/return the unused portion of funds received from the Provincial Government of Abra amounting to ₱139,637.17.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the schedule of receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate submit the basis in setting up the 2016 RPT/SET Receivable as well as the schedule to support the RPT/SET Receivable and the corresponding Deferred Credits account balances as of December 31, 2016. The Accountant is likewise advised to review and analyze the nature of the discrepancy of the above-mentioned accounts and effect necessary adjustments in order to present fairly the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive – 

instruct the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the updated Real Property Assessment Roll generated by the PATAS in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I; 

require the Municipal Treasurer to continue to update the list of real property units and the related status of payments; and

require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in support of the RPT and SET Receivable balances.

We recommended that Management utilize the geo-hazard map in crafting more informative IEC materials that are disseminated to all levels of the community, thus increase the resiliency and decrease the vulnerability of men and women in the community which may result in their participation in making innovative solutions for preventive or minimal occurrence of calamity and emergencies

We recommended and Management agreed that the –

MPDO ensure that the fund is appropriated and utilized in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; 

Municipal Engineer properly schedule and monitor the implementation of the projects so that early achievement of its objective would be attained; and

Municipal Accountant ensure that all DVs are supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular 2012-001 before making any payment to provide reasonable basis for establishing the validity of claims.
We recommended that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the disallowances.

We recommend that management submit justification on the late submission of perfected contracts and henceforth require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval.
observations.managementAction: Contracts were submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit written justification/comment on the excess of ABC and total contract price versus the COA cost.

Further, adhere to the provisions of Section 6 of COA Resolution and DPWH Department Order No. 22 in the preparation of ABC.
observations.managementAction: Submitted written explanation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that LDRRM Officer make proper revision of the DRRM Plan in CY 2020 and in the succeeding years to ensure that the total appropriation for DRRM related activities is at least 5% of the estimated revenue from regular sources.

We further recommend that this practice be discontinued immediately.

Further, submit the liquidation reports including the complete set of approved plans and drawings and other necessary documents for evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit written justification on the deficiencies noted above.

Further, observe to the provisions of Section 23 of Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular 2012-001 in the processing and granting of special monetization. Also, complete details must be provided before processing or approving the special monetization.
observations.managementAction: To submit written justification for the deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management expedite the promotion of deserving workers among the present pool of manpower resources in the municipality and the hiring of personnel to fill-up these vacant positions.
observations.managementAction: Filled-up 5 out of 5 vacant positions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to charge and record the wages of the contractual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services.

We also recommend that management limit the hiring of casuals/job order employees for intermittent, emergency jobs or specific piece of work with short duration as guided by CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and maximize the services of the permanent employees for regular agency functions.
observations.managementAction: Management has started the charging and recording of casual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all collectors remit their collections intact daily to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Sections 15 and 29 of the New Government Accounting System Manual, in order to preclude the accumulation of unremitted collections by the collectors.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the use of the accountable forms strictly by the person to whom it was issued.

Transfer the collector of water fees under the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for the proper monitoring of his collections and prompt capture of the collection data.

The Municipal Treasurer strictly monitors the Accountable Forms. Also an AF officers designated is the one in-charge in approving of AF requested by the requesting officer and is recorded in the logbook. Collection of water fees is already in the MTO.
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:

To immediately prepare adjusting entries to show the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan accounts.

In the succeeding amortization payments, review amounts in the billing statements before effecting payments and see to it that there is proper recording of the charges made.

We recommend that management require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted within 10 days from receipt of Bank Statements. The failure to submit required reports may warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts under Due to GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these payable accounts.

Further, make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to reconcile the negative/abnormal balances of the account.

Cubrado which rendered 1 and half month duty after being re-elected. The GSIS System will not accept the remittance due to her status in the system as terminated. Her status in the system change last year and we are able to remit the full balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year. 

We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer furnished a soft copy of the list of taxpayers with the assessed value of the real properties. Also, there were adjustments made last October 2018.
observations.reason: In CY 2020, there is a difference of ₱91,026.91 between the SET and RPT. (Restated in Finding No. 4)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit proof of creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommend that the management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: Currently, there are 2 positions existing in the LDRRMO. These are LDRRMO I and II. This year 2021, the position of LDRRMO V is created and pending for approval.
observations.reason: 2 positions were created - LDRRMO I and LDRRMO II

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Also, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: 74% of the unliquidated cash advance is the confidential fund liquidation submitted to ICFAU for 2014 and below but no credit advice receives till today. The remaining advances are from LGU which are no longer connected to LGU.
observations.reason: Several employees were no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. Also, require the MT/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant, which shall be used as basis in recording issuance.

We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or GSO as the case may be, conduct physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies and the Municipal accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2020, First-in, First-out inventory method was used in costing of the supplies inventory. The Accounting Office also maintained the supplies inventory schedule.
observations.reason: Report for the physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies was not submitted

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, prepares stock/property cards and that the Municipal Accountant also maintain separate ledger cards to establish the details of the assets.

We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: Physical count was conducted this year 2020 and the Physical Count per department already submitted to COA. The reconciliation between count versus system is ongoing. The Management will also send the list of Unserviceable PPE.
observations.reason: Report for the physical count of the property, plant and equipment was not submitted. (Reiterated in Finding No. 8)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.

observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, partial settlements of ₱1,009,732.00 were made. (Reiterated in Finding No. 13)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the contractor to submit sufficient justification in the delay of the full implementation of the Bohol Earthquake Assistance (BEA) projects; otherwise, rescind the contracts, forfeit the contractors’ performance security and takeover the prosecution of the projects.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and deduct liquidated damages from the retention money pursuant to Item 8 Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: All BEA projects were already complete.
observations.reason: The projects are 100% as of December 31, 2019 per monitoring of government projects submitted. However, computations for the liquidated damages were not done. (Restated in Finding No. 5)

We also recommend that the municipal accountant monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.

We further recommend that succeeding expenditures in the Trust Fund be made in accordance with Sections 97 and 104 of the NGAS Volume 1, to avoid misstatements in the financial statements of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify the revenue collection efforts to meet the budgetary requirements to finance the municipality’s programs, projects, and activities.   Further, appropriate/budget within the reasonable or attainable estimates so as not to incur negative appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the provisions of the Revised Cash Examination Manual and deposit all his collections  intact to the authorized government depository bank not later than the next banking day or as soon as collections reach ₱10,000.00.
observations.managementAction: MTO deposit their collection intact in this current year audit CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Sangguniang Bayan impose and implement the required taxes, fees and permits as mandated in the Revised Revenue Code and whenever applicable, consider the revision of the said code in order to impose rates which are responsive to the present economic situation considering that the same was enacted way back in CY 2006.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality strictly observe the pertinent provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG-DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects and ensure that said fund is optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: The agency implemented most of their projects under 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections in her possession to the authorized government depository bank to safeguard government funds from risks of loss.
observations.managementAction: MTO deposit their collection intact in this current year audit CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GAD Accomplishment Report, duly endorsed by the DILG, to the Audit Team for proper evaluation of the GAD Plan and Budget execution.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted GAD report in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor sign appropriate issuances to institutionalize the establishment of the GFPS of the LGU to ensure gender-responsive governance.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Assistant to update and reconcile the individual personnel Leave Cards and deduct from the leave credit balances the corresponding tardiness and undertime incurred, otherwise the overpayment caused by the non-deduction will be disallowed in audit.  Further, monitor the tardiness and undertime of the employees and consider the provisions of the Revised Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service in the imposition of sanctions if warranted to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Management Assistant require the submission of the accomplishment report of the job order personnel to support the payment of their wages.  Further, require the refund from the concerned Job Order personnel the overpayment of wages equivalent to the tardiness and undertime incurred, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee in consultation with the end user ensure that technical specifications of the items to be procured be provided in the preparation of the bidding documents to facilitate competitive bidding and the requirement of the Procuring Entity are met.  Also, make sure that the supporting documents were complete and all the necessary data were available, otherwise the transaction will be disallowed in audit. 
We further recommended for the submission of a written explanation on the deficiencies noted in this audit memorandum.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor intensify the implementation of the projects funded by financial assistance in order to timely achieve the desired benefits.  Also make representation with the respective donors of the financial assistance with unutilized balances and seek authority to use the fund balance to finance other projects/programs of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Implemented some projects out of the financial assistance received from different sources.
observations.reason: Balance of Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts amounted to ₱41,393,233.40 and ₱6,719,780.85, respectively, as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the heads of the Municipal Accounting Office, Property and Supply Office and the Inventory Team to continue their commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all its properties and its reconciliation with the books and records of the Accounting and Property Office, to ensure a reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management will ensure to continue  their commitment and efforts to ensure a reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office continue to:

Issue demand letters compelling the accountable officers or employees to immediately settle in full their outstanding cash advances, either by liquidation, by payment/refund or through salary deductions; and


Strictly comply with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and all related rules and regulations pertaining to cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Issued demand letters to compel concerned employees to immediately settle in full all Cash Advances.

Complied with the requirements, rules and regulations concerning cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MA and his staff continue to maintain the same efficiency and best efforts toward making quality and reliable financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Management will ensure to continue and maintain the efficiency and best efforts towards making quality and reliable Financial Reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure proper safekeeping of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration.

We also recommended that Management direct the:

Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in COA Circular No. 92-386 which are either through (a) public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by destruction; and

MA to make necessary entries to derepcognize the unserviceable properties from the books of the LGU after the duly conducted disposal procedure.
observations.managementAction:
Ensured proper safekeeping of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration.

Facilitated the concerned employees for immediate disposal of unserviceable properties.


Made necessary entries for derecognition of unserviceable properties from the books of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

The Local Finance Committee to:

Conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;



Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating departments towards attaining the targets set; and



Provide for the targets on accounts that did not have projections which should be based on statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectations.

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and other departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to perform efficiently in the revenue administration by considering, among others, the following:

Conduct a periodic review to ascertain whether collection targets are attained and in case of shortfall in collections, to analyze the reasons and to look into alternative actions to avoid incurrence of lapses in projection in the future; and



Closely monitor the personnel involved in the verification of documents, assessment and collection of taxes as to whether they are performing their mandated functions and see to it that taxes are correctly imposed, assessed, collected and remitted to the government. Appropriate action be initiated against personnel not complying with the requirements of laws, rules, regulations, and existing policies of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Local Finance Committee shall be more prudent in projecting 
LGUs revenue.


The MTO and other departments are now involved in generating revenues endeavor. To perform efficiently in the revenue administration.

Management is now using prior years’ experience in providing targets on accounts



Municipal Treasurer with the assistance of the Local Finance Committee is coming up with strategies to improve revenue generation.

Municipal Treasurer strictly monitored those personnel if they performing their responsibilities and correctly imposed, assessed, collected and remitted the taxes to the government.
observations.reason:
Still in the process of availing remedies in enforcing payments particularly in tax delinquency and due to pandemic







Still in the process of identifying strategies to improve revenue generation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Initiate measures to collect the delinquencies from market rentals amounting to almost ₱2.0 million and employ strategies to intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and

Ensure that the market stall holders have a valid and subsisting “Contract of Lease”.



We also recommended that:

The Municipal Market Supervisor employ strategies to maximize the lease of vacant stalls and intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and


The Local Sangguniang Bayan enact a new or revised Market Code to adjust the rates to current levels.
observations.managementAction:
Measures are being undertaken to collect delinquencies from market rentals.


Check contracts of stall holders, expired contracts will be renewed by concerned holders who are not delinquent.



Due to pandemic, revised Market Code was not fully implemented.
observations.reason:
Payment of delinquencies of stall holder are in installment basis.




Contract of Lease of some stall holders are not yet reviewed because of their inability to pay at least ¼ of their arrears.





Due to pandemic, revised Market Code was not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Demand from the identified suppliers the settlement of tax deficiency of ₱63,870.55; and



Perform examination of the books of accounts of business establishments and/or exhaust possible means in obtaining the correct gross receipts/sales for the preceding calendar year thru the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) or the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

Further, we recommended that the BPLO and the MTO:

Require the taxpayer to present and submit the Annual Income Tax Return (ITR) for the preceding calendar year, or if not yet available, the ITR for the next preceding calendar year, together with the other financial documents such as Audited FS and proof of payment, to be retained by both offices in the taxpayer’s permanent file for current and future reference; and



Require the submission of the BIR Certificate of Registration or BIR Form 2303 for all businesses applying or renewing business permits to validate the correctness of the line of business indicated in their application forms and to avoid misapplication of tax rates.
observations.managementAction:
Requested the concerned suppliers for settlement of tax deficiency.

Management is now stricter in examining Books of Accounts of Businesses and require them to submit Financial Statement.


Management is now requiring taxpayers to responsibly present and submit the said necessary documents to be retained by the offices in the taxpayer’s permanent file for current and future reference.



Management is now requiring the stated documents as part of the requirements in Business Permit application form.

Included in the requirements in renewing Business Permits, the Business proof of income to cause accuracy of sales being declared.

In the revision of the Municipal LRC, Management is now including the presumptive income levels to be used in the PILAA.

Also, they will only accept and process properly filled out application form.

Management have revised the Business Permit application form to include the requirements in Section 6.1.1 of DILG-DICT-DTI JMC No. 01.

Instructed the Municipal Treasurer to mandate all AOs on remitting full amount that they collected daily. 


Instructed the Municipal Treasurer to always check the accountable forms and cash being remitted before new accountable form is issued.

Ensured that no OR is missing and that all collections are remitted properly.

Instructed the Municipal Treasurer to determine the unremitted collections of collectors, to direct them to settle and to take legal action, If needed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Ensure that the procedures in the post closure land use plan be strictly carried out. These include inspection, maintenance and monitoring after the implementation of closure activities which shall maintain the integrity and compliance of the closed dumpsite; and

Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to expedite the preparation of Initial Environmental Examination (IEE) so that upon issuance of Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC), the construction of the Sanitary Landfill could already be initiated and the cost to provide services for the disposal of solid waste could be reduced or eliminated.
observations.managementAction:
Management will ensure that the procedures in the post closure land use plan must be strictly performed.

The OME shall prepare vital documents for ECC applications prior to procurement of future projects.
observations.reason: Phase I or embankment activities was started on Oct. 1, 2020, and was temporarily suspended on January 13, 2021 due to unworkable site condition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to have an in-depth planning of PPAs to be included in the 20 per cent DF Annual Investment Program (AIP), presenting in details specific items of PPAs embodied therein such as specific project name, location, feasible schedule of project implementation and expected output for timely implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the MDC on having an in-depth planning of PPAs which is included in the 20 per cent DF AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDC and all other implementing offices of the Municipality maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented first quarter of 2021
observations.reason: Due to pandemic some of the Infrastructure projects were just bid this December.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent DF be used solely for development programs and projects and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.
observations.managementAction: Management will make sure that the 20 per cent DF should be used solely for development programs and projects.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that Management strictly require the Municipal DRRM Office (MDRRMO) to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the MA and submit the same immediately through the LDRRM Council and Local Development Council to the concerned offices on the required dates, pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

14a. Non-submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund for CY 2019

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to submit to the Auditor the LDRRMFIP within the prescribed period.

14b. Non-inclusion of PPAs to be charged to the Unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).

We also recommended that the MDRRMO include in the LDRRMFIP the list of PPAs chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
Required the MDRRMO to strictly comply the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.




Instructed the MDRRMO to submit to the Auditor the LDRRMFIP within the prescribed period.




Ensured that MDRRMO must include the list of PPAs chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd Public School District Supervisor (PSDS) and School Division Superintendent (SDS) as well as the required School Improvement Program (SIPs) and Division Education Development Plan  (DEDPs) and other information from DepEd Regional Office, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB shall strictly comply and follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.
observations.managementAction: Required the LSB to prepare three-year plan taking into consideration the recommendation of DepEd, PSDS and SDS including the drafting of SIP and DEDP.
observations.reason: Inability of DepEd (PSDS) to prepare the three-year plan, SIPS and DEDP

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging to SEF the financial transaction or expenditures which are not among the primary purpose of the establishment of SEF and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses chargeable against SEF as provided under Section 272 of the Local Government Code (RA No. 7160) and Item No. 4.0 of Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG.  Moreover, please justify why the non-priority expenses may not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Already enforced strict observance at Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and item No. 4.0 of Joint Circular No. 01 Section 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG on the use of SEF. Already excluded in the 2020 budget all those pertaining to ALS, and all Catering Services and Equipment and Supplies for administrative use.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Treasurer, aggressively exert extra effort in the collection of additional levy on real property for the SEF to generate funds for the implementation of priority programs and projects for the benefit of public school children.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with plans on how to improve the RPT Collections.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

Designate the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) - Technical Working Group (TWG) Chair from among the GFPS-TWG members that will be identified duly endorsed by their supervisors who will catalyze, coordinate, provide direction and monitor the programs and projects on gender and development concerns within the agency and its constituents; and

Issue an Executive Order (EO) or Administrative Order (AO) creating a GAD M&E Team as required under Section 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to provide Management with the necessary data and information essential for policy making.
observations.managementAction:
Assigned GFPS-TWG Chair to manage programs and projects addressing the gender and development concerns.


Issued Executive Order No. 21 series of 2021 dated March 8, 2021 creating the Evaluation and Monitoring Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

Send member/s of the GFPS to trainings on how to use monitoring tools such as HGDG and Project Implementation and Management, and Monitoring and Evaluation (PIMME); and

Direct the GFPS to administer the HGDG tests in order to aid them in the attribution of budgets to the GAD budget and in the determination of PPAs that specifically address 
the gender gaps/issues and concerns and work towards the attainment of their targeted outputs.
observations.managementAction:
The GFPS-TWG and the M&E members attend workshop on the use of monitoring tools. 

There is a budget allocation for CAPDEV of the members

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MA trace and analyze the pertinent transactions, and reconcile the difference of ₱1.63 million in the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the said accounts.
observations.managementAction: This consists of project equity for infra projects which, to date, are not yet completed. MA will adjust/close to Trust Fund once the projects are completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the MA to strictly observe the correct timeline in the release of the barangay share from RPT collection to each barangay pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent DF is used solely for development programs and projects and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

observations.managementAction: Required the MDRRMO to strictly comply with the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd Public School District Supervisor (PSDS) and Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) as well as the required School Improvement Plan (SIPs) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDPs) and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB shall strictly comply and follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.

Not fully implemented due to Covid-19 pandemic

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Market Supervisor employ strategies to maximize the lease of vacant stalls and intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and


Management prioritize the utilization of the vacant market stalls and satellite market by inviting interested parties to occupy/rent the stalls at an affordable price in order to achieve the purpose for which the building was constructed. Further, Management is advised to conduct a thorough feasibility study before embarking on a project to avoid waste of government funds.
observations.managementAction:
Vacant stalls are being rented out and efforts to collect delinquent rental is intensified.


.
observations.reason: The economic effectiveness of the strategies employed still need to be evaluated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Accountant agreed to request for snap shots from the bank every end of the month. observations.managementAction: On-going preparation of BRS.
observations.reason: Preparation of BRS are on-going as books are yet to be closed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to: 
a. Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the adjusting journal entries as follows:

Dr.: Prior Year Adjustment ------------------ ₱10,365,689.04
Cr:  Leave Benefits payable ------------------ ₱10,365,689.04

b. From then on, require the HRMO in charge to submit to the Accounting Unit the periodic report on accumulated leave balances to facilitate the recognition of the cost of the compensated absences incurred for the current year. 

c. Gradually set up the necessary funds for terminal leave benefits of employees by accruing the monthly actual earned leave benefits of its personnel.

d. Henceforth, instruct the Accounting Unit to periodically record in the books of accounts the compensated absences earned and ensure that funds is set up for the timely and expeditious release of the retirement pay in accordance with Paragraph 11 of the Philippine Accounting Standards (PAS) 19 and Section 1 of Republic Act No. 101541, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Funds from savings of Personal Services, were determined to be the fund for terminal leave benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee that will spearhead in the conduct of annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover, direct the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards, respectively and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements.
observations.managementAction: GSO-Designate started tagging of newly acquired properties.
observations.reason: Submitted RPCPPE and RPCI contains unreconciled items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to review and meticulously screen any loan application of its employees, especially those offered by financial institutions aside from the GSIS and PAG-IBIG to ensure that their net take home pay does not fall below the required minimum take home pay provided by law.
observations.managementAction: Employees with net take home pay of < ₱5,000 significantly decreased compared to CY 2020 as there were 4-5 employees whose net take home pays are below the threshold.
observations.reason: Due to lack of control in loan approval process.

observations.recommendation:
