We recommended and the Engineering Office agreed to ensure that project duration and all other required data pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 are provided or printed on the signboards of all implemented infrastructure projects in order to promote good governance through transparency and accountability and to secure the right of the people to information of matters of public concern.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to thoroughly examine and evaluate the activities to be included in its LDRRM plan to ensure its attainability and full implementations towards an effective and efficient performance of the LGU in responding to any natural and man-made calamities.

It is further recommended that efficient and effective utilization of the fund should be anchored on the promotion of the general welfare.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to appoint or designate a GSO who shall specifically take custody and be accountable for all the properties, real or personal, of the LGU and to prepare and maintain stock cards and property cards for all its inventories and PPEs; create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4.8, 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply and adhere to the terms and conditions embodied in an agreement covering a project to ensure its total implementation.  This assures that there will be no losses of government resources and the people will benefit therefrom; conduct competitive bidding to all procurement of the Agency as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; and submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the above discussion as well as the justification on the occurrence of the irregularities.  Non-compliance thereof may cause suspension or disallowance of the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to immediately file before the Office of the Auditor a request for relief from property accountability for the death of 27 cattle with the available supporting evidence as enumerated in COA Memorandum Circular No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992. The matter shall be resolved in accordance with the provisions on request for relief stated in the 2009 COA Revised Rules of Procedures. This shall also be the basis of dropping from the books the cost or value of the animals; and adhere strictly to Section 73(1) of P.D. No. 1445 in case losses of government money and/or property occur.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to formulate and include only GAD PPAs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB and GAR in a manner and in prescribed forms to comply Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC; submit to the Auditor justification/explanation why the management incurred expenses that are not in accordance with the objectives of the GAD fund, as well as documents to prove that the PPAs indeed addressed emerging gender issues and concerns.  Failure to comply may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.
observations.managementAction: PPAs included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive.
observations.reason: Some of the PPAs included in the GAD Plan are still not supportive of the government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women.

observations.reason: Not complied by Management. Reiterated in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality strictly observe the pertinent provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG-DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects and ensure that said fund is optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: The 20% DF was not completely utilized.
observations.reason: Based on the Statement of Comparison Between Budget and Actual Amounts, 20% DF has total appropriation of ₱11,381,574.00 but only ₱6,250,520.62 was utilized during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management return to the National Treasury the unutilized / unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend that management should review and evaluate the list of KKK Fund loan grantees in the attached Annex A if it is still valid and collection is still possible. If found to be futile, request for the write off of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management is not able to comply with the requirements for the Request for Write-off of Accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections in her possession to the authorized government depository bank to safeguard government funds from risks of loss.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2019, Cash Local Treasury has a balance of ₱580,348.35. No proof of deposit was presented in the following month.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should require the Municipal Livestock Coordinator, who is in – charge of the dispersal program of the municipality, to monitor the project, prepare and submit a report regularly to the Accounting Division any increase in the livestock inventory due to returns from recipients as well as the mortality duly supported with an affidavit and witnessed by any of the barangay officials to ensure the reliability, accuracy and validity of  the recorded amount in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Reports were not prepared and the livestock were not monitored regularly.
observations.reason: Not complied by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the adjusting journal entries as follows:
Dr. Retained Earnings (Deficit) P8,614,446.13 Cr. Leave Benefits Payable P8,614,446.13
b. From then on, require the HRMO in charge to submit to the Accounting Unit the periodic report on accumulated leave balances to facilitate the recognition of the cost of the compensated absences incurred for the current year.
c. Set up slowly the necessary funds for terminal leave benefits of employees by accruing the monthly actual earned leave benefits of its personnel.
d. Henceforth, instruct the Accounting Unit to periodically record in the books of accounts the compensated absences earned and ensure that funds is set up for the timely and expeditious release of the retirement pay in accordance with Paragraph 11 of the Philippine Accountings Standards (PAS) 19 and Section 1 of Republic Act No. 101541 respectively.
observations.managementAction: Gradually set-up funds for Terminal Leave Benefits
observations.reason: No enough funds for Terminal Leave Benefits

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to meticulously screen any loan application of its employees, especially those offered by financial institutions aside from the GSIS and PAG-IBIG to ensure that their net take home pay does not go below the required minimum take home pay provided by law.
observations.reason: Various employees still received Net Take Home Pay below P5,000

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to thoroughly examine and evaluate the activities to be included in its LDRRMF plan to ensure its attainability and full implementations towards an effective and efficient performance of the LGU in responding to any natural and man-made calamities.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accountant to revert the accounting entry made by debiting Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and crediting Due to NGAs account for the subject amount. Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1 in dropping from the books the accounts subject for request for relief.
observations.managementAction: No management action undertaken.
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) Submit to the Auditor the above-mentioned documentary requirements. Failure to do so may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and (2) Strictly adhere to the DILG MC No. 97-273 in the conduct of lakbay-aral or study tours.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the accountable officers to submit lacking documents.
observations.reason: Documentary requirements are not all submitted to COA office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to thoroughly examine and evaluate its plans, programs and activities to identify the weaknesses resulting to non-implementation and/or minimal implementation of its programs and activities most importantly the disaster prevention and mitigation and disaster preparedness programs resulting to failure to attain its intended purpose and deliver efficient and effective services to its constituents.
observations.reason: Several programs, projects and activities in the LDRRMF were not implemented. Reiterated in the Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to inform the SB to execute a resolution covering the payments of wages for JOs; remind the HRMO that service contracts provided to the parties shall always be acknowledged by the concerned recipients; instruct the concerned department heads to carefully review and sign the DTRs of the subordinates, specially of the JOs, before corresponding computation of wages shall be made thereto; advise the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the JOs’ performance specially on the collections of revenues. Require the latter to remit all its collections to the former on a daily basis; and adhere faithfully to the prevailing laws, rules and regulations in the payment of wages to JOs.
observations.reason: Recommendations were still not implemented by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the attached schedule. All claims shall always be supported with complete documentation; faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions of any government agency. Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the above-noted irregularities. Failure to comply may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and ensure that all financial transactions shall be recorded in accordance with the prevailing accounting standards; instruct the Accountant to make adjustments in the books which pertain to the erroneous entries made.
observations.managementAction: No management action recommended by the auditor.
observations.reason: Management failed to submit the required documents as recommended by the auditor.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that management exhaust all possible means to
document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer
Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU, after which, the
assessed value should be recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

2.6 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer and
the Municipal Accountant to locate records of road networks in their
offices where the necessary data, specifications and locations of the
Road Networks may be sourced to facilitate the preparation and
submission of the aforementioned reports and the proper recognition of
Road Networks and their corresponding depreciation in accounting
books.

2.7 We also recommended that Management ensure preparation and
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and its components and
disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2015-008.

3.7 We recommended that management see to it that the unexpended /
unobligated LDRRMF are treated as Trust Liability at the end of the
year for easy monitoring and proper classification and presentation of
account balances.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all the
necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF in the financial
statements. Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure
requirements on LDRRMF as provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.

5.7 We recommended that FSs, DVs and ORs should be submitted within
prescribed period to enable timely audit of account balances and
transactions.

6.11.1 	That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement
vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation.
In no case shall a disbursement be paid without the necessary
supporting documents; and

6.11.2 	That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately
submitted to COA for evaluation and review.

7.7 We recommended that Management:
						Justify the noted lapses:
						Ensure the recoupment of the cited outstanding advance
payment; and
						Henceforth, abide with the rules and regulations on the
procurement of infrastructure projects and ensure that the
surety bonds submitted by contractors/suppliers are properly
reviewed before any payment is made.

8.11 In view of the above noted observations, we recommended that
management submit justification as to why reimbursements method
was resorted even in the absence of contingencies, for evaluation.
Henceforth, refrain from using the reimbursement method of payment
unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate
payment thereof.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking
documents and ensure that in future transactions all required
documents are attached and reviewed before processing the
disbursement vouchers and making payment thereof. Ensure that rules
and regulations with regard to the procurement of medicines are
observed at all times.

10.4 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the
Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents and ensure that
all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete
documentation. In no case shall a disbursement be paid without the
necessary supporting documents.

11.6 We recommended that Management:
						Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2019 to COA for
audit purposes, if any; and
						Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a
basis guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section
7.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. A copy thereof be furnished
to COA for audit purposes.

12.5 We recommended that management furnish the Auditor a copy of the
purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working
days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts
together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical
review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

13.8 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Bids
and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the
required documents mentioned above. Henceforth, comply with the
rules and regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

14.5 We recommended that management maintain a separate depository
account for the share from tobacco excise tax to facilitate proper
monitoring of the implementation and utilization of the fund.

15.3 In view of the cited observations, we recommended that management:
						Submit valid justification on the noted lapses; and
						Utilize the unexpended balance of allocated funds for the
intended purposes after due evaluation whether these are still
priority concerns taking into consideration current
developments and to accordingly re-appropriate the funds of
PPA which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current
priority undertakings.

16.5 We recommended that management justify the above-noted lapses.
Utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited on
programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment
of socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital
expenditures that will yield greater benefit to the people in accordance
with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

17.5 In view of the above noted lapses, we recommended that Management:
						Henceforth, prepare SWMP and all relevant costs for the
implementation of the Solid Waste Management programs are
integrated to the Annual Budget as well as to the Annual
Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is
hereby advised; and
						Revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth,
implement quality assurance/ monitoring mechanism to facilitate
an efficient and effective implementation of the programs
indicated in the SWMP.

19.6 We recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The
Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the
preparation, adoption and updating of both plans for CY 2020
onwards.

20.5 We recommended that Management immediately submit the required
supporting documents for further verification of the submitted GAD
Accomplishment Report.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive –
a) instruct the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the updated Real Property Assessment Roll generated by the PATAS in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I;
b) require the Municipal Treasurer to continue to update the list of real property units and the related status of payments; and
c) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in support of the RPT and SET Receivable balances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief 
Executive –
require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to fully comply with its mandated functions as enumerated in R.A. No. 9003 and its IRR; and
through the MSWMB –
revisit and modify priorities, if applicable, as the plan is being implemented; and
pursue its plan, as contained in its MSWMP, to establish a long-term institutionalized process aimed at having law abiding citizens through the implementation of laws, rules and regulation and develop an enormous number of people who have the love and care for the environment.

We recommended that Management utilize the geo-hazard map in crafting more informative IEC materials that are disseminated to all levels of the community, thus increase the resiliency and decrease the vulnerability of men and women in the community which may result in their participation in making innovative solutions for preventive or minimal occurrence of calamity and emergencies.

We recommended and Management agreed that the –
MPDO ensure that the fund is appropriated and utilized in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017;
Municipal Engineer properly schedule and monitor the implementation of the projects so that early achievement of its objective would be attained; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that all DVs are supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular 2012-001 before making any payment to provide reasonable basis for establishing the validity of claims.
We recommended that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the disallowances.

We commended the Management and recommended that it continue its strict adherence with existing revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR, thus avoiding penalties and surcharges which may be imposed for late payment thereof.

We recommend that the OIC - Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer, the
Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere with COA Circular No.
2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. Likewise, the OIC-Municipal Accountant exert
effort in recording the remaining 10% Local Roads Networks of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality shall procure supplies and materials not exceeding
two-month requirement andstrictly adhere with Section 26 of General Provisions of the
General Appropriations Act, FY 2019.

We recommended the following:

a. Management make sure that the building/structure be utilized solely as Municipal
Crisis Center;
b. MSWDO facilitate the physical and documentary requirements needed to qualify for
the accreditation with the DSWD, RO 6; and
c. Management appropriate additional funding, if needed, to improve the current
facilities.

We recommended the following:

a. Sanggunian Bayan formulate ordinances to maximize imposition of taxes, fees and other
charges, and/or include to offer for tax amnesty thru an ordinance to generate more
revenues;
b. Municipal Treasurer issue Notice of Delinquencies and Demand Letters as collection
method to delinquent taxpayers; and
c. Enforce collection remedies allowed by the provisions cited in RA No. 7160, particularly on
the sale of delinquent properties through public auction.

We recommended that agency immediately facilitate the implementation of the project,
and submit Fund Utilization Report to the source agency, as provided in the MOA.
Henceforth, implementation of the LGU’s programs from NGAs/other sources should
be properly monitored and be given utmost priority.

We recommended the following:

a. Management advise the Agency Authorized Officer to be more diligent in approving
the Loan Applications of employees, and ensure that loan amortizations be deducted
monthly without sacrificing the P5,000.00 minimum net take home pay, as required by
the law; and
b. OIC - Municipal Accountant to monitor the strict implementation of related provisions
and restrictions of the law.

We recommended that OIC-Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere with the provisions
cited in the above - mentioned Memorandum Circular.

It is also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant brief the Local School Board the
necessity of the withholding taxes due to BIR.

We recommend that management request the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

Further, prepare adjusting entry to revert stale checks as follows:

We recommend that the Property Officer initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for new purchases.
We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts under Due to GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these payable accounts.

Further, make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to reconcile the negative/abnormal balances of the account.

We recommend that management submit written justification/comment on the excess of ABC and total contract price versus the COA cost.

Further, adhere to the provisions of Section 6 of COA Resolution and DPWH Department Order No. 22 in the preparation of ABC.

We recommend that management submit written justification for the delay in the submission of full liquidation of the fund transfers.

Further, submit the liquidation reports including the complete set of approved plans and drawings and other necessary documents for evaluation.

We recommend that management submit justification on the late submission of perfected contracts and henceforth require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval.

We recommend that LDRRM Officer make proper revision of the DRRM Plan in CY 2020 and in the succeeding years to ensure that the total appropriation for DRRM related activities is at least 5% of the estimated revenue from regular sources.

We recommend that management submit proof of creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommend that the management initiate the creation of the three required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the deficiencies noted above.

Further, observe to the provisions of Section 23 of Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular 2012-001 in the processing and granting of special monetization. Also, complete details must be provided before processing or approving the special monetization.

We recommend that the concerned official and personnel submit written explanation on why the checks were signed even in the absence of a duly processed/approved disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.
We further recommend that this practice be discontinued immediately.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned Municipal Officials refrain from incurring additional PS expenses / cost until such time that the limitation is observed.  The implementation of any increases in PS cost shall be held in abeyance until the Municipality shall not exceed its PS limitation.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the day care centers and conduct inventory of the textbooks and instructional materials and submit the inventory report to the Accounting Office to support the amount of inventory recorded in the books.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to comply the documentary requirements for the write off of the Loans Receivable account for verification and evaluation.

We recommended that Management establish an Internal Audit Service Office with sufficient support from the top management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GAD Accomplishment Report, duly endorsed by the DILG, to the Audit Team for proper evaluation of the GAD Plan and Budget execution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials or offices responsible to submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

We recommended that management return to the National Treasury the unutilized / unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the Infrastructure Assets in the current and prior years to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Disposal Committee to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties to the directives of Sections 379, 380 and 381 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a physical count of the supplies, prepare and submit the RCPI semestrally to the audit team and reconcile the same with the records of the Accounting Office. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should prepare and maintain stock cards/subsidiary ledgers for each type of inventory which should be reconciled always.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor sign appropriate issuances to institutionalize the establishment of the GFPS of the LGU to ensure gender-responsive governance.

We recommended and the Accountant agreed to request for snap shots
from the bank every end of the month. These documents could be used
in lieu of the bank statements to facilitate timely preparation and
submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements to support
Management’s assertion on the existence and accuracy of the reported
valuation of Cash in Bank – LCCA in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

2.8.1 Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the adjusting journal
entries as follows:
Dr: Prior Year Adjustment P10,365,689.04
Cr: Leave Benefits payable P10,365,689.04

2.8.2 From then on, require the HRMO in charge to submit to the
Accounting Unit the periodic report on accumulated leave
balances to facilitate the recognition of the cost of the
compensated absences incurred for the current year.

2.8.3 Gradually set up the necessary funds for terminal leave benefits of
employees by accruing the monthly actual earned leave benefits of
its personnel.

2.8.4 Henceforth, instruct the Accounting Unit to periodically record in
the books of accounts the compensated absences earned and
ensure that funds are set up for the timely and expeditious release
of the retirement pay in accordance with Paragraph 11 of the
Philippine Accounting Standards (PAS) 19 and Section 1 of
Republic Act No. 101541, respectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed to create an Inventory
Committee that will spearhead in the conduct of annual physical count
of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report
thereon to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year.
Moreover, direct the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office to
prepare and maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards,
respectively and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done
before preparation of the annual financial statements.

We have recommended that management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU, after which, the assessed value should be recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that The Municipal Management direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to locate records of road networks in their offices where the necessary data, specifications and locations of the Road Networks may be sourced to facilitate the recognition of road networks and the corresponding depreciation in the accounting books, the preparation of subsidiary ledgers and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We have also recommended that Management ensure compliance with the conduct of inventory and the recognition of Road Networks in the books of accounts as well as the disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management see to it that the unexpended / unobligated funds from prior and current year LDRRM are treated as Trust Liability at the end of the year and duly transferred to a special trust fund account to avoid misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: The year-end balance was transferred to a special trust account but not recognized as Trust Liability - DRRM

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the DVs be submitted immediately to this office, considering that these have already been long overdue for submission. Henceforth, FSs, DVs and ORs should be submitted within prescribed period to enable timely audit of account balances and transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Considerable delays are still incurred in the submission of accounts. Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit justification as to why reimbursements method was resorted even in the absence of contingencies, for evaluation. Henceforth, refrain from using the reimbursement method of payment unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the above-noted lapses. observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management comply with the provisions that require the allocation to GAD activities of a minimum of 5% of the Municipality’s annual total budget.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the required supporting documents, if any. Henceforth, the following should also be implemented:

 The Municipal Engineer Designate ensure that the required materials and quality tests to ensure the quality of the works of the contractors be made mandatory; and
 The Management to ensure that the 10% retention money be withheld and released only upon the final acceptance of the project and after the lapse of the 1-year defects liability period. Else, ensure that a substitute warranty security be filed by the contractor before the 10% retention money be released.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents
observations.reason: One transaction with no retention money bond cited in this report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate submit the basis in setting up the 2016 RPT/SET Receivable as well as the reconciling schedule to support the RPT/SET account Receivable and the corresponding Deferred Credits account balances as of December 31, 2016. The Accountant is likewise advised to review and analyze the nature of the discrepancy of the above-mentioned accounts and effect necessary adjustments in order to present fairly the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: Delays are still incurred in the submission of Contracts/POs
