observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality of Pilar discontinue the purchase of accountable forms from a private printer and strictly comply with the Office of the President of the Philippines MC No. 180, s. 2009 and DILG MC No. 2012-32, and GPPB Resolution Nos. 05-2010; 04-2011; 08-2012 and 24-2012.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
Instructed the BAC and Accounting Office to strictly comply with the
Provisions of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No 2012-001 on all procurement.
observations.reason: Specific provisions still need to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4, paragraph 6 of PD No. 1445 and DSWD MC No. 2015-004.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant must warrant that all disbursements are supported with complete and proper documentation before any transaction be processed for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Require the contractors to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission as required in Sec. 4.2 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, before allowing the 15 per cent advance payment on infrastructure contracts; and
Ensure that contractors abide with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in all procurement activities of the LGU.

We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent DF is used solely for development programs and projects and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01.
observations.managementAction: In the utilization of the 20 per cent DF, Management is now strictly adhering to the provisions of JMC No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM.
observations.reason:
Management overlooked a particular transaction which
led to the similar observation in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same immediately through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the concerned offices on the required dates, pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Maximize the utilization of the Prior Years’ Unspent Balance of DRRMF through the implementation of PPAs provided in the LDRRMF Investment Plan towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities.
observations.managementAction: Required the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare report on LDRRMF and submit same to LDRRMC and LDC. Further, to maximize utilization of Prior Years’ Unspent Balance of DRRMF.
observations.reason: Inability of the concerned personnel to religiously comply with the mandates pertaining to LDRRMF management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Comply with the requirements by the DENR-EMB and submit the revised 10-year SWM Plan to cause the immediate approval of the same in order to pave way and set direction in the effective implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality; and
Observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transport and handling of solid waste as required under 
Section 1(b) Rule X of the IRR of RA No. 9003 by providing the necessary training to the SWM staff and personnel to ensure that solid wastes are properly handled and that the risk of hazards to them and to the public are avoided or minimized.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd Public School District Supervisor (PSDS) and Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) as well as the required School Improvement Plan (SIPs) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDPs) and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB shall strictly comply and follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.
observations.managementAction: Required the LSB to prepare 3-year plan taking into consideration the recommendation of DepEd, PSDS and SDS including the drafting of SIP and DEDP.
observations.reason: DepEd personnel failed to prepare the SIPs and DEDPs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging to SEF the financial transaction or expenditures which are not among the primary purpose of the establishment of SEF and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses chargeable against SEF as provided under Section 272 of the Local Government Code (R.A. No. 7160) and Item No. 4.0 of JC No. 01 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG.
observations.managementAction: Enjoined the members of the LSB to include in the SEF budget only expenditures that are among the primary purpose for the establishment of SEF.
observations.reason: The concerned officers were not able to comply with the recommendations provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipality Development Council to update the CLUP in accordance with the approved Provincial CLUP as mandated by EO No. 72.
observations.managementAction: Conducted CLUP review and for submission and approval of S.P.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in determining the details of all dormant liability accounts in the books of the Municipality and in accordance with the prescribed guidelines, execute measures to fully settle these liabilities by proper implementation of the intended project/s, liquidation of the whole amount, disposition of the excess or thru write-off of account balances, if warranted. Likewise, remit to the recipient agencies all trust receipts due them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute measures to properly dispose these obsolete and unserviceable properties amounting to ₱ 2.25 million pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.
observations.managementAction: Management is taking steps to dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to Sec. 79 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: Actual disposal of obsolete and unserviceable properties is still to be carried out

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management abide by the rules of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM JMC No. 2013 – 01 and other pertinent guidelines on GAD to ensure that mandatory requirements and reports are timely submitted and procedures in its submission are complied. Further, we recommend that GAD Focal Person to prepare and maintain records on the detailed breakdown of expenditures pertaining to GAD activities.
observations.managementAction: Instructed GAD Focal Person and all concerned to prepare and submit reports on time pursuant to JMC No. 2013-01 of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer: 

Make proper planning with detailed listing of the priority projects and appropriation for individual project/activity; and

Ensure that the 20 per cent DF be expended only for specific and prioritized projects listed in the Approved Annual Investment Program.

observations.managementAction: Conducted meeting with all concerned personnel and discussed ways on how to collect delinquent taxes.
observations.reason:
Management still need to study to determine the most suited means to collect delinquent taxes and to develop the best collection schemes applicable within the locality.



Administrative Action still to be enforced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management set guidelines on the limitation of gasoline consumption.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Market Supervisor employ strategies to maximize the lease of vacant stalls and intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency.

Management prioritize the utilization of the vacant market stalls and satellite market by inviting interested parties to occupy/rent the stalls at an affordable price in order to achieve the purpose for which the building was constructed. Further, Management is advised to conduct a thorough feasibility study before embarking on a project to avoid waste of government funds.

Management strictly enforce Section 8 of the Market Ordinance of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Vacant stalls are being rented out and efforts to collect delinquent rental is intensified.
observations.reason:
The economic effectiveness of the strategies employed still need to be evaluated.



Vacant market stalls are being rented out but its viability is still under evaluation.

observations.recommendation: 1. Acting Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding check; 2. OIC Municipal Accountant evaluate outstanding checks aging above six months which are considered stale per Bank Reconciliation Statements as of August 7, 2018 and make necessary adjustments and prepare a JEV to record the transactions as Accounts Payable in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Vol. I; and 3. Maintain a separate subsidiary ledger to facilitate processing of future claims.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer cannot send written notice monthly due to voluminous work in the Office of the Treasurer.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel in the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare statement of accounts for the Lessees, and require to pay the corresponding unpaid monthly rentals and penalty/ies before any renewal of the contract in their favor; b) Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to monitor strictly the collection of market rentals; and c) Strictly enforce compliance with the terms and conditions stipulated in the Lease contract.

We recommended that OIC - Municipal Accountant: a) Submit the required supporting documents for RATA payrolls covering the period January to December 2018; and b) Ensure that the claims for RATA are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment in order to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended that management include and implement PPAs in the LDRRMF Budget that are compliant with the following laws, rules and regulations: a. Republic Act No. 10121 series of 2010; b. Section A of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012; and c. DBM-DILG-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Henceforth, the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer should strictly adhere with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the preparation of PPAs for LDRRMFIP and its utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Provide or allocate sufficient budget for the purchase of brand new equipment; and b) Strictly adhere with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the utilization of LDRRMF, particularly on specifications.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that all unexpended balances in the Trust Fund will be remitted back to the source national government agencies or reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund as soon as the project has been completed. Henceforth, review and monitoring of status of funds received from other government agencies be regularly performed so that appropriate actions thereon could be made.

We recommended that OIC - Municipal Accountant: a) Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annex A which are already overdue; and b) Henceforth, strictly comply Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.
observations.managementAction: Lack of Accounting Personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for these financial assistance/counterparts and require the recipients to submit the needed liquidations.

Moreover, record similar future transactions as Due from LGUs, a receivable account, to be recognized as expense only upon its proper liquidation by the implementing agency.
observations.managementAction: Prepared adjusting journal entries

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of Miscellaneous Income account and refrain from adding surplus from previous years to the current income. In addition, provide copy of the General Journals reflecting the adjustment made.
observations.managementAction: Prepared adjusting journal entries
observations.reason: Adjusting entry amounted to ₱4,937,633.40 was made to correct the error. (JEV No. 100-2019-06-19088 dated June 11, 2019).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately discontinue the granting of cash advances to elected officials other than traveling expenses and special purposes in the absence of legal basis for granting the same.
observations.managementAction: Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials other than traveling expenses
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management observe and comply with the provisions prescribed in Sec. 325(h) of RA 7160. Appropriations and actual expenses for EME in the ensuing year should be within the limitation.

We further recommend that management disburse municipal funds only in pursuance of an appropriation ordinance/budget to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: In the Annual Budget of CY 2018, there is no more Discretionary Fund or EME under the Sangguniang Bayan; the Budget Officer was just not able to edit it to Other MOOE. Further, actual expenses charged to EME in the financial statements pertain to traveling and expenses incurred during the athletic activities, e.g. Congressional Meet.
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare and submit the required report to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month per Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Submit the required report to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit a copy of the endorsement and review made by the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) of the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget. 

We also recommend that management make proper revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The plan was made in advance with an estimated budget of ₱4,237,819.21 but for the CY 2018 accomplishment report, utilized amount was increased to ₱5,815,588.09.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the GAD Plan and Budget is equal to 5% of the total annual budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management adhere to the provisions prescribed by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017, amending the Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security in the procurement of goods, in order to protect the interest of the government.

We further recommend that future procurements be deducted with the one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) retention money or require a special bank guarantee which is equivalent to one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price, otherwise, expenses due to the defects of the goods within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials approving the payment.
observations.managementAction: Implemented 3% retention fee for new purchases of goods.
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation..

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the granting of transportation allowance to the Municipal Mayor or any local official who has been issued with government vehicle. Further, deduct a proportionate amount from the transportation allowance of any official who used government vehicle.
observations.managementAction: The vehicle is for general use of the Municipality. Further the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment was transferred to the Property Officer
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services on a reimbursement/cash advance basis, except for petty expenses to be paid out from Petty Cash Fund. Instead, use any of the alternative methods of procurement as provided for under the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, if public bidding is not feasible.
observations.managementAction: Stop the practice of procuring goods and services on a reimbursement/cash advance basis
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management direct the BAC to judiciously prepare the technical specifications of the items to be procured. Only relevant characteristics and performance required of the equipment should be indicated in the Purchase Request to ensure fair competition among suppliers and obtain the most advantageous price without reference to brand names.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to be extra careful in preparation of the Purchase Requests (PR).
observations.reason: For CY 2019, brand names were not indicated in the PR

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184.
	
We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties to dispel presumptions of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) follows the provisions in the RA 9184 throughout the procurement process
observations.reason: All advertisements for public bidding on infra projects were all posted in the PHILGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to fast track the transmittal of source documents to the Accounting Unit. Also, require the Accountant to submit monthly trial balances to the Auditor on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Submitted reports to the Office of the Auditor
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant-designate prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013. 

It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2014 and its ensuing annual reports.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the AAPSI to the Auditor
observations.reason: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted within 10 days from receipt of Bank Statements. The failure to submit required reports may warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were prepared and submitted to COA. Adjustments were also made.
observations.reason: Some documents to support the preparation of the necessary journal entries were not available/furnished to the current Accountant. There were still reconciling items carried over from previous years.  (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Also, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation for the Due to LGUs account
observations.reason: Several employees were no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year. 

We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer furnished a soft copy of the list of taxpayers with the assessed value of the real properties. Also, there were adjustments made last October 2018.
observations.reason:
The list provided by the Treasurer has no breakdown as to the amount collectible for the current year and prior years.

Documents to support the preparation of the necessary journal entries were not available/furnished.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. Also, require the MT/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant, which shall be used as basis in recording issuance.

We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or GSO as the case may be, conduct physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies and the Municipal accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2019, First-in, First-out inventory method was used in costing of the supplies inventory. The Accounting Office also maintained the supplies inventory schedule.
observations.reason: Report for the physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies was not submitted

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, prepares stock/property cards and that the Municipal Accountant also maintain separate ledger cards to establish the details of the assets.

We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: Physical count was conducted in CY 2019 but was not completed and reconciled with recorded balances.
observations.reason: Report for the physical count of the property, plant and equipment was not submitted. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.
observations.managementAction: Settled in the current year totaling ₱206,013.70
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2019, full settlement of the disallowance amounting to ₱230,000.00, while partial settlements of ₱989,643.00 were made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the contractor to submit sufficient justification in the delay of the full implementation of the Bohol Earthquake Assistance (BEA) projects; otherwise, rescind the contracts, forfeit the contractors’ performance security and takeover the prosecution of the projects.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and deduct liquidated damages from the retention money pursuant to Item 8 Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: All projects are completed physically and only one package is still on process minor corrections.
observations.reason: The projects are 100% as of December 31, 2019 per monitoring of government projects submitted. However, computations for the liquidated damages were not done.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management expedite the promotion of deserving workers among the present pool of manpower resources in the municipality and the hiring of personnel to fill-up these vacant positions.
observations.managementAction: Filled-up 4 out of 5 vacant position as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: The appointment of Local Legislative Assistant in Vice Mayor’s Office was disapproved by the Civil Service Commission

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to charge and record the wages of the contractual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services.

We also recommend that management limit the hiring of casuals/job order employees for intermittent, emergency jobs or specific piece of work with short duration as guided by CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and maximize the services of the permanent employees for regular agency functions.
observations.managementAction: Management has started the charging and recording of casual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services
observations.reason: Job Orders has been charged to different programs or projects. Management is still in process of identifying workers involved in regular agency functions that will be recorded as PS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts, including purchase orders, with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval. Any unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: BAC and the Secretariat are advised and committed to comply with the submission of completed Purchase Orders and Contracts to COA within the prescribe period of all the incoming LGU procurement transactions.
observations.reason: Some contracts were not submitted within five (5) days after approval. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly and facilitate liquidation of fund transfers from source agency specially those long outstanding/ dormant accounts. 

Further, maintain and update subsidiary ledger in order to reconcile and trace long outstanding/dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation for the Due to other NGAs account
observations.reason: Accounts traced were due to error in the entry to transfer the completed projects from Trust fund to General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management direct the former Municipal Accountant to assist the current designate to reconcile and trace long outstanding/dormant accounts. It is further recommended that adjusting entries be made on errors and misclassification in order to present a reliable balance of the Due to LGUs.

We also recommend that the municipal accountant monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation for the Due to LGUs account
observations.reason: Accounts traced were due to error in the entry to transfer the completed projects from Trust fund to General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Property Custodian or the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS and request for computation to ensure that properties are adequately covered with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: On-going inventory of all insurable properties.
observations.reason: Partial insurance premium coverage is included in the 2019 Budget. The total insurance premium for all LGU properties is scheduled for appropriation in the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Discontinued the practice of releasing financial aid
observations.reason:
Non-submission of legal basis on the financial aid and the justification on the relevance of the activities.

The VMLP issued a resolution stating that the annual due is now at ₱40,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP meeting.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer to not accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction: Require the VMLP and PCL to submit the Legal basis for using multiple sets of receipts. If not complied the Municipal Treasurer will no longer accept said receipts for liquidation.
observations.reason: For further inquiry to  VMLP

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize the asset as Construction in Progress and record back the liability account. Upon submission of full liquidation to the grantor, the assets in the Trust Fund be closed to equity or Due to LGUs and transferred to the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity.

We further recommend that succeeding expenditures in the Trust Fund be made in accordance with Sections 97 and 104 of the NGAS Volume 1, to avoid misstatements in the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: All CY 2019Trust Fund transactions were recorded using the appropriate asset/ expense account and closed to the due/payable account; and transferred to General Fund upon liquidation.
observations.reason: Needs time to trace the trust fund transaction to effect the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit justification why these properties remained in the Trust Fund despite completion.

We further recommend the immediate transfer of all completed property, plant and equipment to the General Fund upon liquidation to the grantor and discontinue the practice of recognizing depreciation expense in the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction:
No depreciation expense was recognized in Trust Fund in CY 2016 and onwards.

Two projects, CICL and road, were transferred to General Fund.
observations.reason: Completed PPE still remained in the Trust Fund. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). 

We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: UCPB accounts were closed and transferred to DBP.
observations.reason: The LGU still maintains two (2) bank accounts with FCB, with year-end balance of more than P500,000.00/account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of ₱100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction:
No fund transfer was made in CY 2015-2019.

Instead of liquidation reports, the financial statements of the CHDI were submitted.
observations.reason: The CHDI did not submit liquidation reports. Fund transfer from member-LGU was included in the general budget of the CHDI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly and facilitate liquidation of fund transfers from source agency specially those long outstanding/ dormant accounts. It is further recommended that adjusting entries be made on errors and misclassification discussed in the foregoing paragraphs. The municipal accountant should also monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.
observations.managementAction: Submitted liquidation on current fund transfers.
observations.reason: Absence of sufficient records

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all collectors remit their collections intact daily to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Sections 15 and 29 of the New Government Accounting System Manual, in order to preclude the accumulation of unremitted collections by the collectors.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the use of the accountable forms strictly by the person to whom it was issued.

Transfer the collector of water fees under the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for the proper monitoring of his collections and prompt capture of the collection data.
observations.managementAction:
All collectors are directed to remit their collections daily.

The Municipal Treasurer strictly monitors the Accountable Forms. Also an AF officers designated is the one in-charge in approving of AF requested by the requesting officer and is recorded in the logbook. Collection of water fees is already in the MTO.
observations.reason: There are still instances of delayed remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to speed up the titling of these lands in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants.
observations.managementAction:
With the Land Titling Program of the DENR, the Municipal Assessor requested for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.

The Municipal Assessor expressed that the land donated wherein the municipal hall was constructed already had a court order but was not implemented. She already asked assistance from the Sangguniang Bayan regarding that matter, but none was done to enforce the order.  Also, the lots to be transferred to the Philippine National Police, Bureau of Fire and Rural Health Unit cannot be transferred due to the problem of the mother lot.
observations.reason: On-going titling of the land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require:

The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly.

The Municipal Treasurer to see to it that funds are really available when he signs the certification.
observations.managementAction:
Monthly transactions were reconciled for all bank accounts. 

The adjusted beginning balance of FCB-SEF has been established. However, other bank accounts were not.
observations.reason: Beginning balances were not reconciled since these were carried over from previous officers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

 Conduct immediate follow-ups to facilitate the issuances of title in its name, otherwise, cancel the transaction and request for the return of the payment from the lot owner.

Request for an SB Resolution confirming the purchase made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor has requested the DENR for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.
observations.reason: No appropriation for the processing of documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the accountant:

To immediately prepare adjusting entries to show the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan accounts.

In the succeeding amortization payments, review amounts in the billing statements before effecting payments and see to it that there is proper recording of the charges made.
observations.managementAction: Needs further verification from MDFO to reflect the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit explanation on the cause of the delay in the implementation of the CFLC project and the action taken to address the problem so that the desired purpose of the project can be attained.
observations.managementAction: Already operational, the CFLC is used as “bagsakan” for the catches of the fishermen and women.
observations.reason: Complied by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor intensify the implementation of the projects funded by financial assistance in order to timely achieve the desired benefits.  Also make representation with the respective donors of the financial assistance with unutilized balances and seek authority to use the fund balance to finance other projects/programs of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Implemented some projects out of the financial assistance received from different sources.
observations.reason: Balance of Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts amounted to ₱41,393,233.40 and ₱6,719,780.85, respectively, as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of Equipment Ledger Cards for every category is on-going
observations.reason: Preparation of Equipment Ledger Cards took a lot of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management to instruct the Cashier or the Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, all paid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents be properly stamped “PAID” and dated as to payment to preclude double payment of the same transactions.
observations.managementAction: All DVs were stamped “PAID”

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to collect the said amount of liquidated damages from the contractor and henceforth, instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor implementation of the projects to ensure whether these are completed as scheduled in order to properly determine the percentage of unfinished work, in cases where projects were not finished on time, which would serve as the basis in the computation of liquidated damages to be deducted in the subsequent/final billing of the contractor.
observations.managementAction: Billed the contractor for the amount of liquidated damages and deducted from payment of progress billing

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of P785,539.63 from general fund to 20% DF account immediately in compliance with Section 287 of RA 7160 and also to avoid the risk of utilizing it other than its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: Transferred P785,539.63 from the General Fund to Development Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Point System to refrain from granting financial assistance to individuals in the GAD Plan and Budget without conducting appropriate gender analysis and/or gender assessment and ensure that the planned GAD programs and activities are truly gender-responsive.
observations.managementAction: Refrained from granting financial assistance to individuals.
On-going process of reconciliation of records
observations.reason: 1) Non- implementation of ordinance on fines & penalties 2) No segregation of wastes stored in the Barangay MRF

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the unreconciled differences. Records ascertaining the details of each account per fund shall be maintained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of reconciling the discrepancy amount.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of reconciling the discrepancy amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all procurement undertaken is included and in accordance with the approved APP, including approved changes thereto. Management shall plan its procurements meticulously and prudently. We further recommend that Management shall revise its APP whenever there is procurement of necessary items not included in the Approved APP.
observations.managementAction: Observed the documentary requirements on common government transactions provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Procurement documents provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to include Project Inspection Report and Statement of Time Elapsed to its punch-list of the basic essential required documents to support the claims for progress and final payments to the contractors to ensure the quality of the cumulative value of work and in accordance with the approved plans and specifications. The Management shall strictly observe the proper and complete documentation of any claims against government funds by strengthening internal control through submission of the above mentioned documents before disbursing government funds.

We recommended and Management agreed with the proper conditions stated above shall be strictly complied with by the LGU. Ensure the compliance to the documentary requirements required with the guidelines and submit the same to the proper authorities.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the documentary requirements and submitted the same to the proper authorities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the abovementioned contract projects be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for auditorial and technical review, so that Management shall be informed immediately of any deficiencies or flaws that may be noticed or observed thereon and such be corrected.
observations.managementAction: Perfected contract projects were submitted to the Office of the Auditor for auditorial and contract review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the BAC to prepare a letter inviting observers at any stage of the procurements process in compliance with Section 13 of the IRR of RA 9184 in order to enhance the transparency of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Observers were invited in all stages of the procurement

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to deduct the equivalent amount of P265,919.44 from the claims of the contractor representing the excessive computation of VAT which is at the advantage of the contractor resulting to loss of government fund.
We further recommended and the concerned Municipal Engineer agreed to closely supervise the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) to ensure that correct VAT percentage is used in accordance with existing laws and rules and regulations relative to the preparation of ABCs

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to speed up the completion of the construction of a central MRF in order that timely desirable environmental management outcomes can be enjoyed by the general public.
observations.managementAction: Completed the construction of Central MRF

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to reconcile and trace its records as to when and to whom the refund of compulsory premiums applies. Make representation with the GSIS inquiring the details of such and take appropriate actions to avoid idle funds. Utilize the fund according to its purpose and according to its account classification in conformity with existing rules and regulations. It is further recommended that efficient and effective utilization of the fund should be anchored on the promotion of the general welfare.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation of records
observations.reason: The Municipality still reconcile and trace its records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; instruct the Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks; and adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency.
observations.managementAction: Made journal entry to restore the cash account for the equivalent amount of stale checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to appoint or designate a GSO who shall specifically take custody and be accountable for all the properties, real or personal, of the LGU and to prepare and maintain stock cards and property cards for all its inventories and PPEs; create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of Equipment Ledger Cards for every category is on-going
observations.reason: Preparation of Equipment Ledger Cards took a lot of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management instructed the GSO to prepare IIRUP for approval of the proper authorities; the Committee on Appraisal of Property appraises those properties and request the disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and regulation to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Lack of Equipment Ledger Card

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to designate an employee who shall be responsible to facilitate the LTO registration and insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality. Apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented in the process of applying for LTO registration and insurance.
observations.reason: The management is in the process of applying for LTO registration and insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of disbursing government funds without valid and proper supporting documents. Further, Management shall utilize first the proceeds from sale of bid documents and other related fees collected in the payment of honoraria to the members of the BAC, Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat before sourcing out from the savings of the Municipality since honoraria are not considered mandatory expenditure items which will be given priority in the use of savings from Personal Services of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Honoraria paid to the BAC members were limited to the proceeds from the sales of Bidding Documents

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2016 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the total amount of P3,725,661.17in accordance with Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Item 6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically the guidelines on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF to ascertain that the financial information on LDRRMF are taken up appropriately in the books and properly disclosed in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P3,725,661.17 was transferred to a Special Trust Fund account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations in the documentations of disbursements of government funds to ensure its validity and propriety. Further, require the BAC to submit all other documents that would prove the legitimacy of suppliers as authorized dealers.
observations.managementAction: Observed the documentary requirements on common government transactions provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: documentary requirements on common government transactions provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and Management agreed to: 1) effect the corrections and recommendations by the EMB; 2) once the plan was approved by the EMB, submit its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and integration in the provincial solid waste management plan; 3) implement the rules and procedures on proper segregation, collection, transportation and disposal of solid waste materials as prescribed in Sections 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, and 37 of the Act; and 4) adhere to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in the creation, implementation and monitoring of the LGU solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and Management agrees that in the disbursement of government funds the Municipality shall provide the necessary supporting documents required on COA Circular No. 2012-001 and in compliance of Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Municipality provides the necessary supporting documents required on COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management required the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly comply with the regulations relative to the submission of the monthly reports and supporting documents and the Financial Statements to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the receipt of the said documents to ensure the timely review and verification of the accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The accountant submitted the 2016 FS before required period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the attached schedule. All claims shall always be supported with complete documentation; faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions of any government agency. Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the above-noted irregularities. Failure to comply may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required supporting documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to make certain that all barangay shares from the Real Property collections are remitted to the LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer’s Office prepares the DVs for Brgy. RPT share quarterly.
observations.reason: RPT share of the barangays were still not remitted on time.

observations.managementAction: Completed the projects and cottages are now utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to establish and/or strengthen the institutional mechanisms as required in JMC No. 2013-01 by reconstituting the GFPS in accordance with the structure and composition in the MCW-IRR which includes a TWG; directing the Municipal Planning and Development Office to set up GAD Database and maintain the same; and once established and strengthened, the GFPS are enjoined to thoroughly review the GPB to ensure that it addresses concrete gender issues/GAD mandate and that attribution from regular PPAs are gender-responsive.
observations.managementAction: Creation of GAD database ongoing
observations.reason: Still working with the GAD database

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain general and subsidiary ledgers for the financial accounts of the LGU. These ledgers shall be the source of information to prepare schedules to facilitate verification of the accounts in the financial statements. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4.8, 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Still on the process of creating general and subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: Still on the process of creating general and subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accountant to revert the accounting entry she made by debiting Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and crediting Due to NGAs account for the subject amount. Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1 in dropping from the books the accounts subject for request for relief.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant drew JEV to revert the entry.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant drew JEV to revert the entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply and adhere to the terms and conditions embodied in an agreement covering a project to ensure its total implementation. This assures that there will be no losses of government resources and the people will benefit therefrom; conduct competitive bidding to all procurement of the Agency as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184. Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; and submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the above discussion as well as the justification on the occurrence of the irregularities. Non-compliance thereof may cause suspension or disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Charging of prior year’s transactions from prior year’s to current year’s transactions already minimized.
observations.reason: Charging of prior year’s transactions to current year’s appropriations already minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop the practice of charging against the 20% development fund the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures; refrain from charging against the fund the expenditures which do not have corresponding appropriation to comply with Section 4.1 of P.D. No. 1445; provide the Auditor with computation as basis for the amount contributed to KALAHI-CIDDS-NCDDP amounting to P1,700,000.00; submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the irregularities noted in Nos. 1 and 2. Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal use.
observations.managementAction: Expenses to 20% Development Fund were allowed only when in accordance with the regulation.
observations.reason: Charging of expenses to 20% Development Fund were allowed only when in accordance with the regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the accountable officers to immediately submit to the Accounting Office liquidation of their cash advances; Failure to comply may cause the filing of appropriate charges against the accountable officer pursuant to Sections 9.3.2 and 9.3.3 of the above-mentioned COA Circular; prohibit the granting of additional cash advance to the accountable officer if his/her previous accountabilities had not been liquidated or a proper accounting thereof had been made; remind the accountable officers to always settle their cash advances within the required period or at the end of each year; and strictly observe the rules, laws and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Agency directed the Municipal Accountant to stop granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances are first liquidated.
observations.reason: Management instructed the Municipal Accountant to stop granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances are first liquidated.

Issue demand letters compelling the AOs or employees to immediately settle in full their outstanding cash advances, either by liquidation, by payment/refund or through salary deductions; and
Strictly comply with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and all related rules and regulations pertaining to cash advances.

Management Comment:
Management assured that they we will continue this good practice from hereon.

Management Comment:

Management Comment:

We recommended that Management ensure proper safekeeping of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration.
We also recommended that Management direct the:
Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in COA Circular No. 92-386 which are either through (a) public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by destruction; and
Municipal Accountant to make necessary entries to derecognize the unserviceable properties from the books of the LGU after the duly conducted disposal procedure.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require:
The Local Finance Committee to:
Conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;
Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating departments towards attaining the targets set; and
Provide for the targets on accounts that did not have projections which should be based on statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectations.
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and other departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to perform efficiently in the revenue administration by considering, among others, the following:
Conduct a periodic review to ascertain whether collection targets are attained and in case of shortfall in collections, to analyze the reasons and to look into alternative actions to avoid incurrence of lapses in projection in the future;
Intensify further the collection of business and real property taxes and avail of administrative and judicial remedies in enforcing payments particularly in tax delinquency; and
Closely monitor the personnel involved in the verification of documents, assessment and collection of taxes as to whether they are performing their mandated functions and see to it that taxes are correctly imposed, assessed, collected and remitted to the government. Appropriate action be initiated against personnel not complying with the requirements of laws, rules, regulations, and existing policies of the Municipality.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Initiate measures to collect the delinquencies from market rentals amounting to almost ₱2.0 million and employ strategies to intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and
Ensure that the market stall holders have a valid and subsisting “Contract of Lease”.
We also recommended that:
The Municipal Market Supervisor employ strategies to maximize the lease of vacant stalls and intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and
The Local Sangguniang Bayan enact a new or revised Market Code to adjust the rates to current levels.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Demand from the identified suppliers the settlement of tax deficiency of ₱63,870.55; and
Perform examination of the books of accounts of business establishments and/or exhaust possible means in obtaining the correct gross receipts/sales for the preceding CY thru the BIR or the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
Further, we recommended that the BPLO and the MTO:
Require the taxpayer to present and submit the Annual ITR for the preceding CY, or if not yet available, the ITR for the next preceding CY, together with the other financial documents such as Audited FS and proof of payment, to be retained by both offices in the taxpayer’s permanent file for current and future reference; and
Require the submission of the BIR Certificate of Registration or BIR Form 2303 for all businesses applying or renewing business permits to validate the correctness of the line of business indicated in their application forms and to avoid misapplication of tax rates.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the business proof of income in the renewal of business permit for the review of the gross receipts/sales declared in the application form that shall be retained by the MTO in the taxpayer’s permanent file for current and future reference;
Include the presumptive income levels to be used in the PILAA in the revision of the Municipal LRC;
Review and ensure that all application forms are properly filled out; and
Include the statement required in Section 6.1.1, illustrated in Annex 1 of DILG-DTI-DICT JMC No. 01 in the business permit application form.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require all AOs to remit daily the full amount collected by them promptly and intact;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to check first the inventory of accountable forms and remittance of cash before issuance of another accountable forms;
Demand from the concerned Collector of Business Tax the missing ORs and the remittance of unremitted collections; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to determine the unremitted full amount by the erring collector and to file appropriate legal sanctions, if warranted.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We also recommended that Management:
Ensure that the procedures in the post closure land use plan be strictly carried out. These include inspection, maintenance and monitoring after the implementation of closure activities which shall maintain the integrity and compliance of the closed dumpsite; and
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to expedite the preparation of IEE so that upon issuance of ECC, the construction of the Sanitary Landfill could already be initiated and the cost to provide services for the disposal of solid waste could be reduced or eliminated.

Management Comment:

We recommended that Management instruct the MDC to have an in-depth planning of PPAs to be included in the 20 per cent DF AIP, presenting in details specific items of PPAs embodied therein such as specific project name, location, feasible schedule of project implementation and expected output for timely implementation of the projects.

Management Comment:

We recommended that the MDC and all other implementing offices of the Municipality maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

Non-inclusion of PPAs to be charged to the Unexpended LDRRMF of previous years’ in the LDRRMFIP
Section 4.4 of the abovementioned Circular provides that the unexpended LDRRMF shall accrue to a special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five years. The LDRRMC shall decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF which shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.
Likewise, Section 5.1.2 of the same Circular provides that the LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program shall be prepared annually. It shall present the 30 per cent allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded. The LDRRMFIP shall also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years. (underscoring supplied)
Moreover, Section 5.1.3 of the said Circular states that the release and use of the LDRRMF shall be supported by the Local Sangguniang Resolution and the declaration of state of calamity for the QRF and LDRRMFIP for projects and activities listed in the approved plan as incorporated in the development plan and annual work program and financial plan.
As previously discussed above, the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2019 presented the amount of ₱11.62 million under the Prior Year’s Unexpended Balance (MOOE). Review however of the submitted documents revealed that the MDRRMO was not able to prepare the PPAs for the funds totaling ₱11.62 million, representing the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for previous years, which should have been incorporated in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2019 as required by the abovementioned regulations.
Further, the Audit Team noted that in CY 2019, disbursements amounting to ₱10.65 million were incurred by the Municipality under the Prior Years’ Unexpended LDRRMF. Details are as follows:
Table 18 – Summary Appropriation and Utilization of Prior Years’ Unexpended LDRRMF
The non-inclusion of the list of PPAs chargeable against the unexpended balance of previous years’ LDRRMF in the LDRRMFIP hindered the Audit Team to properly evaluate the economy, efficiency and effectiveness of disbursements totaling ₱10.65 million. Moreover, validation on whether or not the utilization of the LDRRMF was in accordance with Municipality’s Five-Year Comprehensive LDRRMF Plan cannot be performed.
On the other hand, it was also noted that the CY 2019 LDRRMFIP was submitted by Management to the Audit Team beyond the prescribed period. It was only upon request that the Audit Team was provided with a copy of the LDRRMFIP.
The utilization of the unexpended balance of previous years’ LDRRMF should have been duly embodied under the required LDRRMFIP in order to assure propriety of its fund management. Likewise, the absence of which may pose the risk of fund misuse and/or misapplication.
We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to submit to the Auditor the LDRRMFIP within the prescribed period.
We also recommended that the MDRRMO include in the LDRRMFIP the list of PPAs chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Management Comment:
Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and DEDPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes. Moreover, please justify why the non-priority expenses may not be disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the: Barangay Officials/BSWMB to – submit reliable and correct reports to assess the real status of solid waste management program in the barangay to the responsible department; strictly enforce or recommend collection of fines and penalties for violators of prohibited acts on RA No. 9003; improve barangay MRF or, if possible, establish an ecological facility in every barangay; enforce strict compliance on the “no segregation, no collection policy”; MSWMB to – conduct massive IEC on waste segregation at source, re-use and recycling of wastes; conduct monthly validation/monitoring of the barangay compliance on Solid Waste Management in order to submit reliable consolidated reports to proper authorities and measure the status of the implementation of the program; Municipal Mayor, if possible, to – install a full time MENRO to focus on environmental Management; pursue its plan, as contained in its SWMP, to establish a long-term institutionalized process aimed at having law abiding citizens through the implementation of laws, rules and regulations and develop on enormous number of people who have the love and care for the environment targeting child and youth through values formation; Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of garbage fees and penalties to support the program.
observations.managementAction: The Management is currently looking for an Environmental Specialist I that will lead the MENR Office and focus on environmental management. VAC Clean and Green, a group of employees under their Performance Governance System (PGS), will also help in the implementation of the various programs. The Municipality will help the barangays to reestablish the functionality of the barangay MRFs and the MSWMB will check on other ways and methods that can help reduce and divert their wastes thru benchmarking from best implementers of solid waste management. The Municipal Treasurer will intensify collection of garbage fees and penalties to support the program.
