observations.reason:
AAR CY 2017;
AAR CY 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit explanation on the cause of the delay in the implementation of the CFLC project and the action taken to address the problem so that the desired purpose of the project can be attained.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management require the concerned Municipal employees to refund the cost of gasoline issued to privately owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Sangguniang Bayan impose and implement the required taxes, fees and permits as mandated in the Revised Revenue Code and whenever applicable, consider the revision of the said code in order to impose rates which are responsive to the present economic situation considering that the same was enacted way back in CY 2006.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan is still on the process of revising the LGU’s Revenue Code.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management return to the National Treasury the unutilized / unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2015

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend that management should review and evaluate the list of KKK Fund loan grantees in the attached Annex A if it is still valid and collection is still possible. If found to be futile, request for the write off of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management is not able to comply with the requirements for the Request for Write-off of Accounts.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2012

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections in her possession to the authorized government depository bank to safeguard government funds from risks of loss.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2015; AAR CY 2004; AAR CY 2003

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should require the Municipal Livestock Coordinator, who is in – charge of the dispersal program of the municipality, to monitor the project, prepare and submit a report regularly to the Accounting Division any increase in the livestock inventory due to returns from recipients as well as the mortality duly supported with an affidavit and witnessed by any of the barangay officials to ensure the reliability, accuracy and validity of  the recorded amount in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Reports were not prepared and the livestock were not monitored regularly.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2012

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; instruct the Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks; and adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to appoint or designate a GSO who shall specifically take custody and be accountable for all the properties, real or personal, of the LGU and to prepare and maintain stock cards and property cards for all its inventories and PPEs; create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accountant to revert the accounting entry made by debiting Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and crediting Due to NGAs account for the subject amount.  Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1 in dropping from the books the accounts subject for request for relief.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management instructed the GSO to prepare IIRUP for approval of the proper authorities; the Committee on Appraisal of Property appraises those properties and request the disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and regulation to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations in the documentations of disbursements of government funds to ensure its validity and propriety. Further, require the BAC to submit all other documents that would prove the legitimacy of suppliers as authorized dealers.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: The BAC fail to submit required documents to the COA office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to establish and/or strengthen the institutional mechanisms as required in JMC No. 2013-01 by reconstituting the GFPS in accordance with the structure and composition in the MCW-IRR which includes a TWG; directing the Municipal Planning and Development Office to set up GAD Database and maintain the same; and once established and strengthened, the GFPS are enjoined to thoroughly review the GPB to ensure that it addresses concrete gender issues/GAD mandate and that attribution from regular PPAs are gender-responsive.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain general and subsidiary ledgers for the financial accounts of the LGU.  These ledgers shall be the source of information to prepare schedules to facilitate verification of the accounts in the financial statements.  Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4.8, 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason:
Still no general and subsidiary ledgers prepared and maintained by the Municipality.

Reiterated on AAR CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accountant to revert the accounting entry she made by debiting Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and crediting Due to NGAs account for the subject amount.  Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1 in dropping from the books the accounts subject for request for relief.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: Management failed to revert the accounting entry made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to inform the SB to execute a resolution covering the payments of wages for JOs; remind the HRMO that service contracts provided to the parties shall always be acknowledged by the concerned recipients; instruct the concerned department heads to carefully review and sign the DTRs of the subordinates, specially of the JOs, before corresponding computation of wages shall be made thereto; advise the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the JOs’ performance specially on the collections of revenues.  Require the latter to remit all its collections to the former on a daily basis; and adhere faithfully to the prevailing laws, rules and regulations in the payment of wages to JOs.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: Recommendations were still not implemented by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to conduct and finish annual physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Property Custodian - Designate to maintain and update Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No Inventory taking conducted as of December 31, 2015 and 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to reclassify the current portion or the balance of Long Term Liabilities account which is due for settlement within twelve months after the reporting period into Current Liabilities amounting to P2,550,268.38 from Long Term Liabilities; and adhere to the provisions of PAS No. 1 on proper presentation of financial transactions and accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No management actions taken
observations.reason: No reclassification made yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to prepare trip tickets in every use of the motor transportation; submit to the Office of the Auditor the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption within the first ten (10) days of the succeeding month; and adhere faithfully the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: No Management action
observations.reason: No Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team; instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents before affixing his signature in the disbursement voucher; require the Accounting Department to make sure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments of honorarium in compliance with the provision of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008; and adhere to provisions on the payment of BAC honorarium set forth in DBM BC No. 2004-5A, Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, Section 2.79(I) of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: The Management still failed to submit the documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to maintain separate containers/ receptacles for each type of waste for temporary placement of solid waste prior to its final hauling to an open dump site, and which should be properly marked or identified as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" or "special waste”; require the collectors and other personnel directly dealing with the collection of solid waste to wear personal protective equipment and paraphernalia, such as mask, rubber boots and the like; transport solid waste using trucks/haulers with covers; and convert the open dump to a controlled dump site.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: Still the management failed ti implement the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation: Implement minimum and standard requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collections and their final disposal to the dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The LGU still failed to implement the provisions of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We have recommended that management exhaust all possible means to
document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer
Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU, after which, the
assessed value should be recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

2.6 We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to locate records of road
networks in their offices where the necessary data, specifications and
locations of the Road Networks may be sourced to facilitate the
recognition of road networks and the corresponding depreciation in the
accounting books, the preparation of subsidiary ledgers and necessary
disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

2.7 We have also recommended that Management ensure compliance with
the conduct of inventory and the recognition of Road Networks in the
books of accounts as well as the disclosure thereof in the Notes to
Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

3.8 We have recommended that management see to it that the unexpended
/ unobligated funds from prior and current year LDRRM are treated as
Trust Liability at the end of the year and duly transferred to a special
trust fund account to avoid misuse of funds.

4.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all the
necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF in the financial
statements. Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure
requirements on LDRRMF as provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.

5.7 We have recommended that the Check DVs be submitted immediately
to this office, considering that these have already been long overdue for submission. Henceforth, FSs, DVs and ORs should be submitted within prescribed period to enable timely audit of account balances and transactions.

6.11 We have recommended that management submit justification as to why
reimbursements method was resorted even in the absence of contingencies,
for evaluation. Henceforth, refrain from using the reimbursement method of
payment unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate
payment thereof.

We reiterated that the LCE direct the: (a) Inventory Team to conduct regular physical count of all properties and submit the prescribed report thereon to COA on or before January 31 of each year; (b) Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) and Real Property Ledger Card (RPLC); (c) Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description of cost segregation of road components for road projects, (d) Municipal Assessor exert more effort in ascertaining the ownership of the land and all the improvements therein to minimize the risk of being exposed to third party claim, (e) Inventory Committee Disposal Committee to (i) install adequate control in the properties and supply management to properly safeguard its assets and (ii) undertake the proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with applicable rules and regulations on disposal procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: a) Municipal Assessor follow up with the Provincial Government of Bataan for the immediate action in resolving the linkage problem of the PATAS; b) Management provide personnel to assist the Treasurer prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers; and c) Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the –
BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG to observe strict compliance with the provisions of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of competitive bidding and evaluate thoroughly the completeness and correctness of bid documents submitted by the bidders to ensure that only legally, technically and financially capable contractor/supplier is awarded with the contract;
Municipal Engineer to –
require the contractors to submit warranty security following the required form, amount and validity period under Sections 62.2.3.3 and 62.2.3.4 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
prepare and submit Project Completion Report for the six (6) water system in accordance to the requirements of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure that all programs/projects/activities appropriated from the fund are in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the payment for rehabilitation of Veterans Park/construction of Pilar Museum is supported with document classifying the Veterans Park as historical site before making any payment to provide reasonable basis for establishing the validity of claims, otherwise, require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the subject amount from the General Fund to the 20% Development Fund; and (c) direct the Municipal Engineer to properly monitor the project implementation by submitting accomplishment reports vis-a-vis planned targets on a quarterly basis.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the Philhealth deadlines in the remittance of contributions so as to avoid the payment of interest and penalties; and make representation with the Philhealth on behalf of employees on their unremitted contributions made due to its non-membership thereof.

We commended the Municipality’s continuous efforts in prioritizing pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.  We also recommended and Management agreed to reconstitute the member of GFPS EXECOM to include therein representatives from the private and woman’s Organizations to comply with the guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the –
BAC to faithfully comply with the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement for infrastructure projects;
Municipal Engineer to comply with the requirement of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and various DPWH Department Orders in the preparation of planning documents and implementation of infrastructure projects; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular 2012-001, before making any payment.
We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipality of Pilar discontinue the purchase of accountable forms from a private printer and strictly comply with the Office of the President of the Philippines Memorandum Circular No. 180, s. 2009 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-32, and GPPB Resolution Nos. 05-2010; 04-2011; 08-2012 and 24-2012.
We recommended that the Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of RIRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management adhere to the documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4, paragraph 6 of PD No. 1445 and DSWD MC No. 2015-004.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant must warrant that all disbursements are supported with complete and proper documentation before any transaction be processed for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Require the contractors to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission as required in Sec. 4.2 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, before allowing the 15 per cent advance payment on infrastructure contracts; and
Ensure that contractors abide with the implementing rules and regulations of RA No. 9184 in all procurement activities of the local government unit.

We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent DF is used solely for development programs and projects and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same immediately through the LDRRMC and LDC to the concerned offices on the required dates, pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the Prior Years’ Unspent Balance of DRRMF through the implementation of PPAs provided in the LDRRMFIP towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities.

We recommended that Management:
Comply with the requirements by the DENR-EMB and submit the revised 10-year SWM Plan to cause the immediate approval of the same in order to pave way and set direction in the effective implementation of the solid waste management program of the Municipality; and
Observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transport and handling of solid waste as required under Section 1(b) Rule X of the IRR of RA No. 9003 by providing the necessary training to the SWM staff and personnel to ensure that solid wastes are properly handled and that the risk of hazards to them and to the public are avoided or minimized.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and DEDPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly were the bases for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB shall strictly comply and follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned Joint Circular.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging to SEF the financial transaction or expenditures which are not among the primary purpose of the establishment of SEF and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses chargeable against SEF as provided under Section 272 of the Local Government Code (R.A. No. 7160) and Item No. 4.0 of Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG.

We recommended that Management update the 2008 Revenue and Tax Code of the Municipality pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection of local taxes that can be used to finance more valuable projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that:
Acting Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding check;

OIC Municipal Accountant evaluate outstanding checks aging above six months which are considered stale per Bank Reconciliation Statements as of                December 31, 2019 and make necessary adjustments and prepare a JEV to record the transactions as Accounts Payable in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Vol. I; and 

Maintain a separate subsidiary ledger to facilitate processing of future claims.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare statement of accounts for the Lessees, and require to pay the corresponding unpaid monthly rentals and penalty/ies before any renewal of the contract in their favor;

Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to monitor strictly the collection of market rentals; and

Strictly enforce compliance with the terms and conditions stipulated in the Lease contract.

We recommended that management include and implement PPAs in the LDRRMF Budget that are compliant with the following laws, rules and regulations: 

Republic Act No. 10121 series of 2010; 
Section A of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012; and
DBM-DILG-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

Henceforth, the Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer should strictly adhere with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the preparation of PPAs for LDRRMFIP and its utilization.

We recommended that Management:

Provide or allocate sufficient budget for the purchase of brand new equipment; and 

Strictly adhere with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the utilization of LDRRMF, particularly on specifications.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that all unexpended balances in the Trust Fund will be remitted back to the source national government agencies or reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund as soon as the project has been completed.

Henceforth, review and monitoring of status of funds received from other government agencies be regularly performed so that appropriate actions thereon could be made.

We recommended that OIC Municipal Accountant:

Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annex A which are already overdue; and 

Henceforth, strictly comply Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.

We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Also, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.
Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for these financial assistance/counterparts and require the recipients to submit the needed liquidations.
Moreover, record similar future transactions as Due from LGUs, a receivable account, to be recognized as expense only upon its proper liquidation by the implementing agency.

We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, prepares stock/property cards and that the Municipal Accountant also maintain separate ledger cards to establish the details of the assets.
We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that Management direct the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current designated Accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, provide allowance for impairment and/or request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant validate the reconciling items in preparation of journal entries to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU. Further, submit justification/basis and journal entry vouchers for the adjustments made from the previous reconciling items.
Furthermore, we recommend that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted within 10 days from receipt of Bank Statements. The failure to submit required reports may warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of Miscellaneous Income account and refrain from adding surplus from previous years to the current income. In addition, provide copy of the General Journals reflecting the adjustment made.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile and trace long outstanding/dormant accounts. It is further recommended that adjusting entries be made on errors and misclassification in order to present a reliable balance of the Due to LGUs.
We also recommend that the municipal accountant monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly and facilitate liquidation of fund transfers from source agency specially those long outstanding/ dormant accounts.
Further, maintain and update subsidiary ledger in order to reconcile and trace long outstanding/dormant accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit justification on the difference between the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable which was due to the collection of CY 2018.
We further recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year.
Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7.1 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommend that Management immediately discontinue the granting of cash advances to elected officials other than traveling expenses and special purposes in the absence of legal basis for granting the same.

We recommend that management observe and comply with the provisions prescribed in Sec. 325(h) of RA 7160. Appropriations and actual expenses for EME in the ensuing year should be within the limitation.
We further recommend that management disburse municipal funds only in pursuance of an appropriation ordinance/budget to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend to stop the granting of transportation allowance to the Municipal Mayor or any local official who has been issued with government vehicle. Further, deduct a proportionate amount from the transportation allowance of any official who used government vehicle.

We recommend that management prepare and submit the required report to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month per Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Assistant to update and reconcile the individual personnel Leave Cards and deduct from the leave credit balances the corresponding tardiness and undertime incurred, otherwise the overpayment caused by the non-deduction will be disallowed in audit.  Further, monitor the tardiness and undertime of the employees and consider the provisions of the Revised Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service in the imposition of sanctions if warranted to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Assistant require the submission of the accomplishment report of the job order personnel to support the payment of their wages.  Further, require the refund from the concerned Job Order personnel the overpayment of wages equivalent to the tardiness and undertime incurred, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee in consultation with the end user ensure that technical specifications of the items to be procured be provided in the preparation of the bidding documents to facilitate competitive bidding and the requirement of the Procuring Entity are met.  Also, make sure that the supporting documents were complete and all the necessary data were available, otherwise the transaction will be disallowed in audit.
We further recommended for the submission of a written explanation on the deficiencies noted in this audit memorandum.
We recommended that the BAC designate members of the TWG who have technical expertise that will suit to the nature procurement being processed.
We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit proof of submission of the APP to the GPPB as well the explanation as to the non-submission of the PMR.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned personnel exert effort to monitor the status of the agricultural produce for distribution and breeding stocks and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the review and analysis of the details of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify the revenue collection efforts to meet the budgetary requirements to finance the municipality’s programs, projects, and activities.   Further, appropriate/budget within the reasonable or attainable estimates so as not to incur negative appropriation.

1.9	We recommended and Management agreed to create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

2.9	We recommended and the Management agreed to:
2.9.1	Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the adjusting journal entries as follows:
2.9.2	From then on, require the HRMO in charge to submit to the Accounting Unit the periodic report on accumulated leave balances to facilitate the recognition of the cost of the compensated absences incurred for the current year.
2.9.3	 Set up slowly the necessary funds for terminal leave benefits of employees by accruing the monthly actual earned leave benefits of its personnel.
2.9.4	Henceforth, instruct the Accounting Unit to periodically record in the books of accounts the compensated absences earned and ensure that funds is set up for the timely and expeditious release of the retirement pay in accordance with Paragraph 11 of the Philippine Accountings Standards (PAS) 19 and Section 1 of Republic Act No. 101541 respectively.

3.5	We recommended to the management to meticulously screen any loan application of its employees, especially those offered by financial institutions aside from the GSIS and PAG-IBIG to ensure that their net take home pay does not go below the required minimum take home pay provided by law.

4.5	We recommended to the Management to instruct the Cashier or the Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, all paid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents be properly stamped “PAID” and dated as to payment to preclude double payment of the same transactions.

5.7	We recommended and the Management agreed to collect the said amount of liquidated damages from the contractor and henceforth,  instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor implementation of the projects to ensure whether these are completed as scheduled in order to properly determine the percentage of unfinished work, in cases where projects were not finished on time, which would serve as the basis in the computation of liquidated damages to be deducted in the subsequent/final billing of the contractor.

6.4	We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of P785,539.63 from general fund to 20% DF account immediately incompliance with Section 287 of RA 7160 and also to avoid the risk of utilizing it other than its intended purpose.

7.9	We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Point System to refrain from granting financial assistance to individuals in the GAD Plan and Budget without conducting appropriate gender analysis and/or gender assessment and ensure that the planned GAD programs and activities are truly gender-responsive.

8.9	We recommended and the Management agreed to thoroughly examine and evaluate the activities to be included in its LDRRMF plan to ensure its attainability and full implementations towards an effective and efficient performance of the LGU in responding to any natural and man-made calamities.
