observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

Property Custodian to include the immovable (real) property in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and prepare the report on a standard format;

Municipal Accountant to reconcile PPE balances with the Property Custodian; and

Inventory Committee to regularly conduct physical count of PPE as necessary and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in determining the details of all dormant liability accounts in the books of the Municipality and in accordance with the prescribed guidelines, execute measures to fully settle these liabilities by proper implementation of the intended project/s, liquidation of the whole amount, disposition of the excess or thru write-off of account balances, if warranted. Likewise, remit to the recipient agencies all trust receipts due them.
observations.reason:
Initially exerted
effort to determine the details of all dormant account

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to be responsible in the consolidation of End Users PPMPs and APP preparation in the prescribed format as provided by GPPB Circular No. 07-2015; and


Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR), to be approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) and to be submitted to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the CY 2016 RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Local School Board (LSB) with correct income estimate of Special Education Tax in the preparation of annual SEF budget. For the savings generated in CY 2017 transactions, the LSB to pass a supplemental budget and to utilize the fund in accordance with DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRM council:

to evaluate and study carefully the pertinent data on risk assessment so as to determine the priority needs pertaining to LDRR management and accordingly reprogram the unexpended MOOE portion of CY 2017 LDRRM fund; 

to immediately implement all doable PPAs funded from continuing appropriations and from prior years unexpended balances reprogrammed for capital outlay; and
to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to examine carefully the charges from the components of the LDRRM fund and observe proper classification of accounts as prescribed by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in order to produce a more comprehensive, accurate and reliable report.

We likewise recommended the following journal entries to correct the LDRRM fund balance of the Municipality including the balances of the accounts affected:

Trust Fund (TF) books:
Other PPE		                       141,490.00
Prior Period Adjustment	                         141,490.00

To book up PPE purchases previously recorded as expenses.

Trust Liabilities – DRRMF                   843,760.00     
 Due to NGAs 	                                   843,760.00
To correct the entries made for the purchase of medical service vehicle chargeable against the Special Trust Fund for LDRRM erroneously debited to Due to NGAs account in the TF books.

Trust Liabilities – DRRMF                 116,000.00
Due from Other Funds                        116,000.00

To correct the charging of purchased aerial vehicle (drone) which should have been charged against the Special Trust Fund for LDRRM instead of CY 2017 appropriation for Capital Outlay. 

General Fund (GF) books:

Due to Other Funds	116,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment	116,000.00

To reduce the amount to be transferred to the Special Trust Fund for LDRRMF and increase the total Continuing Appropriations for the fund for the ensuing year.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer make proper planning with detailed listing of the priority projects and appropriation for individual project/activity to avoid possible issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management:

maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the planned timeframe; and

require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prepare a monitor system tool to evaluate the implementation phase of each PPAs appearing in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Performance Budget.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the LCE require the following to avoid possible issuance of Notice of Suspension:


the MBO/MPDO to carefully review the budget during its preparation. Do’s and Don’ts in the 20 per cent Development Fund PPAs need to be identified selectively so that non-priority expenses will not be included in the  priority projects of the Municipality; and

the Municipal Accountant to refrain from signing in the Box A of disbursement voucher form if obligations against 20 per cent Development Fund is not in accordance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute measures to properly dispose these obsolete and unserviceable properties amounting to ₱2,248,504.25 pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.
observations.reason: Still verifying records and actual condition of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to meticulously verify the status of long outstanding fund transfers still maintained in its books and require the Municipal Treasurer to remit to the grantor-agency any unutilized amount pursuant to their existing Memorandum of Agreement. If proven non-existing, the Municipality to request write-off the said account.
observations.reason:
Initially exerted
effort to determine the details of these accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the LDRRMF. A detailed plan with specific PPAs should be prepared and the unspent balance of QRF of previous years be treated as mitigation fund and not as QRF of the next ensuing fiscal year. Further, management should strictly adhere with the above cited guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The planned PPAs will be evaluated and be implemented in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Some projects were already implemented and others were not due to reconsideration of the necessity of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipality Development Council to update the CLUP in accordance with the approved Provincial Comprehensive Land Use Plans as mandated by Executive Order (EO) No. 72.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the LGU availed the services of fiber optic internet connection and concerned personnel in the preparation of CLUP will be required to attend seminar and conferences for them to gain additional knowledge in CLUP preparation.
observations.reason: Due to technical difficulty and slow internet connection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management abide by the rules of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 01 and other pertinent guidelines on Gender and Development (GAD) to ensure that mandatory requirements and reports are timely submitted and procedures in its submission are complied. Further, we recommend that GAD Focal Person to prepare and maintain records on the detailed breakdown of expenditures pertaining to GAD activities.
observations.managementAction: GAD personnel will be required to attend trainings and seminar in the preparation of GAD this CY 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of technical knowledge by the GAD focal person.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer:  
	
make proper planning with detailed listing of the priority projects and  appropriation  for  individual project/activity; and

ensure that the 20% Development Fund be expended only for specific and prioritized projects listed in the Approved Annual Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018 Annual Budget, it was already corrected, no more lumpsum appropriation.
observations.reason: Some projects could not be readily specifically identified because the implementation depends on the need as arises.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Issue demand letter to the retired Municipal Treasurer for the immediate deposit of the amount of ₱1,902,524.52 otherwise institute appropriate charges against the erring accountable officer;
observations.managementAction: The Clearance from Property and Accountability was not issued to former Treasurer. For current collection, strict compliance on remittances had been implemented.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer failed to settle her accountabilities despite issuance of Demand Letter.

Demand Letters were periodically sent to delinquent taxpayers with the help of barangay officials. However, some factors hindered the collection efficiency as follows:

a. Heirs have no knowledge in property titling and would not pay taxes unless respective shares are registered under their name
b. Properties still registered in the names of great grandparents and legitimate heir has no knowledge of the existence of the inheritance; c. Properties used as road lots are still registered in the names of the previous owners;
d. Properties owned by absentee owners cannot be located or no forwarding/ incomplete address

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management call the attention of the accounting office to exert extra effort in the retrieval of detailed information on said receivables, otherwise request for write off in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will request for write-off of these account.
observations.reason: No available documents on file.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to review and evaluate the unutilized or unexpended balances of national aids, other financial assistance and other trust funds and request for subsequent use of the balance or return them to the trustor, otherwise these should be remitted to the National Treasury for reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund as required by existing regulations.

We also recommended that management retrieve the Memorandum of Agreement entered by the Municipality and the PFDA and/or make representations with PFDA for the settlement of their obligations.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will request for write-off of these account.
observations.reason:
No available record on file.


No available record on file.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that –

The Municipal Market Supervisor employ strategies to maximize the lease of vacant stalls and intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency.


Management prioritize the utilization of the vacant market stalls and satellite market by inviting interested parties to occupy/rent the stalls at an affordable price in order to achieve the purpose for which the building was constructed. Further, Management is advised to conduct a thorough feasibility study before embarking on a project to avoid waste of government funds.

Management strictly enforce Section 8 of the Market Ordinance of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Demand letters were already sent to the delinquent taxpayers.

Some of the vacant stalls were already rented


Stallholders were notified of the provisions of Section 8 of the Market Ordinance

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank;

Make representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and

Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that Management 

Strictly comply with Section 2.2 and Sub-sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, to ensure that the public is informed of any subsequent projects that will be implemented to foster transparency and accountability and also to encourage public participation. 
Henceforth, contractors should be briefed on the need to put up the required notifications.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that all unexpended balances in the Trust Fund will be remitted back to the source national government agencies or reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund as soon as the project has been completed.

We recommended that Management fully implement the project and immediately utilize the same to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives of RA 9003 in ensuring the sound collection, sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of waste generated in the respective barangays.

We recommended that Management:

GAD Focal Person should make extra effort to look for resource persons and skilled trainors to immediately implement the PPAs on trainings and likewise spearhead in the organization of LGBT community in the municipality; and

Henceforth, GAD Focal Person should closely monitor and supervise its implementation as required by the PCW and strictly implement all programs, projects and activities embodied in the Municipalities approved Annual Investment Plan and Budget.

We recommended that:

LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month using the format hereto attached        (Annex Q); and

Make representation with the Municipal Mayor to request for additional personnel for the Office of the LDRRM.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare journal entries in the succeeding year to adjust the advance recording of Transfers for Project Equity share/LGU counterpart. 

We further recommend that transfers of LGU counterpart from the general fund to the trust fund be recognized only during actual transfer of funds.
observations.managementAction: A journal entry was prepared to adjust and correct the amount

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit sufficient justification in allocating budget for non-legislative activities under the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan.

We further recommend that management discontinue the practice of allocating funds for non-legislative activities that is discretionary in nature under the Sangguniang Bayan pursuant to Section 325(h) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Discontinue in CY 2018
observations.reason: No allocation of non-legislative activities under the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan in CY 2018 Budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create a Local Council for the Protection of Children to advocate child rights, plans and recommends interventions and monitors children’s programs and projects in the locality.

We also recommend that one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) be allocated to ensure that all plans for children are provided with funds, pursuant to Section 15 of Republic Act 9344.
observations.managementAction: Allocated 1% of the IRA in CY 2018 Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the appropriate asset account for the supplies, equipment and materials purchased out of the trust fund in order to recognize the asset funded by other government agency for use by the LGU. Upon liquidation to the grantor, the assets should be removed in the Trust Fund books and transfer them in the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 transactions were recorded in accordance with Sections 97 and 103 of the NGAS Vol. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to replenish the General Fund with the amount of the SEF checks which were not cleared/re-deposited amounting to P53,469.00.

In the meantime, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry setting up the receivable (Due from Other Funds) and payable accounts (Due to Other Funds) in the General Fund and Special Education Fund respectively. 

Stop the practice of encashing checks out of its collections. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare regularly the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

Provide this Office copies of the pertinent documents relating to the re-deposit of P52,687.00 such as validated deposit slip and bank statements.
observations.managementAction:
Adjustment to set up receivable from the Municipal Treasurer was made

Stops the practice of encashing checks out of its collections

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
Stop from involving any employee in the collecting activities of the local government unit if he/she is not regularly appointed and officially designated agency personnel. 
Require all accountable officers to apply for the proper amount of fidelity bond in relation to the handled cash accountabilities so as to safeguard government funds and protect the interest of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Regular accountable officers are bonded.
observations.reason: All employees handling cash are all regular and fully bonded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit sufficient justification in the unlawful transfer and utilization of the trust fund. 

We further recommend that management immediately discontinue the practice of juggling of funds as it violates Section 2 and 84 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Discontinued the practice of juggling of funds

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit the complete set of approved plans and drawings including the detailed breakdown of the approved budget for the contract, unit cost analysis, program of work and the detailed engineering design for the project.

We further recommend that management practice the timely submission of complete set of contract documents to facilitate the review on the reasonableness of contract cost and duration.
observations.managementAction: Complete set of Approved Plans and Drawings with final designs and estimates are submitted to COA thru the Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted within 10 days from receipt of Bank Statements. The failure to submit required reports may warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were prepared and submitted to COA. Adjustments were also made.
observations.reason: Some documents to support the preparation of the necessary journal entries were not available/furnished to the current Accountant. There were still reconciling items carried over from previous years.  (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Also, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Demand letters were issued to officers and employees. 

The Municipal Accountant enforces strictly the prescribed rules and regulations in handling of cash advances and the rendering of accounts.
observations.reason: Several employees were no longer connected with the LGU. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year. 

We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer furnished a soft copy of the list of taxpayers with the assessed value of the real properties. Also, there were adjustments made last October 2018.
observations.reason:
The list provided by the Treasurer has no breakdown as to the amount collectible for the current year and prior years.

Documents to support the preparation of the necessary journal entries were not available/furnished. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. Also, require the MT/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant, which shall be used as basis in recording issuance.

We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or GSO as the case may be, conduct physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies and the Municipal accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, First-in, First-out inventory method was used in costing of the supplies inventory. The Accounting Office also maintained the supplies inventory schedule.
observations.reason: Report for the physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies was not submitted

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, prepares stock/property cards and that the Municipal Accountant also maintain separate ledger cards to establish the details of the assets.

We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: Physical count was conducted in CY 2018 but was not completed and reconciled with recorded balances.
observations.reason: Report for the physical count of the property, plant and equipment was not submitted. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.
observations.managementAction: Settled in the current year totaling ₱206,013.70
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, full settlement of the disallowance amounting to ₱206,013.70, while partial settlements of ₱1,153,928.80 were made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the contractor to submit sufficient justification in the delay of the full implementation of the Bohol Earthquake Assistance (BEA) projects; otherwise, rescind the contracts, forfeit the contractors’ performance security and takeover the prosecution of the projects.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and deduct liquidated damages from the retention money pursuant to Item 8 Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: All projects are completed physically and only one package is still on process minor corrections.
observations.reason: The projects are 100% as of December 31, 2018 per monitoring of government projects submitted. However, computations for the liquidated damages were not done.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management expedite the promotion of deserving workers among the present pool of manpower resources in the municipality and the hiring of personnel to fill-up these vacant positions.
observations.managementAction: Filled-up 4 out of 5 vacant position in CY 2018
observations.reason: The appointment of Local Legislative Assistant in Vice Mayor’s Office was disapproved by the Civil Service Commission

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to charge and record the wages of the contractual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services.

We also recommend that management limit the hiring of casuals/job order employees for intermittent, emergency jobs or specific piece of work with short duration as guided by CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and maximize the services of the permanent employees for regular agency functions.
observations.managementAction: Management has started the charging and recording of casual and job order workers performing regular agency functions to Personal Services
observations.reason: Job Orders has been charged to different programs or projects. Management is still in process of identifying workers involved in regular agency functions that will be recorded as PS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRM Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly report is prepared but not submitted to COA.
observations.reason: The assigned officer prepares and submits a quarterly report to the Department of Interior and Local Government every quarter. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts, including purchase orders, with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval. Any unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: BAC and the Secretariat are advised and committed to comply with the submission of completed Purchase Orders and Contracts to COA within the prescribe period of all the incoming LGU procurement transactions.
observations.reason: Some contracts were not submitted within five (5) days after approval

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit a copy of the endorsement and review made by the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) of the agency’s Gender and Development Plan and Budget.

We also recommend that management make proper revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.
observations.managementAction: A GAD Plan/Program was developed to address gender issues within the concerned sectors.
observations.reason:
For CY 2014, the LGU provided an allocation of only 3% of the total budget.

For CY 2015, the GAD Plan includes funds provided by other government agencies.

For CY 2016, the GAD Budget is only 1% of the total annual budget.

For CY 2017, the GAD Budget is only 3% of the total annual budget.

(Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the Property Custodian or the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS and request for computation to ensure that properties are adequately covered with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: On-going inventory of all insurable properties.
observations.reason: Partial insurance premium coverage is included in the 2019 Budget. The total insurance premium for all LGU properties is scheduled for appropriation in the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Discontinued the practice of releasing financial aid
observations.reason:
Non-submission of legal basis on the financial aid and the justification on the relevance of the activities.

The VMLP issued a resolution stating that the annual due is now at ₱40,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP meeting.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer to not accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction: Require the VMLP and PCL to submit the Legal basis for using multiple sets of receipts. If not complied the Municipal Treasurer will no longer accept said receipts for liquidation.
observations.reason: For further inquiry to  VMLP

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize the asset as Construction in Progress and record back the liability account. Upon submission of full liquidation to the grantor, the assets in the Trust Fund be closed to equity or Due to LGUs and transferred to the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity.

We further recommend that succeeding expenditures in the Trust Fund be made in accordance with Sections 97 and 104 of the NGAS Volume 1, to avoid misstatements in the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: All CY 2018 Trust Fund transactions were recorded using the appropriate asset/ expense account and closed to the due/payable account; and transferred to General Fund upon liquidation.
observations.reason: Needs time to trace the trust fund transaction to effect the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit justification why these properties remained in the Trust Fund despite completion.

We further recommend the immediate transfer of all completed property, plant and equipment to the General Fund upon liquidation to the grantor and discontinue the practice of recognizing depreciation expense in the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction:
No depreciation expense was recognized in Trust Fund in CY 2015 and onwards.

Two projects, CICL and road, were transferred to General Fund.
observations.reason: Need to trace numerous transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). 

We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: UCPB accounts were closed and transferred to DBP.
observations.reason: The LGU still maintains two (2) bank accounts with FCB, with year-end balance of more than P500,000.00/account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184.

We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties to dispel presumptions of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee follows the provisions in RA 9184 throughout the procurement process.
observations.reason: Contracts are still awarded to lone bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to fast track the transmittal of source documents to the Accounting Unit. Also, require the Accountant to submit monthly trial balances to the Auditor on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Submitted reports to the Office of the Auditor
observations.reason:
Source of documents from MTO were not submitted ahead; thus late preparation of Accounting Office of monthly trial balances.

Some reports were not submitted within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of ₱100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction:
No fund transfer was made in CY 2015-2018.

Instead of liquidation reports, the financial statements of the CHDI were submitted.
observations.reason: The CHDI did not submit liquidation reports. Fund transfer from member-LGU was included in the general budget of the CHDI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant-designate prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013. 

It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2014 and its ensuing annual reports.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the AAPSI to the Auditor
observations.reason: Delayed preparation and submission of the AAPSI

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly and facilitate liquidation of fund transfers from source agency specially those long outstanding/ dormant accounts. It is further recommended that adjusting entries be made on errors and misclassification discussed in the foregoing paragraphs. The municipal accountant should also monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.
observations.managementAction: Submitted liquidation on current fund transfers.
observations.reason: Absence of sufficient records

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all collectors remit their collections intact daily to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Sections 15 and 29 of the New Government Accounting System Manual, in order to preclude the accumulation of unremitted collections by the collectors.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the use of the accountable forms strictly by the person to whom it was issued.

Transfer the collector of water fees under the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for the proper monitoring of his collections and prompt capture of the collection data.
observations.managementAction:
All collectors are directed to remit their collections daily.

The Municipal Treasurer strictly monitors the Accountable Forms. Also an AF officers designated is the one in-charge in approving of AF requested by the requesting officer and is recorded in the logbook. Collection of water fees is already in the MTO.
observations.reason: There are still instances of delayed remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to speed up the titling of these lands in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants.
observations.managementAction: With the Land Titling Program of the DENR, the Municipal Assessor requested for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor expressed that the land donated wherein the municipal hall was constructed already had a court order but was not implemented. She already asked assistance from the Sangguniang Bayan regarding that matter, but none was done to enforce the order.  Also, the lots to be transferred to the Philippine National Police, Bureau of Fire and Rural Health Unit cannot be transferred due to the problem of the mother lot. | On-going titling of the land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require:

The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly.

The Municipal Treasurer to see to it that funds are really available when he signs the certification.
observations.managementAction:
Monthly transactions were reconciled for all bank accounts. 

The adjusted beginning balance of FCB-SEF has been established. However, other bank accounts were not.
observations.reason: Beginning balances were not reconciled since these were carried over from previous officers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

 Conduct immediate follow-ups to facilitate the issuances of title in its name, otherwise, cancel the transaction and request for the return of the payment from the lot owner.

Request for an SB Resolution confirming the purchase made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor has requested the DENR for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.
observations.reason: No appropriation for the processing of documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the accountant:

To immediately prepare adjusting entries to show the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan accounts.

In the succeeding amortization payments, review amounts in the billing statements before effecting payments and see to it that there is proper recording of the charges made.
observations.managementAction: Needs further verification from MDFO to reflect the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit regularly and on a timely basis all the Reports of Collections and Deposits to facilitate the recording in the books of accounts.  Further, consider the assignment of a Liquidating Officer to assist in the receipt / review of reports submitted by collectors and to hasten the report preparation and submission to the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: Assigned a Liquidating Officer as recommended and the submission of RCD have been regulated and timely.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% Development Fund be utilized to fund priority programs or projects which are capital in nature in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: PPAs included the AIP funded by the 20% DF are all capital in nature.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the payments by checks of the municipality be made payable under the name of the business entity pursuant to Section 93 and 124 of P.D. 1445 to avoid risk of possible misuse and /or misappropriation.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor intensify the implementation of the projects funded by financial assistance in order to timely achieve the desired benefits.  Also make representation with the respective donors of the financial assistance with unutilized balances and seek authority to use the fund balance to finance other projects/programs of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Implemented some projects out of the financial assistance received from different sources.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2016

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that contract documents for all procurement of infrastructure projects contain all the general and special conditions required of a contract and to provide protection to the rights of the LGU when a matter relating to the poor or non-performance by the contractor occurs.

We recommend further, that the municipality pursue forfeiture of performance bond in favor of the LGU in case the contractor falls short in performing its contractual obligations to the LGU for the construction of these projects.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to reconcile the result of the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments thereof, preferably before the year ends so as not to reiterate this observation.  Further, cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers / cards which shall be kept for each class of PPE to record the description, acquisition, transfer/disposal and other information about the asset.
observations.managementAction: Physical count of Inventory and PPE was conducted but was not completed.
observations.reason:
AAR CY
2014; AAR CY 2013; AAR CY 2009 
AAR CY 2010;
AAR CY 2011; AAR CY 2007; AAR CY 2004

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality strictly observe the pertinent provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG-DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects and ensure that said fund is optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: The 20% DF was not completely utilized.
observations.reason: AAR CY 2017; AAR CY 2015

observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to transfer the refunds for GSIS compulsory premiums from the Trust Fund to the General Fund. Prepare the suggested adjusting entries in order to correct the errors which resulted to misstatements of the financial statements in the General and Trust Funds by P202,622.50. :
Suggested Journal Entry
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the unreconciled differences. Records ascertaining the details of each account per fund shall be maintained.
observations.managementAction: Partial Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of reconciling the discrepancy amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry for the improper recognition of the funds transferred to the barangays by debiting Due From LGUs (1-03-03-030) and crediting Prior Period Adjustment (3-01-01-020) in the amount of P957,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of P549,998.00 from general fund to 20% DF account immediately in order to avoid the risk of utilizing it other than its intended purpose and to comply with Section 287 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to accomplish all required documents to support the disbursements of General Fund and 20% Development Fund. Once the projects shall be completed, require the Municipal Engineer to inspect said projects. Further, require the barangays to liquidate the funds received by submitting liquidation reports attached with Summary of Checks Issued, Summary of Cash Payments, its corresponding disbursement vouchers and pertinent supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to advise the Municipal Treasurer to carefully examine each transaction and its supporting documents before issuing a check to avoid committing the same error in the future; and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer, Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile their records regularly so that errors or revisions taken up in either department will be adjusted appropriately by the rest.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to transfer the deficient amount of P784,129.70 from General Fund to LDRRMF and ensure the appropriation for LDRRMF have full cash back-up to have ample fund to defray disaster-related expenses in time of emergencies.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all procurement undertaken is included and in accordance with the approved APP, including approved changes thereto. Management shall plan its procurements meticulously and prudently. We further recommend that Management shall revise its APP whenever there is procurement of necessary items not included in the Approved APP.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to make certain that 50 percent provincial share from Special Education Fund are remitted to the Provincial Treasury Office within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 9 of RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to (1) stop the disposal operations at the open dumpsite; and (2) immediately formulate a Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) in accordance to the guidelines set in Section 17 of RA 9003 and DAO No. 9 series of 2006 and submit it to EMB for approval then implement it by employing environmentally sound procedures in order to comply with RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC, including the BAC Secretariat and the TWG, to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents. Adhere strictly with the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR, COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2012-001 on the procurement of goods, infrastructures and services.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to include Project Inspection Report and Statement of Time Elapsed to its punch-list of the basic essential required documents to support the claims for progress and final payments to  the contractors to ensure the quality of the cumulative value of work and in accordance with the approved plans and specifications. The Management shall strictly observe the proper and complete documentation of any claims against government funds by strengthening internal control through submission of the above mentioned documents before disbursing government funds.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed with the conditions stated above shall be strictly complied with by the LGU. Ensure the compliance to the documentary requirements required with the guidelines and submit the same to the proper authorities.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU shall prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers to ensure that transferred funds are used only for the intended purpose and that proper accounting and reporting is made of the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the abovementioned contract projects be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for auditorial and technical review, so that Management shall be informed immediately of any deficiencies or flaws that may be noticed or observed thereon and such be corrected.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the BAC to prepare a letter inviting observers at any stage of the procurements process in compliance with Section 13 of the IRR of RA 9184 in order to enhance the transparency of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: There was still some procurement wherein the Team did not receive a letter invitation for observers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to deduct the equivalent amount of P265,919.44 from the claims of the contractor representing the excessive computation of VAT which is at the advantage of the contractor resulting to loss of government fund.
We further recommended and the concerned Municipal Engineer agreed to closely supervise the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) to ensure that correct VAT percentage is used in accordance with existing laws and rules and regulations relative to the preparation of ABCs
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to speed up the completion of the construction of a central MRF in order that timely desirable environmental management outcomes can be enjoyed by the general public.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to reconcile and trace its records as to when and to whom the refund of compulsory premiums applies. Make representation with the GSIS inquiring the details of such and take appropriate actions to avoid idle funds. Utilize the fund according to its purpose and according to its account classification in conformity with existing rules and regulations. It is further recommended that efficient and effective utilization of the fund should be anchored on the promotion of the general welfare.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipality still reconcile and trace its records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to make certain that all withheld VAT, EVAT and percentage taxes withheld are remitted to the Authorized Agent Banks or Revenue District Offices having jurisdiction over the place where the government office is located within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 12 of RA No. 9337 and Section 4.112 of Revenue Regulation (RR) No. 16-2005.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to designate an employee who shall be responsible to facilitate the LTO registration and insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality. Apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: The management is on process of applying for LTO registration and insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of disbursing government funds without valid and proper supporting documents. Further, Management shall utilize first the proceeds from sale of bid documents and other related fees collected in the payment of honoraria to the members of the BAC, Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat before sourcing out from the savings of the Municipality since honoraria are not considered mandatory expenditure items which will be given priority in the use of savings from Personal Services of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2016 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the total amount of P3,725,661.17in accordance with Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Item 6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically the guidelines on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF to ascertain that the financial information on LDRRMF are taken up appropriately in the books and properly disclosed in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality opened a Special Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017 the Municipal Treasurer transferred P2,495,861.17 only to the Special Trust fund account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and Management agreed to: 1) effect the corrections and recommendations by the EMB; 2) once the plan was approved by the EMB, submit its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and integration in the provincial solid waste management plan; 3) implement the rules and procedures on proper segregation, collection, transportation and disposal of solid waste materials as prescribed in Sections 16, 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, and 37 of the Act; and 4) adhere to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in the creation, implementation and monitoring of the LGU solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: The Management submitted 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan to NSWMC but still on the process of implementing the rules and procedures on proper segregation, collection, transportation and disposal of solid waste materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and Management agrees that in the disbursement of government funds the Municipality shall provide the necessary supporting documents required on COA Circular No. 2012-001 and in compliance of Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Municipality provides the necessary supporting documents required on COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: Still there are some disbursements without complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management required the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly comply with the regulations relative to the submission of the monthly reports and supporting documents and the Financial Statements to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the receipt of the said documents to ensure the timely review and verification of the accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The accountant submitted the FS before required period.
observations.reason: Notes to FS and Condensed consolidated of Financial Performance and Cash flow was not submitted on or before the required period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the attached schedule. All claims shall always be supported with complete documentation; faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions of any government agency. Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the above-noted irregularities.  Failure to comply may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Some documents required were not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to make certain that all barangay shares from the Real Property collections are remitted to the LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: RPT share of the barangays were still not remitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) Submit to the Auditor the above-mentioned documentary requirements.  Failure to do so may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and (2) Strictly adhere to the DILG MC No. 97-273 in the conduct of lakbay-aral or study tours.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Documentary requirements required are not all submitted to COA office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to thoroughly examine and evaluate its plans, programs and activities to identify the weaknesses resulting to non-implementation and/or minimal implementation of its programs and activities most importantly the disaster prevention and mitigation and disaster preparedness programs resulting to failure to attain its intended purpose and deliver efficient and effective services to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Several programs, projects and activities in the LDRRMF were not implemented

observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal had already written a letter regarding the matter and no reply yet from TIEZA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply and adhere to the terms and conditions embodied in an agreement covering a project to ensure its total implementation.  This assures that there will be no losses of government resources and the people will benefit therefrom; conduct competitive bidding to all procurement of the Agency as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; and submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the above discussion as well as the justification on the occurrence of the irregularities.  Non-compliance thereof may cause suspension or disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management already stopped charging transactions from prior year’s to current year’s appropriation
observations.reason: Charging of prior year’s transactions to current year’s appropriations already minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop the practice of charging against the 20% development fund the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures; refrain from charging against the fund the expenditures which do not have corresponding appropriation to comply with Section 4.1 of P.D. No. 1445; provide the Auditor with computation as basis for the amount contributed to KALAHI-CIDDS-NCDDP amounting to P1,700,000.00; submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the irregularities noted in Nos. 1 and 2.  Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal use.
observations.managementAction: Charging of expenses to 20% Development Fund were allowed only when in accordance with the regulation.
observations.reason: Required documents were not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the accountable officers to immediately submit to the Accounting Office liquidation of their cash advances.  Failure to comply may cause the filing of appropriate charges against the accountable officer pursuant to Sections 9.3.2 and 9.3.3 of the above-mentioned COA Circular; prohibit the granting of additional cash advance to the accountable officer if his/her previous accountabilities had not been liquidated or a proper accounting thereof had been made; remind the accountable officers to always settle their cash advances within the required period or at the end of each year; and strictly observe the rules, laws and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Liquidations were already submitted to the Office of the Accountant and a memorandum was issued requiring the Accountable Officers to settle their obligations.
observations.reason: There are still unliquidated cash advances granted from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to immediately file before the Office of the Auditor a request for relief from property accountability for the death of 27 cattle with the available supporting evidence as enumerated in COA Memorandum Circular No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992.  The matter shall be resolved in accordance with the provisions on request for relief stated in the 2009 COA Revised Rules of Procedures.  This shall also be the basis of dropping from the books the cost or value of the animals; and adhere strictly to Section 73(1) of P.D. No. 1445 in case losses of government money and/or property occur.
observations.managementAction: The management was on the process of complying the supporting documents for request of relief from property accountability.
observations.reason: No request for relief submitted to the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to formulate and include only GAD PPAs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB and GAR in a manner and in prescribed forms to comply Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC; submit to the Auditor justification/explanation why the management incurred expenses that are not in accordance with the objectives of the GAD fund, as well as documents to prove that the PPAs indeed addressed emerging gender issues and concerns.  Failure to comply may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.
observations.managementAction: PPAs included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive.
observations.reason: Most of the PPAs included in the GAD Plan are still not supportive of the government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to advise the contractors to fast track the implementation of the projects, programs and activities to facilitate speedy completion thereof and improve performance rates.  As to PPAs administered by the Municipality, effect implementation timeliness to serve as guide in monitoring the status of project and enhance monitoring activities in order to effect prompt attainment of the program.
observations.managementAction: PAPs under 20% Development Fund were implemented.
observations.reason: Not all PAPs identified in the Annual Investment Plan were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to liquidate immediately with the Accounting Section the unsettled cash advances; revert the accounting entry made in booking up the CIF liquidation amounting to P869,000.00; and strictly observe the provisions set forth in COA Circular Nos. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Agency directed the Municipal Accountant and to effect the required liquidation/settlement of cash advances.
observations.reason: Some AOs already settled and liquidated their accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to ensure that service contracts must contain the attestation clause and the duties and responsibilities to be performed, duly signed by two instrumental witnesses, before a hired employee under job order could render services.  A copy of the service contracts must also be issued to and received by the employee concerned; seek SB authority/resolution allowing the Municipal Mayor to incur obligation in paying the wages of the job orders; and revised the rates given to the job orders, from fixed monthly rate to hourly, daily or piecework rate basis.
observations.managementAction: Job Order prepared.
observations.reason: Recommendation of the Auditor not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required financial statements and reports to the Audit Team in a manner and frequency required in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and Chapter 4 of NGAS Manual for LGU.
observations.managementAction: Reports were prepared monthly.
observations.reason: Accounts submitted to the Office of the Auditor but with no FS for the period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Agency, through the Local Government Unit GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), formulate and include programs, projects and activities that would only address women’s human rights and gender discrimination in the government policies, programs and procedures, in a manner prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: PAPs prepared related to GAD
observations.reason: Most of the PAPs prepared are not gender responsive.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to utilize the programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development and Trust Funds through proper planning and timing of execution and coordination among LGU officials and employees to ensure implementation of the same; fast track the implementation of the PPAs to facilitate speedy completion thereof and improve performance rates; and effect implementation timeliness to serve as guide in monitoring the status of project and enhance monitoring activities in order to effect prompt attainment of the program.
observations.managementAction: PPAs are implemented for 20% Development and Trust Funds.
observations.reason: PPAs were implemented but delayed

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of charging expenses to the 20% Development Fund which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The Municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The management only  charge authorized expenses to 20% Development Fund
observations.reason: ineligible charges to 20% Development Fund were already minimized.

observations.recommendation: That management instruct the municipal officials/employees concerned to submit the lacking supporting documents to COA for evaluation. Henceforth, honoraria should be granted only to officials/employees/ personnel under the above stated circumstances.

observations.managementAction: Reimbursement method is still being extensively resorted to by management.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the IRA for development projects. Likewise, consider utilizing the fund for rooted developments which are permanent in nature to benefit more the constituents of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank;

Make a representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and

Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant:

Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annex H which are already overdue; and 

Henceforth, strictly comply Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.
We recommended that LDRRMO prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month using the format hereto attached (Annex M).
We recommended that Management fully implement the project and immediately utilize the same to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the MSWMB strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the establishment of a sanitary landfill.

We recommend that management return to the National Treasury the unutilized / unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.
observations.managementAction: Not yet remitted to the Bureau of Treasury,

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management require the concerned Municipal employees to refund the cost of gasoline issued to privately owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The amount of gasoline issued to privately owned vehicle was not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should require the Municipal Livestock Coordinator, who is in – charge of the dispersal program of the municipality, to monitor the project, prepare and submit a report regularly to the Accounting Division any increase in the livestock inventory due to returns from recipients as well as the mortality duly supported with an affidavit and witnessed by any of the barangay officials to ensure the reliability, accuracy and validity of the recorded amount in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: No inventory was conducted to verify the existence and status of the livestock distributed

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; instruct the Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks; and adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to appoint or designate a GSO who shall specifically take custody and be accountable for all the properties, real or personal, of the LGU and to prepare and maintain stock cards and property cards for all its inventories and PPEs; create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accountant to revert the accounting entry made by debiting Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and crediting Due to NGAs account for the subject amount.  Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1 in dropping from the books the accounts subject for request for relief.

observations.managementAction: No management action

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to establish and/or strengthen the institutional mechanisms as required in JMC No. 2013-01 by reconstituting the GFPS in accordance with the structure and composition in the MCW-IRR which includes a TWG; directing the Municipal Planning and Development Office to set up GAD Database and maintain the same; and once established and strengthened, the GFPS are enjoined to thoroughly review the GPB to ensure that it addresses concrete gender issues/GAD mandate and that attribution from regular PPAs are gender-responsive.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1 in dropping from the books the accounts subject for request for relief.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to inform the SB to execute a resolution covering the payments of wages for JOs; remind the HRMO that service contracts provided to the parties shall always be acknowledged by the concerned recipients; instruct the concerned department heads to carefully review and sign the DTRs of the subordinates, specially of the JOs, before corresponding computation of wages shall be made thereto; advise the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the JOs’ performance specially on the collections of revenues.  Require the latter to remit all its collections to the former on a daily basis; and adhere faithfully to the prevailing laws, rules and regulations in the payment of wages to JOs.

observations.managementAction: No management actions taken

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to conduct and finish annual physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Property Custodian - Designate to maintain and update Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
