observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to reclassify the current portion or the balance of Long Term Liabilities account which is due for settlement within twelve months after the reporting period into Current Liabilities amounting to P2,550,268.38 from Long Term Liabilities; and adhere to the provisions of PAS No. 1 on proper presentation of financial transactions and accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No management actions taken
observations.reason: No reclassification made yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to prepare trip tickets in every use of the motor transportation; submit to the Office of the Auditor the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption within the first ten (10) days of the succeeding month; and adhere faithfully the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: No Management action
observations.reason: No Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team; instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents before affixing his signature in the disbursement voucher; require the Accounting Department to make sure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments of honorarium in compliance with the provision of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008; and adhere to provisions on the payment of BAC honorarium set forth in DBM BC No. 2004-5A, Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, Section 2.79(I) of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: The Management still failed to submit the documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to maintain separate containers/ receptacles for each type of waste for temporary placement of solid waste prior to its final hauling to an open dump site, and which should be properly marked or identified as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" or "special waste”; require the collectors and other personnel directly dealing with the collection of solid waste to wear personal protective equipment and paraphernalia, such as mask, rubber boots and the like; transport solid waste using trucks/haulers with covers; and convert the open dump to a controlled dump site.
observations.managementAction: No managem.ent action
observations.reason: Still the management failed ti implement the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation: Implement minimum and standard requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collections and their final disposal to the dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The LGU still failed to implement the provisions of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended that the
Municipal Officials submit justification as to why reimbursements method
was resorted even in the absence of contingencies. Henceforth, refrain
from using the reimbursement method of payment unless there are
unforeseen events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management exhaust all
possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a
Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU, after which, the
assessed value should be recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

We have recommended that management coordinate with the DepED for
the proper turnover of the School Buildings still being maintained in the
books of the municipality. Based on the turnover documents, the municipal
accountant shall derecognize the school buildings from its books of
accounts.

We have recommended that management undertakes the following
measures:
a. Create an Inventory Committee, with the Municipal Engineer and the
Property Custodian as indispensable members, and conduct a yearly
physical count of local road network projects and consequently update
the local road map of the LGU;
b. The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road
components of various road infrastructure projects;
c. The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for
roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the
physical count at the end of each year.

 That management through the municipal officials/employees concerned
submit the lacking supporting documents to COA for evaluation.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that all financial
assistance are fully supported with the required documentation provided
under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payments are made; and
 Institutionalize a non-discretionary determination of amounts given to
beneficiaries of financial assistance by setting a predetermined amount
for each type of client through the issuance of a Sangguniang Bayan
Resolution.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Inventory Committee prioritize the completion of the physical inventory and reconciliation of all records of PPEs, including Infrastructure Assets, and prepare a reconciled RPCPPE for submission to the Auditor and recommend measures for the titling of properties;
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, (i) maintain the required PPELC, RPLC and Property Cards, respectively; (ii) provide complete and necessary information to facilitate verification and reconciliation; (iii) fast-track the reconciliation of records; and (iv) effect the required adjustments after the reconciliation of records is made; and
Municipal Accountant effect the reclassification of PPE items valued at ₱487,320.88 to proper PPE accounts and transfer the PPE items below ₱10,000.00 with book value of ₱1,110,505.53 to Inventories – Semi-expendable Supplies, Containers and Property account to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

2.7   We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) Local Chief Executive appoint a support staff in the Accounting and Treasury department who will maintain the records of taxpayers; (b) Treasurer (i) prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and submit it to the Accountant; and (ii) comply with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS on the setting up of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.
2.8   Likewise, we reiterated the prior year’s recommendation and the Management also agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) use the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by the office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year; and (b) prioritize the establishment of subsidiary ledgers for the respective accounts.

4.18 	We recommended and Management agreed that the:
LDRRM Officer include  PPAs of unexpended balances of previous years in the LDRRMFIP and submit reports to the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD)  in compliance with  COA Circular 2012-002;
Local SB members intensely review resolutions before issuance in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1; and
LCE initiate the creation of one additional position and the eventual hiring of two (2) mandatory positions for the LDRRMO in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

8.11  	We reiterated previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Barangay Officials/BSWMB to –
ensure strict implementation of waste segregation on households and non-households;
improve barangay MRF or, if possible, establish an ecological facility in every barangay; and
enforce strict compliance on the “no segregation, no collection policy” to discipline its constituents.
8.12 The Management commented that the BSWMB will improve the functionality of Barangay MRF through the continuous monitoring of the Municipal Engineering Office with the collaboration of our MLGOO. The VAC’s Clean, a group of employees under their Performance Governance System (PGS) will be helping in monitoring the monthly progress of Barangay’s MRF and the number of households practicing proper waste segregation.  The MSWMB will check the actual waste segregation of the Barangays at source to enforce compliance on “No Segregation, No Collection Policy”.  They will also provide proper training to their garbage collectors and sorters and equipped them with suitable Personal Protective Equipment (PPE).

9.8   	We recommended and Management agreed that:
The LDRRM Officer monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMP to ensure the full implementation of the planned activities; and
The Accountant refrain from charging expenses that were not enumerated in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01 to the 5% LDRRMF.

11.25 	We recommended and the Management agreed that -
	they will re-study, revisit and or re-evaluate the existing business strategies and policies relative to the operations and profitability of the BGC and Mt. Samat Zipline and Dunsulan Park so that the objectives of establishing these economic enterprises could be achieved efficiently and effectively;
	Municipal Accountant (i) record the receivables for the unpaid balances of electrical billings and maintain subsidiary ledgers per lessee; (ii) revise the accounting procedures for the recognition of income and electrical expenses; and (iii) create special accounts in the General Fund for each economic enterprise and prepare the financial reports for each enterprise/special account.
	Municipal Treasurer ensure prompt collection of electricity/water and rental payments and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and designated BGC Administrator regarding the unpaid balances; and
	Municipal Engineer fast track the review on the possibility for the subdivision of the BGC for potential increase in income generation and decrease in expenses.

12.7   	We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. 	Municipal Accountant ensure that all payments are supported with complete documentation in order to substantiate the claims and avoid suspension/disallowances in audit;
b. 	Human Resources Management Officer (HRMO) require all casual, job order and contractual employees to submit duly approved accomplishment reports and DTR’s before the preparation of payroll and payment of their wages is made; Also, conduct thorough review of the existing number of permanent, casual contractual/job order employees to determine the proper handling of work demands and the necessity of employing these non-organic personnel to limit, if not eliminate, the hiring of JOs; and
c. 	Municipal Treasurer delegate collecting function to regular and bonded employees to secure government resources.

13.6  	The Management commented that the delay on the remittance of September 2017 withholding taxes was due to the work load of the person-in-charge.  The Municipal Accountant already relayed it to her staff and made a reassignment of work to prevent further delay on remittances.
13.7   	We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, strictly ensure compliance with the remittance of taxes withheld so as to avoid the payment of interests/surcharges.

15.6  We recommended and Management agreed to:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly ensure the LGU’s compliance in the remittance of premium contributions so as to avoid the payment of interests/penalties; and
require the persons liable for the delay of remittance to refund the interest charged to the LGU of ₱6,793.96.

16.7  	The Management commented that the delay in the remittance for the months of March and April was because of the failure to meet the deadline on the online remittances of contributions because of the incomplete entries of the HR Office on the online system.  The Management was issued a certification from HDMF allowing them to remit through manual remittance awaiting enrolment on e-gov of the Development Bank of the Philippines.
16.6  	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the HDMF deadlines in the remittance of contributions so as to avoid the payment of interests and penalties.

17.8   We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Budget Officer to appropriate at least 5% of its budget for GAD; (b) direct the GFPS to administer the HGDG tests in order to aid them in the formulation of PPAs that specifically address the gender gaps/issues and concerns and work towards the attainment of their targeted outputs;  (c) require the GFPS to submit Annual GAD AR accompanied with other required reports; and (d) issue an Executive Order for the establishment of monitoring and evaluation system through the creation of Monitoring and Evaluation Team that will evaluate the results of the LGUs GAD policies, programs and projects.

18.5 	We reiterated previous year’s recommendation and Management agreed to –
	instruct all drivers, users and those in-charge of government motor vehicles to properly accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets, submit the required reports and observe all regulations pertaining to the use of government motor vehicles for proper control and monitoring thereof; and
	strengthen controls on the issuance of fuel to government vehicles to ensure that consumptions are within the prescribed or reasonable limits.

19.5   	We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE continuously take the necessary actions on the disallowances and require the continuous payment by all persons liable thru installment until the receipt of the COA approval of staggered scheme of restitution.

We recommended that OIC Municipal Accountant ensure that the claims for RATA should be properly reviewed and are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment in order to avoid suspension and /or disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended that Management
Strictly comply with Section 2.2 and Sub-sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, to ensure that the public is informed of any subsequent projects that will be implemented to foster transparency and accountability and also to encourage public participation.
Henceforth, contractors should be briefed on the need to put up the required notifications.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that all unexpended balances in the Trust Fund will be remitted back to the source national government agencies or reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund as soon as the project has been completed.
Henceforth, review of status of funds received from other government agencies be regularly performed so that appropriate actions thereon could be made.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant:
Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annex A which are already overdue; and
Henceforth, strictly comply Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.

We recommended that Management fully implement the project and immediately utilize the same to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives of RA 9003 in ensuring the sound collection, sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of waste generated in the respective barangays.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the MSWMB strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the establishment of a sanitary landfill.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the GAD Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to facilitate the establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU; and
Direct the GAD Focal Person to assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that should be implemented.

We recommended that Management:
GAD Focal Person should make extra effort to look for resource persons and skilled trainors to immediately implement the PPAs on trainings and likewise spearhead in the organization of LGBT community in the municipality; and
Henceforth, GAD Focal Person should closely monitor and supervise its implementation as required by the PCW and strictly implement all programs, projects and activities embodied in the Municipalities approved Annual Investment Plan and Budget.

We recommended that:
LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month using the format hereto attached        (Annex Q); and
Make representation with the Municipal Mayor to request for additional personnel for the Office of the LDRRM.

Recommendation:

       We  recommend  that  management  require   the   previous   Municipal
Accountant  to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the  substantiation
of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the  journal  entries
in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU.

      It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements  of  all
Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and  submitted  within
10 days from receipt of Bank Statements.  The  failure  to  submit  required
reports  may  warrant  the  withholding  of  the  salary  of  the  concerned
official.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management remind all  officials  and  employees  to
submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel  and  other  time-bound
undertakings. Also, ensure that all cash advances are  fully  liquidated  at
the end  of  each  year.  Otherwise,  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.


      Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or
refund from the former employees of the  LGU  for  their  unliquidated  cash
advances.

Recommendation:

      We recommend  that  management  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and  the  amount
due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list  must
be submitted  to  the  Municipal  Accountant  for  the  preparation  of  the
necessary accounting entries to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable  and
Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year.

      We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a  certified
list of all real property tax delinquencies which  remained  uncollected  or
unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason  or  reasons
for  such  non-collection  or  non-payment,  and  submit  the  same  to  the
sanggunian concerned on or  before  December  31  of  the  year  immediately
succeeding the year  in  which  the  delinquencies  were  incurred,  with  a
request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

Recommendation:

       We  recommended  that  Management  direct  the   previous   Municipal
Accountant  to   assist   the   current   designated   Accountant   in   the
identification of the receivables of the  municipality.  Otherwise,  provide
allowance for impairment and/or  request  write-off  of  the  balance  after
exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local
Treasurer, as the case maybe, prepares stock/property cards and that the
Municipal Accountant also maintain separate ledger cards to establish the
details of the assets.

      We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee
to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and
submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper
recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that Management strictly impose  the  bonding  requirements  of
collectors and assign back to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office  the  Revenue
Collection Clerk who was assigned at  the  Mayor’  Office  to  maximize  her
services.  Also, stop the practice of assigning the tasks of  collecting  to
personnel hired as casual / job orders.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit regularly and on  a  timely
basis all  the  Reports  of  Collections  and  Deposits  to  facilitate  the
recording in the books of accounts.  Further, consider the assignment  of  a
Liquidating Officer to assist in the receipt / review of  reports  submitted
by collectors and to hasten the report preparation  and  submission  to  the
accounting office.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer strictly  observe  the  provisions
of the Revised Cash Examination  Manual  and  deposit  all  his  collections
intact to the authorized government depository bank not later than the  next
banking day or as soon as collections reach P10,000.00.

We recommend that the Municipality comply the existing regulations for  the
establishment of the LDDRM Office and fill up the  mandatory  positions  to
perform the functions of administration & training, research & planning and
operations & warning.

We recommend that  henceforth  the  concerned  municipal  officials  adhere
strictly to the provisions of RA 7160 in the computation of  PS  limitation
to avoid excess expenditures for Personal Services.

We recommend that management submit explanation on the cause  of  the  delay
in the implementation of the CFLC project and the action  taken  to  address
the problem so that the desired purpose of the project can be attained.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure  that the 20%  Development  Fund
be utilized to fund priority programs  or  projects  which  are  capital  in
nature in accordance  with  the  provisions  of  DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommend that the  payments  by  checks  of  the  municipality  be  made
payable under the name of the business entity pursuant  to  Section  93  and
124  of  P.D.  1445  to  avoid  risk   of   possible   misuse   and   /or
misappropriation.

We recommended and Management agreed to transfer the refunds for GSIS compulsory premiums from the Trust Fund to the General Fund. Prepare the suggested adjusting entries in order to correct the errors which resulted to misstatements of the financial statements in the General and Trust Funds by P202,622.50. :

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of P549,998.00 from general fund to 20% DF account immediately in order to avoid the risk of utilizing it other than its intended purpose and to comply with Section 287 of RA 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed to transfer the deficient amount of P784,129.70 from General Fund to LDRRMF and ensure the appropriation for LDRRMF have full cash back-up to have ample fund to defray disaster-related expenses in time of emergencies.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all procurement undertaken is included and in accordance with the approved APP, including approved changes thereto. Management shall plan its procurements meticulously and prudently. We further recommend that Management shall revise its APP whenever there is procurement of necessary items not included in the Approved APP.

We recommended that Management (1) stop the disposal operations at the open dumpsite; and (2) immediately formulate a Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) in accordance to the guidelines set in Section 17 of RA 9003 and DAO No. 9 series of 2006 and submit it to EMB for approval then implement it by employing environmentally sound procedures in order to comply with RA 9003.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC, including the BAC Secretariat and the TWG, to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents. Adhere strictly with the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR, COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2012-001 on the procurement of goods, infrastructures and services.

We recommended and Management agreed to include Project Inspection Report and Statement of Time Elapsed to its punch-list of the basic essential required documents to support the claims for progress and final payments to  the contractors to ensure the quality of the cumulative value of work and in accordance with the approved plans and specifications. The Management shall strictly observe the proper and complete documentation of any claims against government funds by strengthening internal control through submission of the above mentioned documents before disbursing government funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to deduct the equivalent amount of P265,919.44 from the claims of the contractor representing the excessive computation of VAT which is at the advantage of the contractor resulting to loss of government fund.

We further recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to closely supervise the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) to ensure that correct VAT percentage is used in accordance with existing laws and rules and regulations relative to the preparation of ABCs.

We recommended and Management agreed with the conditions stated above shall be strictly complied with by the LGU. Ensure the compliance to the documentary requirements required with the guidelines and submit the same to the proper authorities.

We recommended that the LGU shall prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers to ensure that transferred funds are used only for the intended purpose and that proper accounting and reporting is made of the utilization of the fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the abovementioned contract projects be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for auditorial and technical review, so that Management shall be informed immediately of any deficiencies or flaws that may be noticed or observed thereon and such be corrected.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to speed up the completion of the construction of a central MRF in order that desirable environmental management outcomes can be enjoyed by the general public.

We recommended and Management agreed to reconcile and trace its records as to when and to whom the refund of compulsory premiums applies. Make representation with the GSIS inquiring the details of such and take appropriate actions to avoid idle funds. Utilize the fund according to its purpose and according to its account classification in conformity with existing rules and regulations. It is further recommended that efficient and effective utilization of the fund should be anchored on the promotion of the general welfare.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that an Annual Procurement Plan be prepared to plan and schedule the procurement activities of the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: That the BAC observes the required pre-procurement conference for the procurement of goods with an ABC above ₱2 million to ensure that the procurement is in accordance with the project and annual procurement plans and determine the readiness of the procurement at hand, among others; That the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat, observe the proper advertising and posting of the Invitation to Bid as prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to encourage more participants in the bidding process and intensify the competition among them, which in turn, will ensure that the LGU will get the best possible proposals, as to quality and cost, for the project sought to be procured. That the Municipality reserve a specific and prominent public place for posting of the Invitation to Bid such as bulletin board in the municipal hall; That the BAC, through the BAC Secretariat, post the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement procedures; That the one (1) year defects liability period from the project’s completion date up to the final acceptance of the projects be fully covered by the Performance Security of the contractor. Ensure that applicable warranty security against structural defects and failures are to be imposed by the contractors. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant is advised to ensure that the contractors post the warranty security covering the full amount in the first year and renew the same every year thereafter, subject to reduction every year by the amount of depreciation on a straight-line basis; and That the Municipal Engineer conduct ocular inspection of the infrastructure projects after the one (1) year defects liability period, and in the event that any of the projects manifested structural defects, oblige the contractor to undertake the necessary repair works or forfeit the corresponding warranty security.
observations.managementAction: ITBs were duly posted and advertised. Inspection of previous projects shows no need of repair works within the defects liability period. Retention monies were still released without the substitute security. Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for strict compliance with Paragraphs 3-6 of the PPSAS 17 and Sections 2 to 3 of COA Circular No. 2003-007 dated December 11, 2003. For reference, the Table of Estimated Useful Life of PPE is hereto attached as Annex G.

We recommended that Management procure drugs and medicines in accordance with the approved APP otherwise, an amendment to the original APP should be made to reflect the actual needs of the agency.

Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 in the agency’s procurement process.
We recommended that Management strictly follow DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 on the utilization of the 20% DF.

We recommended that Management include and implement PPAs in the Annual School Board Budget that are compliant with the following laws, rules and regulations: 

RA 5447 and its implementing guidelines, Letter of Instruction No. 1462, series of 1985; and

DECS-DBM-DILG No. 01, series of 1998; No.01-A dated March 14, 2000 and       No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001.

We recommended that Management follow-up the release of the remaining budget with the DA to fully implement the Vermi Culture Production and Composting Project and facilitate the utilization of the project.

We recommend that Management:

Submit to the  Audit Team the documentary requirements as required under    Section 2 of  COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review ; and

Henceforth, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to League of Municipalities  prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor, as the case maybe.

We recommended that Management through its SWMB develop/formulate a SWM Manual of Procedures and/or Implementing Rules and Procedures on SWM to properly monitor and review its SWM programs and activities.

We recommend that management require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU. It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted within 10 days from receipt of Bank Statements. The non-submission of the required reports may warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016 Bank Reconciliation Statements were prepared and submitted to COA. However, there are still reconciling items carried over from previous years. (Reiterated)
observations.reason: Documents to support the preparation of the necessary journal entries were not available/furnished to the current Accountant. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the appropriate asset for supplies, equipment and materials purchased out of the trust fund in order to recognize the asset funded by other government agency for use by the LGU. Upon full liquidation to the grantor, the assets should be closed to the liability account in the Trust Fund and transferred to the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Disbursements for CY 2016 and onwards of completed projects have been transferred and recorded in the General Fund. Cost of the BEA projects were adjusted and carried as assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year. We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016 The Treasurer furnished a soft copy of the list of taxpayers with the assessed value of the real properties.
observations.reason: No RPT Receivable was set up in the beginning of the year for CY 2013-2017. The list provided by the Treasurer has no breakdown as to the amount collectible for the current year and prior years. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2016 Demand letters were issued to officers and employees. The Municipal Accountant enforces strictly the prescribed rules and regulations in handling of cash advances and the rendering of accounts.
observations.reason: Several employees were no longer connected with the LGU. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current designated Accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.
observations.reason: Old accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. Also, require the MT/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant, which shall be used as basis in recording issuance. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, or GSO as the case may be, conduct physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies and the Municipal accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2015, 2016 Starting CY 2018, purchase of items will be recorded as inventories and recognize expense only upon issuance. Management will also conduct semestral physical inventory on supplies.
observations.reason: Inaction by management (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant support the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts with subsidiary ledgers to establish the details of the assets. We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive issue an office order creating an Inventory Committee and require them to conduct a complete physical count of the property, plant and equipment of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016 Physical count was conducted in CY 2017 but was not completed and reconciled with recorded balances.
observations.reason: Time constraints

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adopt the 5% Residual Value for Property, Plant and Equipment if a more appropriate percentage is not yet determined. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant re-compute the depreciation as well as the accumulated depreciation in the preceding year and effect the necessary adjustments to have a fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 A journal entry was prepared to adjust and correct the amount of the depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to furnish a copy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan to the OIC Municipal Accountant for the recording/adjustment of the unexpended CYs 2011-2017 Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE as Trust Liability in the Trust Fund, and to the Budget Officer for the recording of the DRRMF-Capital Outlay as continuing appropriation in the SAAOB. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer issue check to actually set aside and transfer to the Trust Fund the unexpended LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE) for CY 2011-2017.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The unexpended Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE were transferred as trust liability in the trust fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the contractors to correct and repair the defects observed in the projects, otherwise forfeit the performance security. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and deduct liquidated damages on the final billing of the projects pursuant to Section 68 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.reason: No management action; No disbursements were made since claims for the final billing were not yet submitted. (Reiterated as delayed implementation)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Property Custodian or the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS and request for computation to ensure that properties are adequately covered with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 On-going inventory of all insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management explain why the projects were provided in generic terms and do not contain sufficient details and cost of the each project. Henceforth, require from the Local Development Council a list of duly identified projects in the formulation of future annual budgets.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The Local Development Council identified the projects in the formulation of budgets.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implements the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality and allocate funds for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in the LGU pursuant to RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015, 2016 SWM Plan was approved by SB in December 2016. Appropriation was provided in CY 2017 budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit written justification on giving 13th month pay and bonuses to personnel under job order and contract of service. We also recommend the immediate discontinuance on the grant of the aforesaid benefits.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Discontinued the practice of granting the said benefits to job order and contractual workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Discontinued the practice of releasing financial aid.
observations.reason: Non-submission of legal basis on the financial aid and the justification on the relevance of the activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: Require the VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP meeting. Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer to not accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 For further inquiry to VMLP.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department Circular 001-2015.
observations.reason: To draft a letter to request approval from BLGF to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Quarterly report is prepared but not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management allocates at least 5% of the annual budget for Gender and Development. We also recommend that management submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) for review and endorsement pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01 of the Philippine Commission on Women, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012, 2016 A GAD Plan/Program was developed to address gender issues within the concerned sectors.
observations.reason: For CY 2014, the LGU provided an allocation of only 3% of the total budget. For CY 2015, the GAD Plan includes funds provided by other government agencies. For CY 2016, the GAD Budget is only 1% of the total annual budget. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize the asset as Construction in Progress and record back the liability account. Upon submission of full liquidation to the grantor, the assets in the Trust Fund be closed to equity or Due to LGUs and transferred to the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity. We further recommend that succeeding expenditures in the Trust Fund be made in accordance with Sections 97 and 104 of the NGAS Volume 1, to avoid misstatements in the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 CY 2017 transactions were recorded in accordance with Sections 97 and 103 of the NGAS Vol. 1.
observations.reason: Need further verification from MDO to reflect the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the immediate transfer of all completed property, plant and equipment to the General Fund upon liquidation to the grantor and discontinue the practice of recognizing depreciation expense in the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Two projects, CICL and road, were transferred to General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 UCPB accounts were closed and transferred to DBP.
observations.reason: The LGU still maintains two (2) bank accounts with FCB, with year-end balance of more than P500,000.00/account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184. We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties to dispel presumptions of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015, 2016 The Bids and Awards Committee follows the provisions in RA 9184 throughout the procurement process.
observations.reason: Contracts are still awarded to lone bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require coordination between the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in the identification of all completed public infrastructure projects based on the transfers recorded by the Municipal Accountant. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the registries of public infrastructures in preparation of the five-year transitional application for the recognition of the Public Infrastructures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) application.
observations.reason: Management had difficulty in tracing projects since the currently designated Accountant has no data of the Public Infrastructure from the very beginning.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to fast track the transmittal of source documents to the Accounting Unit. Also, require the Accountant to submit monthly trial balances to the Auditor on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014, 2015 Quarterly reports were submitted but not within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Needs time to trace the trust fund transaction to effect the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P 100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 No fund transfer was made in CY 2015-2017. Instead of liquidation reports, the financial statements of the CHDI were submitted.
observations.reason: The CHDI did not submit liquidation reports. Fund transfer from member-LGU was included in the general budget of the CHDI.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis, except petty expenses to be paid out of the Petty Cash Fund. We further recommend that management use the alternative methods of procurement provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184, if public bidding is not feasible.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Management discontinued the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant-designate prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013. It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2014 and its ensuing annual reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Submitted the AAPSI to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Delayed preparation and submission of the AAPSI

observations.recommendation: Issue demand letters to all concerned parties, including retired/transferred employees and employees of national agencies, to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately. For officers and employees who are still connected with the LGU, cause the withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445. Request authority to write-off long outstanding cash advances which are deemed uncollectible and dormant, as provided in COA Resolution No. 80-122 dated April 23, 1980. Advise the Municipal Accountant to enforce strictly the prescribed rules and regulations in the handling of cash advances and the rendering of accounts. It is further recommended that a journal entry be made to adjust the credit balances of the officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Demand letters were issued to officers and employees who are still connected with the LGU. There is still an on-going payroll deduction for those employees with outstanding cash advances. To determine if the long outstanding cash advances which are deemed uncollectible and dormant will qualify to be written off. The Municipal Accountant enforces strictly the prescribed rules and regulations in handling of cash advances and the rendering of accounts. A journal entry has been made to adjust the credit balances of the officers and employees.
observations.reason: Several employees were no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant summarize all payrolls attributable to the constructed assets in the current year and prepare adjusting entries to capitalize these labor costs. Previous years’ projects should be traced and adjusted accordingly.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The Accounting Office capitalized labor costs attributable to the construction of assets of the transaction for the CY 2014 onwards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the appropriate asset account for the supplies, equipment and materials purchased out of the trust fund in order to recognize the asset funded by other government agency for use by the LGU. Upon liquidation to the grantor, the assets in the Trust Fund should be removed in the Trust Fund books and transfer them in the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity. We further recommend that succeeding expenditures in the Trust Fund be made in accordance with Sections 97 and 104 of the NGAS, Volume 1 to avoid misstatements in the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 CY 2017 transactions were recorded in accordance with Sections 97 and 103 of the NGAS Vol. 1.
observations.reason: The designated accountant is just following the procedure of the previous Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly and facilitate liquidation of fund transfers from source agency specially those long outstanding/ dormant accounts. It is further recommended that adjusting entries be made on errors and misclassification discussed in the foregoing paragraphs. The municipal accountant should also monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2015 Submitted liquidation on current fund transfers.
observations.reason: Absence of sufficient records

observations.recommendation: We recommend: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all collectors remit their collections intact daily to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Sections 15 and 29 of the New Government Accounting System Manual, in order to preclude the accumulation of unremitted collections by the collectors. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the use of the accountable forms strictly by the person to whom it was issued. Transfer the collector of water fees under the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for the proper monitoring of his collections and prompt capture of the collection data.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012, 2016 All collectors are directed to remit their collections daily. The Municipal Treasurer strictly monitors the Accountable Forms. Also an AF officers designated is the one in-charge in approving of AF requested by the requesting officer. Transfer the collector of water fees under the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for the proper monitoring of his collections and prompt capture of the collection data. Collection of water fees is already in the MTO.
observations.reason: There are still instances of delayed remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to speed up the titling of these lands in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 With the Land Titling Program of the DENR, the Municipal Assessor requested for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents. The Municipal Assessor expressed that the land donated wherein the municipal hall was constructed already had a court order but was not implemented. She already asked assistance from the Sangguniang Bayan regarding that matter, but none was done to enforce the order. Also, the lots to be transferred to the Philippine National Police, Bureau of Fire and Rural Health Unit cannot be transferred due to the problem of the mother lot.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to replenish the General Fund with the amount of the SEF checks which were not cleared/re-deposited amounting to P53,469.00. In the meantime, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry setting up the receivable (Due from Other Funds) and payable accounts (Due to Other Funds) in the General Fund and Special Education Fund respectively. Stop the practice of encashing checks out of its collections. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare regularly the Bank Reconciliation Statements. Provide this Office copies of the pertinent documents relating to the re-deposit of P52,687.00 such as validated deposit slip and bank statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 The adjusted beginning balance of FCB-SEF has been established. However, other bank accounts were not. Monthly transactions were reconciled for all bank accounts. The adjusted beginning balance of FCB-SEF has been established. However, other bank accounts were not. Monthly transactions were reconciled for all bank accounts. The adjusted beginning balance of FCB-SEF has been established. However, other bank accounts were not.
observations.reason: As per Accountant, the check was already cleared by the bank but taken up twice as deposit, causing the understatement of the Cash in Vault.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly. The Municipal Treasurer to see to it that funds are really available when he signs the certification. The Municipal Treasurer to see to it that funds are really available when he signs the certification. The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Monthly transactions were reconciled for all bank accounts. The adjusted beginning balance of FCB-SEF has been established. However, other bank accounts were not.
observations.reason: Beginning balances were not reconciled since these were carried over from previous officers.

observations.recommendation: Stop from involving any employee in the collecting activities of the local government unit if he/she is not regularly appointed and officially designated agency personnel. Require all accountable officers to apply for the proper amount of fidelity bond in relation to the handled cash accountabilities so as to safeguard government funds and protect the interest of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Regular accountable officers are bonded.
observations.reason: Some job order employees are still allowed to perform the collecting functions but under the supervision of a bonded employee.

observations.recommendation: Conduct immediate follow-ups to facilitate the issuances of title in its name, otherwise, cancel the transaction and request for the return of the payment from the lot owner. Request for an SB Resolution confirming the purchase made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 The Municipal Assessor has requested the DENR for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.
observations.reason: No appropriation for the processing of documents.

observations.recommendation: To immediately prepare adjusting entries to show the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan accounts. In the succeeding amortization payments, review amounts in the billing statements before effecting payments and see to it that there is proper recording of the charges made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Needs further verification from MDFO to reflect the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Property/Supply Officer and the LGU Inspector to henceforth, accomplish the Pre and Post     Inspection Reports to establish basis to determine the extent of repair to be done and to evaluate the attainment of the purpose of the repair performed on the Municipality’s equipment/property.
observations.managementAction: The Pre   and Post  Inspection Reports   were  already  accomplish ed by the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Engineer comply the provisions of Circular No. 0213-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly  reports of on-going projects were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to make representation to the Provincial Treasurer to confirm the validity and existence of the amount deposited and request available records as basis for the action to be taken by the agency in connection with the said deposit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had made representa tion to the Provincial Treasurer inquiring the status of the amount and was informed that based on the records of the Provincial Accounting Office and the Cash Divisions of the Provincial Treasurer’ s Office, the account does not exist.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the undersigned the list of ongoing projects/programs/activities for the 4th quarter of CY 2013.
observations.managementAction: Notificati ons for the various projects were prepared and quarterly reports of on-going projects were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: 1. Require the Accountant to book up the receivable of P264,201.00 to correct the understatement of income account for waterworks. 2. Intensify collection of receivable by requiring the collectors to meet certain targets. 3. Require the collectors to submit monthly report of receivables to be used as basis for the recording of receivables in the books. 4. Review and investigate causes for the non collection of monthly dues and adopt measures to correct them. 5. Enforce disconnection of delinquent consumers if warranted as provided in the Municipal Ordinance 2013-05. 6. Ensure safekeeping of the subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The amount of collection for waterworks were updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LDRRMO submit the required monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to the COA Auditor within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports on sources and utilizatio n of the LDRRMF was submitted to DBM and COA

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO to adhere strictly to the provisions of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015 in authorizing overtime services and granting of overtime pay. Further, require the officials who authorized the overtime including the concerned drivers to refund the amount of overtime pay.
observations.managementAction: The amount of overtime pay granted to two drivers was already refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor intensify the implementation of the projects funded by financial assistance in order to timely achieve the desired benefits. Also make representation with the respective donors of the financial assistance with unutilized balances and seek authority to use the fund balance to finance other projects/programs of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Some of the projects were already implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that contract documents for all procurement of infrastructure projects contain all the general and special conditions required of a contract and to provide protection to the rights of the LGU when a matter relating to the poor or non-performance by the contractor occurs. Further, that the municipality pursue forfeiture of performance bond in favor of the LGU in case the contractor falls short in performing its contractual obligations to the LGU for the construction of these projects.
observations.managementAction: Management had a meeting with the contractor and agreed that liquidated damages be imposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Sangguniang Bayan impose and implement the required taxes, fees and permits as mandated in the Revised Revenue Code and whenever applicable, consider the revision of the said code in order to impose rates which are responsive to the present economic situation considering that the same was enacted way back in CY 2006.
observations.managementAction: The taxes, fees and other permits will be collected as soon as the Local Revenue Code is revised.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend that management should review and evaluate the list of KKK Fund loan grantees in the attached Annex A if it is still valid and collection is still possible. If found to be futile, request for the write off of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Request for Write Off was made for the KKK loans which cannot be collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to reconcile the result of the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments thereof, preferably before the year ends so as not to reiterate this observation. Further, cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers / cards which shall be kept for each class of PPE to record the description, acquisition, transfer/disposal and other information about the asset.
observations.managementAction: Physical count of PPE was conducted but was not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality strictly observe the pertinent provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG-DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects and ensure that said fund is optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: The 20 % Development Fund was not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 7160 in the computation of PS limitation to avoid excess expenditures for Personal Services.
observations.managementAction: Management is still implementin g the salary standardiza tion law.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections in her possession to the authorized government depository bank to safeguard government funds from risks of loss.
observations.managementAction: Collections were deposited but not regularly and intactly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to adopt measures to strengthen the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan. Require the Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation of the merchandise inventory recorded in the books as against the actual and ensure the proper recording of the transactions / the receipt and disposition of the funds as well as the preparation of the periodic report on the financial status of the economic enterprise.
The Municipal Treasurer should strictly follow the provisions of Sections 174 and 258 of R.A 7160 to enforce the collection of the delinquent real property tax receivables totaling P1,071,895.86.

The Municipal Accountant should record cash advances for Intelligence Fund under the Due From Officers and Employees (account 123) pursuant to the above-cited provision. Likewise, monitor the liquidation of cash advances through constant follow-ups so that these could be immediately recorded in the agency books. Lastly, furnish the COA Auditor with a copy of all transmittal letters as required under Section 6 of COA Circular No. 2003-003.
observations.managementAction: The liquidation reports for the cash advances for intelligence fund were already submitted to COA Central Office. However, as of this date, the agency has not received copy of the credit notice as basis in dropping from the books.  To properly monitor the account and in conformity with the PPSAS, it is recommended that it will be reclassified to Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account. As of December 31, 2016, total cash advance was P1,060,965.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to designate an employee who shall be responsible to facilitate the LTO registration and insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality. Apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of disbursing government funds without valid and proper supporting documents. Further, Management shall utilize first the proceeds from sale of bid documents and other related fees collected in the payment of honoraria to the members of the BAC, Technical Working Group and BAC Secretariat before sourcing out from the savings of the Municipality since honoraria are not considered mandatory expenditure items which will be given priority in the use of savings from Personal Services of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2016 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the total amount of P3,725,661.17in accordance with Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Item 6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013. 

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically the guidelines on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF to ascertain that the financial information on LDRRMF are taken up appropriately in the books and properly disclosed in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality opened a Special Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017 the Municipal Treasurer transferred P2,495,861.17 only to the Special Trust fund account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop the practice of charging against the 20% development fund the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures; refrain from charging against the fund the expenditures which do not have corresponding appropriation to comply with Section 4.1 of P.D. observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the accountable officers to immediately submit to the Accounting Office liquidation of their cash advances.  Failure to comply may cause the filing of appropriate charges against the accountable officer pursuant to Sections 9.3.2 and 9.3.3 of the above-mentioned COA Circular; prohibit the granting of additional cash advance to the accountable officer if his/her previous accountabilities had not been liquidated or a proper accounting thereof had been made; remind the accountable officers to always settle their cash advances within the required period or at the end of each year; and strictly observe the rules, laws and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Liquidations were already submitted to the Office of the Accountant and a memorandum was issued requiring the Accountable Officers to settle their obligations.
observations.reason: There are still unliquidated cash advances granted from previous years.

No. 1445 in case losses of government money and/or property occur.

Failure to comply may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; instruct the Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks; and adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain general and subsidiary ledgers for the financial accounts of the LGU. These ledgers shall be the source of information to prepare schedules to facilitate verification of the accounts in the financial statements. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4.8, 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to appoint or designate a GSO who shall specifically take custody and be accountable for all the properties, real or personal, of the LGU and to prepare and maintain stock cards and property cards for all its inventories and PPEs; create an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No management action

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accountant to revert the accounting entry she made by debiting Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and crediting Due to NGAs account for the subject amount. Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume 1 in dropping from the books the accounts subject for request for relief.
observations.managementAction: No management action

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to inform the SB to execute a resolution covering the payments of wages for JOs; remind the HRMO that service contracts provided to the parties shall always be acknowledged by the concerned recipients; instruct the concerned department heads to carefully review and sign the DTRs of the subordinates, specially of the JOs, before corresponding computation of wages shall be made thereto; advise the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the JOs’ performance specially on the collections of revenues. Require the latter to remit all its collections to the former on a daily basis; and adhere faithfully to the prevailing laws, rules and regulations in the payment of wages to JOs.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: Recommendations were still not implemented by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to immediately file before the Office of the Auditor a request for relief from property accountability for the death of 27 cattle with the available supporting evidence as enumerated in COA Memorandum Circular No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992. The matter shall be resolved in accordance with the provisions on request for relief stated in the 2009 COA Revised Rules of Procedures. This shall also be the basis of dropping from the books the cost or value of the animals; and adhere strictly to Section 73(1) of P.D. No. 1445 in case losses of government money and/or property occur.
observations.managementAction: No management action yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the attached schedule. All claims shall always be supported with complete documentation; faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions of any government agency. Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the above-noted irregularities. Failure to comply may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and ensure that all financial transactions shall be recorded in accordance with the prevailing accounting standards; instruct the Accountant to make adjustments in the books which pertain to the erroneous entries made, as follows: 1. Due from Officers/Employees 45,477.56 Cash in Bank – LCCA 45,477.56 (to correct the erroneous entry made by recognizing the receivable amount) 2. Prior Period Adjustments 148,692.44 Due from Officers/Employees 9,673.78 Cash in Bank – LCCA 9,673.78 Advances to Officers/Employees 168,040.00 (to correct the erroneous entry made by recognizing prior period adjustments & receivables) 3. Due from Officers/Employees 8,170.00 Prior Period Adjustments 8,170.00 (to correct the erroneous entry made by recognizing the receivable amount).
observations.managementAction: No management action
