observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to conduct and finish annual physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Property Custodian - Designate to maintain and update Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No Inventory taking conducted as of December 31, 2015 and 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to reclassify the current portion or the balance of Long Term Liabilities account which is due for settlement within twelve months after the reporting period into Current Liabilities amounting to P2,550,268.38 from Long Term Liabilities; and adhere to the provisions of PAS No. 1 on proper presentation of financial transactions and accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: No reclassification made yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to prepare trip tickets in every use of the motor transportation; submit to the Office of the Auditor the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption within the first ten (10) days of the succeeding month; and adhere faithfully the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: No Management action
observations.reason: No Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team; instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents before affixing his signature in the disbursement voucher; require the Accounting Department to make sure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments of honorarium in compliance with the provision of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008; and adhere to provisions on the payment of BAC honorarium set forth in DBM BC No. 2004-5A, Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, Section 2.79(I) of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: The Management still failed to submit the documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to maintain separate containers/receptacles for each type of waste for temporary placement of solid waste prior to its final hauling to an open dump site, and which should be properly marked or identified as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" or "special waste”; require the collectors and other personnel directly dealing with the collection of solid waste to wear personal protective equipment and paraphernalia, such as mask, rubber boots and the like; transport solid waste using trucks/haulers with covers; and convert the open dump to a controlled dump site.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: Still the management failed to implement the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation: Implement minimum and standard requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collections and their final disposal to the dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. We have also
recommended that management reconcile the discrepancies noted on the PPE
accounts between the Subsidiary Ledgers and the Financial Statements.
Furthermore, it is advised that the School Buildings be turned over to the DepEd
and the recorded amounts be correspondingly deleted from the books of the LGU.

2.8 We have recommended that the inventory committee conduct physical count of
inventory every semester of the year, and prepare and submit the Report of
Physical Count of Inventories on July 31 and January 31 of each year. Maintain
Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards for the effective handling/control of
inventories to ensure fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements.

3.7 In view of the foregoing audit observations, we have recommended the following:
 That management furnish COA with a copy of any relevant record containing
sufficient information about the foregoing receivable balances and exert effort in
the collection of said receivables. In the absence of such documents, a request
for authority to write-off together with a valid justification may be considered in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005;
 That the breakdown of the beginning balance be prepared and submitted to
support the balances of the Due to Officers and Employees account presented in
the Financial Statements. Since claimants of terminal leave benefits are no
longer connected with the agency, the Accountant is advised to reclassify the
benefits accruing to them to the Other Payables account;
 That the amounts set up for Due to Officers and Employees be supported with a
copy of duly accomplished disbursement voucher with complete supporting
documents to prove that the claim is valid;
 That the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the
necessary disbursement voucher and effect payment/return of the unused
portion of funds received to the source agency or request from the agency
concerned for authority to use the remaining balance of the account Due to
NGAs for other purposes. Meantime, determine the nature of the amount
₱147,309.65 of the Due to NGAs account;
 That schedules to support the beginning balance of Due to LGUs account be
prepared and submitted. Prepare the necessary disbursement voucher and
effect payment/return the unused portion of funds received from the Provincial
Government of Abra amounting to ₱139,637.17;
 That management furnish the COA with a copy of any relevant record
containing sufficient information to support the balance of the Other Payables
account.

4.10 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate submit the
basis in setting up the 2016 RPT/SET Receivable as well as the schedule to support
the RPT/SET Receivable and the corresponding Deferred Credits account
balances as of December 31, 2016. The Accountant is likewise advised to review
and analyze the nature of the discrepancy of the above-mentioned accounts and
effect necessary adjustments in order to present fairly the financial statements.

4.11 We have also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain/update
Taxpayer’s Index Card to keep track of the yearly collection of real property tax
and adopt remedies to improve its collection. The Municipal Accountant and
Treasurer be instructed to coordinate with one another to attain proper
accounting of RPT. A copy of Report of Delinquent Taxpayers with their
corresponding dues as of December 31, 2016 shall also be maintained and
submitted to serve as basis in sending notice/bill to enforce collection as well as to
support the year end balances of accounts.

5.8 We have recommended that all financial accounts and reports be submitted to the
Commission on Audit every 10th day of the following month. Likewise, the
Municipal Mayor is advised to take a look into the possibility of assigning
additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate submission
of accounts.

6.7 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
 That management justify the noted lapses;
 That management instructs the municipal officials/employees concerned to submit
the lacking supporting documents to COA for evaluation. Henceforth, the
Municipal Accountant is advised to ensure that all financial assistance, honoraria
and terminal leave benefits are fully supported with the required documentation
provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payments are made;
 Institutionalize a non-discretionary determination of amounts given to beneficiaries
of financial assistance by setting a predetermined amount for each type of client
through the issuance of a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or Executive Order; and
 That management submit the authority to claim honorarium to justify the payment
thereof. Henceforth, honoraria should be granted only to officials/employees/
personnel under the above stated circumstances.

7.11 We have recommended that the Municipal Officials refrain from using the
reimbursement method of payment unless there are unforeseen events which will
justify the immediate payment thereof.

8.4 We have recommended that management furnish the Auditor copy of the purchase
orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance
of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished
supporting documents for technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2009-001.

9.5 We have recommended that an Annual Procurement Plan be prepared to plan and
schedule the procurement activities of the Municipality in accordance with the
provisions of RA 9184.

10.12 In view of the foregoing audit observations, we have recommended the following:
 That management justifies the noted lapses.
 That the BAC observes the required pre-procurement conference for the
procurement of goods with an ABC above ₱2 million to ensure that the
procurement is in accordance with the project and annual procurement plans
and determine the readiness of the procurement at hand, among others;
 That the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat, observe the proper advertising
and posting of the Invitation to Bid as prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA
9184 to encourage more participants in the bidding process and intensify the
competition among them, which in turn, will ensure that the LGU will get the
best possible proposals, as to quality and cost, for the project sought to be
procured. That the Municipality reserve a specific and prominent public place
for posting of the Invitation to Bid such as bulletin board in the municipal
hall;
 That the BAC, through the BAC Secretariat, post the Notice of Award and
Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS to promote transparency and efficiency in
the conduct of procurement procedures;
 That the one (1) year defects liability period from the project’s completion
date up to the final acceptance of the projects be fully covered by the
Performance Security of the contractor. Ensure that applicable warranty
security against structural defects and failures are to be imposed by the
contractors. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant is advised to ensure that the
contractors post the warranty security covering the full amount in the first
year and renew the same every year thereafter, subject to reduction every
year by the amount of depreciation on a straight-line basis;
 That the Municipal Engineer conduct ocular inspection of the infrastructure
projects after the one (1) year defects liability period, and in the event that
any of the projects manifested structural defects, oblige the contractor to
undertake the necessary repair works or forfeit the corresponding warranty
security; and
 Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by COA
Circular No. 2013-004 and the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

1.6	We reiterated that the:
Municipal Accountant:
submit to the Audit Team Leader the confidential fund DVs along with the complete supporting documents within seven days from the release of the check on a sealed envelope and properly labeled as “ Confidential – for COA ATL only”;
provide certification stamped on the DVs that the previous cash advance for the same purpose was already liquidated and proper accounting thereof has been made prior to the granting of the cash advance; and
Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the guidelines under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular 2015-01 on the granting, recording and reporting of confidential fund.

2.5	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SEF tax receivable in the succeeding year;
require the Accountant to –
use the Certified List of taxpayers prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
prioritize the establishment of subsidiary ledgers for the respective accounts.
The Management commented that both the Treasurer’s Office and the Accounting office have undertaken to coordinate with each other for the determination of the appropriate basis for the setting-up of RPT/SEF Tax receivables at the beginning of the year, based on the Report of Assessments coming from the Assessor’s Office.  However, the updating of posting of RPT payments and the establishment of subsidiary ledgers for each taxpayer is hampered by the inadequacy of manpower.  They added that nevertheless, the forthcoming full implementation of the Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) may aid in the correction of the deficiencies.

3.10	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify/determine the causes of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement; and (b) perform a regular reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts for the early detection and correction of any error.
The Management commented that the Accounting Office had exerted best efforts to reconcile the balances of Intra-Agency Accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds effective CY 2017.

4.8	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of General Services Officer to:
maintain the required Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) and Property Cards, respectively;
reconcile the deficiencies noted by the Audit Team in the records maintained by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor and effect the necessary actions;
ensure that all PPE items covered with Memorandum Receipt for Equipment (MR) were included in the RPCPPE and Inventory Report of the Accounting Office. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to use Acknowledgment receipt for Equipment (ARE) in issuing the property to end-users, and ensure that AREs were renewed every January of the third year after issue; and
Municipal Accountant to effect the reclassification of Furniture and Fixtures amounting to ₱536,440.00 to Office Equipment to fairly present the financial statements.
4.9	The Management commented that efforts are being exerted to update the ledger cards and the Accounting Office has already coordinated with the Assessor’s Office for the annual reconciliation of their records.

7.13	We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Local Development Council (i) ensure that the mandatory Development Fund appropriated out of the Internal Revenue Allotment be optimally utilized to implement prioritized identified programs, projects and activities to help achieve the desirable outcome on socio-economic development and environmental Management outlined in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1; and (ii) come up with a better plan in identifying priority programs and projects with its corresponding full implementation for the immediate benefits that could be derived therefrom;
LCE (i) install regular monitoring mechanism to assessing the progress of projects implementation; and (ii) ensure that a closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring is enhanced to achieve the desired level of efficiency; and
Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer ensure compliance with the previous year’s recommendations.
7.14	The Management commits to conduct review of the 2017 allocations of its 20% Development Fund and if warranted, shall immediately undertake a revision of its 2017 AIP.

9.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate with the SB members for the revisit/review/revision of the established ordinances in line with the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-01; (b) ensure the strict implementation of the established ordinances for the operations and franchising of tricycles for hire; and (c) require traffic enforcers to maintain record of apprehensions of traffic violators to record names and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office a logbook to record the related corresponding fines for validation purposes.

11.10	We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require Public School officials to submit their PPMPs for their requirements of supplies, uniforms, meals and equipment and all needed repairs for the whole year which shall be consolidated to come up with the LGU’s Annual Procurement Program. The APP shall then serve as basis in scheduling and planning for the procurement activity of the agency to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained and supplies are available when needed.
The Management commented that they have called the attention of the DepEd Supervisor, on which they have committed to undertake to coordinate with the Municipality for the submission of their consolidated PPMP for its inclusion with the LGU’s APP.

12.13	We recommended and Management agreed that the:
BAC strictly –
exercise extra diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities in the procurement activities; and
ensure compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its IRR and evaluate thoroughly the technical, financial and legal qualification of the prospective bidders for the implementation of its PPAs.
concerned procurement personnel attend more trainings and seminar that will deeply enhance their knowledge about procurement process;
Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before affixing her signature on the disbursement vouchers; and
Office of the Municipal Treasurer fill out the data needed, not just stamp PAID all the disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents.
The Management commented that the LCE had called the attention of the offices concerned and instructed its personnel to strictly adhere with the recommendations of the Audit Team.  The Management also commented that they admit committing certain lapses during the procurement process and are grateful for the information provided by the Audit Team as they could have continued making the same mistakes had they not been made aware of it.  Also the Office of the Municipal Treasurer had undertaken that all data needed in the stamp mark PAID shall be supplied in all disbursement vouchers, in order to avoid possibility of its being confused to similar documents for future transactions.

13.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Management to submit the approved copy of the contracts and the documents forming part thereof within the prescribed period as required under item 3.1 of the COA Circular No. 2009-001;
Municipal Engineering Office to –
Submit the pertinent documents required under Item 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-001 for the extra work order and extension of contract time thereof;
follow the minimum test requirements governing items of work following  the DPWH Standard Specifications; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all DVs are supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before making any payment, for proper control and prevent improper or fraudulent use of government funds and provide reasonable basis for establishing the validity of claims.
The Management undertakes ensure compliance with DPWH Standard Specifications on the performance of the minimum required testing of materials and instructions has been given to the Accounting Office to prepare an updated checklist of every disbursement and re-orient all concerned departments in the preparation and submission of required documents.

14.5	We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Local Chief Executive –
reconstitute and install additional members of the GFPS who are   representatives from the private sector and Women’s organization;
if possible, designate an alternate Chair and Vice Chair for the GFPS   in case of absence and voluminous tasked to attend to;
designate the GFPS-TWG Chair and properly identify its members duly endorsed by their supervisors who will catalyze, coordinate, provide direction and monitor the programs and projects on gender and development concerns within the agency and its clientele;
require employees to undertake orientation and capability  development on GAD including GAD – related laws and commitments; and
establish the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation (ME) Team for proper evaluation of GAD PPAs.
Reconstituted GFPS and TWG –
develop or gather GAD information such as gender statistics and sex- disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; and
strictly comply with the timely submission of GPB and support the submission of GAD-AR with the required attachment in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.
14.6	The Management commented that a meeting spearheaded by the LCE was held on January 30, 2017, on which they reviewed the existing composition of their GFPS bodies and they are in the process of revalidating the qualifications of the present members, with the possibility of appointing additional members to further strengthen their operational capability.  They also added that the Management is inclined to incorporate the functions of a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team into the duties of their existing IAS Team, due to manpower constraints.  However, they are still assessing the viability since the team members may be unduly overburdened and may not be able to provide optimal performance output.

15.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to (a)  instruct all drivers, users and those in-charge of government motor vehicles to properly accomplish all Driver’s Trip Tickets, submit the required reports and observe all regulations pertaining to the use of government motor vehicles for proper control and monitoring thereof; and (b) strengthen controls on the issuance of fuel to government vehicles to ensure that consumptions are within the prescribed or reasonable limits.
15.6	The Management commented that the Accounting office had undertaken to coordinate with the personnel in-charge for the preparation of monthly Report of Official Travel and re-orient the drivers with the proper accomplishment of the duly approved driver’s trip ticket.

16.10	We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Local Chief Executive assign a monitoring committee which will ensure compliance with the full disclosure policy requirements specifically in the period and modalities of posting the required financial documents in at least three conspicuous places of the Municipality and in the Full Disclosure Policy Portal pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-140;
Concerned department heads implementing the PPAs, update the monitoring committee of the posting of required documents for the timely information and awareness to the citizenry; and
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer
(i)	provide Report on Government PPAs covering both infrastructure and non-infrastructure projects and comply strictly with COA Circular No. 2013-004 and inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the project or before the start of the implementation of the PPA that the appropriate project signboard and/or public notices were already posted at the project sites;
(ii) ensure that all the required information in infrastructures projects are included in the tarpaulin signboards; and
(iii)	 submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year are for audit and validation.
The Management commented that they have adopted the recommendations of the Audit Team and are devising a scheme to incorporate an appropriate monitoring scheme, within the operations of their revised Internal Audit System (IAS) team.

19.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of remitting contribution in excess of the amount required as share of the employer.
19.6	The Management commented that the excess in the calculated mandatory contributions has been stopped effective January 2017.

20.7	We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE take the necessary actions on the disallowances and require all persons responsible and liable for the disallowances with COE to immediately settle the same to avoid further action on the matter.
20.8	The Management commented that they will reiterate their previous request with the City Government of Balanga for the immediate restitution of the disallowed allowances received for the double payment of RATA.  On the other hand, the rest of disallowances and charges with COE’s, representations shall be made with the COA Regional Office for the possibility of a staggered scheme of restitution.

22.8	We reiterated previous years’ recommendations that the –
Municipal Accountant prepare separate financial statements for special accounts to determine its net worth and income capacity;
Municipal Treasurer, ensure the purchase of safe deposit box, preferably vault in order to secure funds, cashbooks/records and accountable forms with the combination known only to the AO and store it in a safe and protected room;
the LCE, thru the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer,
hire or provide additional workforce/staff that has the proficiency in performing the required tasks in the Treasury and Accounting Departments; and
ensure posting of safety precautions for the information of the riders/visitors.
The Management commented that the Municipal Treasurer has given instructions to the assigned collectors at the Zipline and Dunsulan Park for the regular reporting on the status of daily collections and the accumulated collections shall be picked up from the site by treasury personnel for remittance to the Treasurer’s Office.

We recommended for strict compliance with Paragraphs 3-6 of the PPSAS 17 and Sections 2 to 3 of COA Circular No. 2003-007 dated December 11, 2003. For reference, the Table of Estimated Useful Life of PPE is hereto attached as Annex G.

We recommended the following:
Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank;
Make a representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant:
Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annex H which are already overdue; and
Henceforth, strictly comply Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review.

We recommended that Management procure drugs and medicines in accordance with the approved APP otherwise, an amendment to the original APP should be made to reflect the actual needs of the agency.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 in the agency’s procurement process.
We recommended that Management strictly follow DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 on the utilization of the 20% DF.

We recommended that Management include and implement PPAs in the Annual School Board Budget that are compliant with the following laws, rules and regulations:
RA 5447 and its implementing guidelines, Letter of Instruction No. 1462, series of 1985; and
DECS-DBM-DILG No. 01, series of 1998; No.01-A dated March 14, 2000 and       No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001.
Likewise, instruct the Local School Board to strictly adhere with the Letter of Instruction No. 1462 series of 1985 as to the preparation of PPAs in the Annual School Board Budget and its utilization.
We recommended that LDRRMO prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month using the format hereto attached (Annex M).

We recommended that Management follow-up the release of the remaining budget with the DA to fully implement the Vermi Culture Production and Composting Project and facilitate the utilization of the project.

We recommend that Management:
Submit to the  Audit Team the documentary requirements as required under    Section 2 of  COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review ; and
Henceforth, require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to League of Municipalities  prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor, as the case maybe.

We recommended that Management through its SWMB develop/formulate a SWM Manual of Procedures and/or Implementing Rules and Procedures on SWM to properly monitor and review its SWM programs and activities.
We recommended that Management fully implement the project and immediately utilize the same to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the MSWMB strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the establishment of a sanitary landfill.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the previous Municipal
Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant in the substantiation of the
reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries in order to adjust
the cash in bank of the LGU.
It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash
in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted within 10 days
from receipt of Bank Statements. The non-submission of the required reports may
warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries in
the preceding year to reflect fairly the balances of the asset and liability accounts of
the local government unit.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the appropriate
asset for supplies, equipment and materials purchased out of the trust fund in order
to recognize the asset funded by other government agency for use by the LGU. Upon
full liquidation to the grantor, the assets should be closed to the liability account in
the Trust Fund and transferred to the General Fund by debiting the appropriate
asset account and credit to government equity.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to
the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to
set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at
the beginning of the year.
We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list
of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at
least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection
or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before
December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies
were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for
collection.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to
submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound
undertakings. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold
the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.
Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or
refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the previous Municipal
Accountant to assist the current designated Accountant in the identification of the
receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after
exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in
order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. Also,
require the MT/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant, which shall be used as basis in
recording issuance.
We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, or GSO as the case may
be, conduct physical count of inventory on the unutilized supplies and the Municipal
accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant support the Property, Plant
and Equipment accounts with subsidiary ledgers to establish the details of the
assets.
We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive issue an office order
creating an Inventory Committee and require them to conduct a complete physical
count of the property, plant and equipment of the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management adopt the 5% Residual Value for
Property, Plant and Equipment if a more appropriate percentage is not yet
determined.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant recompute the
depreciation as well as the accumulated depreciation in the preceding year and
effect the necessary adjustments to have a fair presentation of the financial
statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to furnish a copy of
the LDRRMF Investment Plan to the OIC Municipal Accountant for the
recording/adjustment of the unexpended CYs 2011-2016 Quick Response Fund and
DRRMF-MOOE as Trust Liability in the Trust Fund, and to the Budget Officer for
the recording of the DRRMF-Capital Outlay as continuing appropriation in the
SAAOB.
We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer issue check to actually set
aside and transfer to the Trust Fund the unexpended LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE)
for CY 2011-2016.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the contractors to correct and
repair the defects observed in the projects, otherwise forfeit the performance
security.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and deduct
liquidated damages on the final billing of the projects pursuant to Section 68 of the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management require the Property Custodian or the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit inventory reports of all insurable
properties to GSIS and request for computation to ensure that properties are
adequately covered with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management explain why the projects were provided in
generic terms and do not contain sufficient details and cost of the each project.
Henceforth, require from the Local Development Council a list of duly identified
projects in the formulation of future annual budgets.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management implements the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Plan of the municipality and allocate funds for the safe and sanitary
management of solid waste generated in the LGU pursuant to RA 9003.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management submit written justification on giving 13th
month pay and bonuses to personnel under job order and contract of service.
We also recommend the immediate discontinuance on the grant of the
aforesaid benefits.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors League of the
Philippines (VMLP)  Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top
of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the
activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning
local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the
basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts
contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP
meeting.
Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer to not accept the old receipts of the
leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National
Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and
registration fees.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to
transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing
bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).
We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau
of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not
compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department
Circular 001-2015.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management allocates at least 5% of the annual budget
for Gender and Development.
We also recommend that management submit the GAD Plan and Budget to
the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) for review and
endorsement pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular 2016-01 of the Philippine
Commission on Women, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor intensify the implementation of the projects funded by financial assistance in order to timely achieve the desired benefits.  Also make representation with the respective donors of the financial assistance with unutilized balances and seek authority to use the fund balance to finance other projects/programs of the agency.

We recommend that the Property/Supply Officer and the LGU Inspector to henceforth, accomplish the Pre and Post Inspection Reports to establish basis to determine the extent of repair to be done and to evaluate the attainment of the purpose of the repair performed on the Municipality’s equipment/property.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer comply the provisions of Circular No. 0213-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.

We recommend Management require the concerned Municipal employees to refund the cost of gasoline issued to privately owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.

We recommend that the LDRRMO submit the required monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to the COA Auditor within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO to adhere strictly to the provisions of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015 in authorizing overtime services and granting of overtime pay.  Further, require the officials who authorized the overtime including the concerned drivers to refund the amount of overtime pay.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% Development Fund be utilized to fund priority programs or projects which are capital in nature in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

1.6        We recommend that  Management  require  the  Accountant  to
    make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent  amount  of
    stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; and adhere to Section
    59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in  the  proper  treatment  of
    stale checks in the records of the Agency.

2.8     We recommended and Management agreed to appoint or designate  a
    GSO who shall specifically take custody and be accountable for all  the
    properties, real or personal, of the LGU and to  prepare  and  maintain
    stock cards and property cards for all its inventories and PPEs; create
    an inventory committee who shall perform physical inventory of all  the
    properties of the municipality and to prepare and submit to  the  Audit
    Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) pursuant  to  Section
    124 of NGAS Manual  for  LGU;  instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
    maintain and update Inventories and PPE Ledger Cards for each  category
    of inventory and PPE and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the
    GSO records; and adhere to the provisions on  property  management  set
    forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS  Manual,  Volume  1  and
    other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

3.6     We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Accountant
    to revert the accounting entry made by  debiting  Agricultural  Produce
    for Distribution account and crediting Due  to  NGAs  account  for  the
    subject amount.  Adhere to the provisions of Section 37(1) of P.D.  No.
    1445 and Section 54 of NGAS for LGU, Volume  1  in  dropping  from  the
    books the accounts subject for request for relief.

4.6     We recommended and the Management instructed the GSO to prepare
    IIRUP  for  approval  of  the  proper  authorities;  the  Committee  on
    Appraisal of  Property  appraises  those  properties  and  request  the
    disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and  regulation  to  prevent
    further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.

5.7     We recommended and Management agreed to designate  an  employee
    who shall  be  responsible  to  facilitate  the  LTO  registration  and
    insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality. Apply and/or
    renew the  registration  of  all  serviceable  motor  vehicles  of  the
    municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid
    additional expenses in terms of penalties and  payment  of  damages  to
    third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.

6.9     We recommended and Management agreed to stop  the  practice  of
    disbursing  government  funds  without  valid  and  proper   supporting
    documents. Further, Management shall utilize first  the  proceeds  from
    sale of bid documents and other related fees collected in  the  payment
    of honoraria to the members of the BAC, Technical Working Group and BAC
    Secretariat before sourcing out from the savings  of  the  Municipality
    since honoraria are not considered mandatory  expenditure  items  which
    will be given priority in the use of savings from Personal Services  of
    the Municipality.

7.7     We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the  Municipal
    Accountant and the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  transfer  the  unutilized
    balances of LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2016 from the General Fund  to  the
    Special Trust Fund in the total amount  of  P3,725,661.17in  accordance
    with Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12,  2012  and
    Item 6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No.  2013-1
    dated March 25, 2013.


7.8     We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant  agreed  to
    observe the requirements set forth  under  COA  Circular  No.  2012-002
    specifically the guidelines on the accounting and reporting  of  LDRRMF
    to ascertain that the financial information  on  LDRRMF  are  taken  up
    appropriately in the books and  properly  disclosed  in  the  Financial
    Statements.

8.8     We recommended and Management agreed to  strictly  adhere  with
    the prescribed laws, rules and regulations  in  the  documentations  of
    disbursements of government funds to ensure its validity and propriety.
    Further, require the BAC to submit all other documents that would prove
    the legitimacy of suppliers as authorized dealers.

9.7     We recommended and Management required the  Municipal  Engineer
    to ensure that all programs, projects and activities are provided  with
    the prescribed tarpaulin signboards with the project status in order to
    promote  good  governance  and  secure  the  right  of  the  people  to
    information on matters of public concern.

10.5     We  recommend  and  Management  agreed  to:  1)   effect   the
    corrections and recommendations by  the  EMB;  2)  once  the  plan  was
    approved by the EMB, submit its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan  to
    the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and  integration
    in the provincial solid waste management plan; 3) implement  the  rules
    and procedures on proper segregation,  collection,  transportation  and
    disposal of solid waste materials as prescribed in Sections 16, 17, 21,
    22, 23, 24, and 37 of the Act; and  4)  adhere  to  the  provisions  of
    Republic Act No. 9003 in the creation, implementation and monitoring of
    the LGU solid waste management.

11.5    We recommend and Management agrees that in the disbursement  of
    government  funds  the  Municipality  shall   provide   the   necessary
    supporting documents required on  COA  Circular  No.  2012-001  and  in
    compliance of Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.

12.7    We recommended and Management required the Municipal Accountant
    and Treasurer to strictly comply with the regulations relative  to  the
    submission of the monthly reports  and  supporting  documents  and  the
    Financial Statements to the Auditor  within  ten  (10)  days  from  the
    receipt  of  the  said  documents  to  ensure  the  timely  review  and
    verification of the accounts of the Municipality.

13.9    We recommended and the  Management  agreed  to  submit  to  the
    Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the attached  schedule.  All
    claims  shall  always  be  supported   with   complete   documentation;
    faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable  to  financial
    transactions  of  any  government  agency.  Submit   to   the   Auditor
    justification/explanation  on  the  occurrence   of   the   above-noted
    irregularities.   Failure  to  comply  may  cause  the  suspension   or
    disallowance of the transactions.

14.4    We recommended and Management agreed to make certain  that  all
    barangay shares from the Real Property collections are remitted to  the
    LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with  Section
    271(d) of RA 7160.

15.5  We recommended and Management  agreed  to:  (1)  Submit  to  the
      Auditor the above-mentioned documentary requirements.  Failure  to  do
      so may cause the suspension or disallowance of the  transactions;  and
      (2) Strictly adhere to the DILG MC No. 97-273 in the conduct of lakbay-
      aral or study tours.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions of
COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-001 in processing payments and liquidations for
cash advances to avoid suspensions/disallowances in qudit. We likewise recommend that
the Local Chief Executive require the officers responsible to coordinate and organize the
activities to be funded from Trust Fund to well facilitate its implementation and avoid
recurrence of this subject AOM.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Siorekeeper to update
PPE reports and require the Inventory Committee to regularly (at least every December
31 of the year) conduct an amnual physical count of all of the Municipality's Property,
Plant and Equipment and submit to the Office of the Auditor the related reports thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute measures to properly
dispose these obsolete and unserviceable properties amounting to P2,248,504.25
pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of R4 7160 and
in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive o require the General Services
Officer or the Storekeeper to ensure that all motor vehicles and transportation equipment
owned by the Municipality are permanently marked “For Official Use Only” (3 inches)
under which is the name and logo of the Local Government of Pilar. No government
Jfunds shall be utilized to provide fuel, parts, repair and maintenance to any government
vehicle without the said markings. Strict adherence to the mandates of existing laws,
rules and regulations on the use of government vehicles is likewise recommended to
avoid audit suspensions or disallowances.

We recommended that Management require:

1. the Municipal Accountamt to strictly comply with Section 6 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001;

2. the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions enumerated in RIRR of
RA 9184; and

3. the contractor to submit all the supporting documents noted above as
deficiencies in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-
002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the contractor to
complete the project as soon as possible and require the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that liquidated damages are deducted from the final payment of the contractor who fails
to complete projects within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Bids and Awards
Committee refrain from awarding infrastructure contracts unless the Road Right of Way
issue has been settled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

e the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with Land of the Philippines and
Department of Budget and Management central offices to verify the fund
source of the unknown deposit of P1,000,000.00. Once verified, secure
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) jor the utilization of the said deposit; and

o the Municipal Health Office (MHO) and Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Office (MSWDO) to submit work programs to Trust Fund
project-Focal Person to implement fund transferred from Other NGA’s.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
meticulously verify the status of long oulstanding fund transfers still maintained in its
books and require the Municipal Treasurer fo remit to the grantor-agency any unutilized
amount pursuant to their existing Memorandum of Agreement. If proven non-existing, the
Municipality to request write-off the said account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities
to be funded out of the LDRRMF. A detailed plan with specific PPAs should be prepared
and the unspent balance of ORF of previous years be treated as mitigation fund and not
as ORF of the next ensuing fiscal year. Further, management should strictly adhere with
the above cited guidelines.

We recommended the LCE fo require the District Supervisor to prepare the
budget of the school board concerned which shall be supported by the programs, projects
and activities of the LSB for the ensuing year.

We recommended that:

1. the Local School Board make proper planning with detailed listing of the
priority projects and activities in line with the SEF appropriation;

2. the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the allowed proper charges on the
SEF, likewise, refrain from certifying disbursemenis not related to SEF to
avoid possible issuance of COA audit suspension and/or disallowance; and

3. the Municipal Budget Officer assist the LSB in the proper charging of
appropriation during the preparation of the Local School Budget.

We recommended that Management require the Municipality Development
Council to update the CLUP in accordance with the approved Provincial Comprehensive
Land Use Plans as mandated by Executive Order (EO) No. 72.

We recommended that Management abide by the rules of PCW-DILG-NEDA-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 — 01 and other pertinent guidelines on
Gender and Development (GAD) to ensure that mandatory requirements and reports are
timely submitted and procedures in its submission are complied. Further, we recommend
that GAD Focal Person to prepare and maintain records on the detailed breakdown of
expenditures pertaining to GAD activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

1

the Municipal Budget/ Development Planning Qfficer to review carefully the
budger during its preparation. Do's and Don'ts in the 20 per cent
Development Fund Programs, Projects and Activities be identified selectively
so that non-priority expenses be not included in the priority projects of the
Municipality; and

the Municipal Accountant to refrain signing in the box A of disbursement
voucher form if obligations against 20 per cent Development Fund is not in
accordance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2611-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer:

a) make proper planning with detailed listing of the priority projects and
appropriation for individual project/activity; and

b ensure that the 20% Development Fund be expended only for specific and
prioritized projects listed in the Approved Annual Investment Program.

 Municipal Accountant prioritize the establishment of subsidiary ledgers for the respective accounts.
