observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of tax payers with the    amounts due and collectible and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to establish/draw a JEV to record in the Municipality’s books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts as required in Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs,   Vol. I.

We recommend strict compliance with Section 04(o) of Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Sections 2 to 5 of COA Circular No. 2003-007 dated December 11, 2003. For reference, the Table of Estimated Useful Life of PPE is hereto attached in   Annex C.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the OIC- Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the monthly authorized payroll deductions in the salaries of municipal officials and employees pursuant to Section 48 of General Provisions of the GAA for CY 2015.
We recommend that Management prosecute the immediate implementation of identified priority programs/projects/ activities funded from the 20% Development Fund.

Direct the concerned Municipal Officials to refund the project deficiency amounting P15,351.00; and

Require the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of FMR projects to ensure that the projects are implemented in accordance with POW, plans and specifications, and DPWH standards. Any deviation from POW during project implementation should be covered by revised POW and as-built plan.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant-designate to effect the immediate recording/adjustment on the books based on valid supporting documents, giving priority to long outstanding prior years’ reconciling items. Likewise, require the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current Municipal Accountant-designate in the substantiation of the reconciling items to warrant the preparation of the journal entries. It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted on time, as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015 Bank Reconciliation Statements were prepared and submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Documents to support the preparation of the necessary journal entries were not available/furnished to the current Accountant. (Reiterated) There are still reconciling items carried over from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to adjust the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable. We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the sanggunian concerned on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015 The Treasurer furnished a soft copy of the list of taxpayers with the assessed value of the real properties.
observations.reason: No RPT Receivable was set up in the beginning of the year for CY 2013- 2016. The list provided by the Treasurer has no breakdown as to the amount collectible for the current year and prior years. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize the asset as Construction in Progress and record back the liability account. Upon submission of full liquidation to the grantor, the assets in the Trust Fund be closed to equity or Due to LGUs and transferred to the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity. We further recommend that the succeeding expenditures in the Trust Fund be made in accordance with Sections 97 and 104 of the NGAS Volume 1, to avoid misstatements in the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Not implemented. Needs time to trace the trust fund transaction to effect the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: It will take time to trace prior years’ transactions since the currently designated Accountant has no data from the very beginning.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies using the Perpetual Inventory Method to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. For the unutilized inventories at year end, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a physical count and that the Municipal Accountant book the same as inventory balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2015 Inventory records were not adjusted. Management explained purchases are decentralized; the Municipal Treasurer has control of Accountable Forms. Management is amenable that the accounting records were not adjusted to reflect the correct balance at year-end.
observations.reason: Inaction by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant support the PPE accounts with subsidiary ledgers to establish the details of the assets. We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive issue an office order creating an Inventory Committee and require them to conduct a complete physical count of the property, plant and equipment of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2014, 2015 Attempts to conduct physical count were not completed. Management vowed to issue an order creating an inventory and disposal committee.
observations.reason: Not implemented. Inventory committee is not functional and absence of records. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the previous Municipal Accountant to assist the current designated Accountant in the identification of the receivables of the municipality. Otherwise, request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Management commented these are old accounts; predecessor’s reports have no details/breakdown.
observations.reason: Old accounts. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit justification why these properties remained in the Trust Fund in CY 2015 and onwards. We further recommend the immediate transfer of all completed property, plant and equipment to the General Fund upon liquidation to the grantor and discontinue the practice of recognizing depreciation expense in the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Not implemented. The Municipal Accountant indicated it needs to trace prior years’ transactions for the CY 2014 only; two projects CICL and a road were transferred to General Fund.
observations.reason: Partially implemented. Need to trace numerous transactions. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that any unobligated balance of the discretionary fund be transferred to the Office of the Local Chief Executive and that a supplemental budget, enumerating the specific projects, be prepared as identified by the Municipal Development Council. We further recommend that the local government unit discontinue the practice of allocating discretionary funds of the Sangguniang Bayan in its Annual Budget. Any similar unconstitutional methods and mechanisms should not be adopted in any system of governance, by any name or form, in compliance with the Supreme Court decision promulgated on November 19, 2014.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The practice of allocating discretionary funds of the Sangguniang Bayan in its Annual Budget was discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 An SB resolution was made to authorize the transfer of the UCPB accounts to the new DBP branch in Ubay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit justification on the incurrence of personal services in excess of the PS limitation. Otherwise, PEI in CY 2015 shall be disallowed in audit. We further recommend that management observe the 55% Personal Services cap/limitation in the LGU’s Annual and Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 325 (a) of R. A. 7160 in order to avoid payment of personnel benefits in excess of what is allowed by law and other pertinent guidelines.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 A notice of disallowance was issued for PEI 2015 and for settlement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184. We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties to dispel presumptions of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Bids and Awards Committee follows the provisions in RA 9184 throughout the procurement process.
observations.reason: Contracts are still awarded to lone bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive form a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prepare the SWM Plan of the municipality. We further recommend that funds be appropriated for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in the LGU pursuant to RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The SWM Plan was prepared and adopted; SWM Plan was approved by SB in December 2016.
observations.reason: Appropriation was not yet provided in CY 2016 budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and the Local School Board submit legal basis and justification in charging these expenses to the Special Education Fund. Otherwise, disbursements charged in violation of the guidelines will be disallowed in audit. We further recommend that funds allocated for Special Education Fund be budgeted and utilized in accordance with Section 272 of RA 7160 and the rules and regulations issued under the DECS (now Department of Education), DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B to effectively attain the purpose for which the fund was created and avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Submitted explanations; School supplies were no longer included in CY 2016 budget.
observations.reason: Compliance to a notice of disallowance was on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that succeeding cash advances be duly approved by two-thirds (2/3) of the Municipal Peace and Order Council (MPOC) pursuant to Sections 4.6 of COA-DBM-DILG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No cash advance for confidential fund was released in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require all officials and employees to discontinue the practice of using their RATA as collateral in loan agreements and instead utilize the allowances in the actual performance of their duties pursuant to Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The practice of using RATA as collateral in loan agreements was discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit justification on the irregularity in the release of the financial assistance. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to record the assistance as receivable and require liquidation from the recipient-barangays. We also recommend that the officials and employees of the municipality adhere to Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Submitted justification on the disbursements. A notice of disallowance amounting to P250,000.00 was issued for the irregular release of financial assistance; the amount was for settlement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend coordination between the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in the identification of all completed public infrastructure projects based on the transfers by the Municipal Accountant. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the registries of public infrastructures in preparation of the five-year transitional application for the recognition of the Public Infrastructures under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) application.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013, 2015 Submitted. The Municipal Assessor requested for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.
observations.reason: Difficulty in tracing projects since the currently designated Accountant has no data from the very beginning.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer fast track the transmittal of source documents to the Accounting Unit. Also, require the Accountant to submit monthly trial balances to the Auditor on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Monthly reports were submitted but not within the reglementary period. The accounting office submitted reports; late preparation of monthly trial balances due to late transmittal of documents from MTO.
observations.reason: Delayed transmittal of documents from MTO; late preparation of monthly trial balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of liquidation reports of all fund transfers made in prior years to the Carmen Health District, Inc., including the current year release of P100,000.00 for CY 2014 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Henceforth, fund transfers to NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Submitted liquidation reports were not provided; instead the financial statements of the CHDI were submitted.
observations.reason: The CHDI did not submit liquidation reports. Fund transfer from member-LGU was included in the general budget of the CHDI.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a written explanation on the necessity of the above-listed disbursements. If found unwarranted, a corresponding disallowance will be issued. We recommend that management discontinue the incurrence of excessive and unnecessary catering services for meals and snacks. Likewise, we recommend that expenses for food and snacks for meetings, session/gatherings must be charged to the monthly representation allowance of the department heads which is the very purpose of their allowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011, 2014 Submitted explanation on necessity of the disbursements. Management discontinued certain practices.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit an explanation on the irregularity observed in the procurement of goods which was paid by reimbursement. It is further recommended that management comply with the guidelines provided in the Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA 9184 and judiciously adhere to the streamlined process of procurement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Submitted explanation. Compliance to a notice of disallowance was on-going.
observations.reason: On-going compliance to notice of disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management discontinue the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis, except petty expenses to be paid out of the Petty Cash Fund. We further recommend that management use the alternative methods of procurement provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184, if public bidding is not feasible.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Minimized reimbursements. Management to follow alternative procurement methods when appropriate.
observations.reason: Not firmly implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned employees to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports pursuant to Sections 100 and 107 of PD 1445 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and other existing rules and regulations. We also recommend that management provide space for the audit team that will serve as a workplace during regular audit and a space to store DVs, supporting papers and other reports relative to the agency’s operations. Continued failure to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Submitted reports once generated and complete. Office space and storage room were not provided to Auditors; on-going construction of municipal hall.
observations.reason: Delayed transmittal of documents; no office space and storage room provided to auditors; on-going construction of municipal hall.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant-designate prepare the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013 and submit as soon as possible. It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2014 and its ensuing annual reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Submitted the AAPSI for AAR 2013.
observations.reason: Delayed preparation and submission of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Issue demand letters to all employees including retired/transferred employees to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately. For officers and employees who are still connected with the LGU, cause the withholding or suspension of salaries for non-compliance pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445. b. Request authority to write-off long outstanding cash advances which are deemed uncollectible and dormant, as provided in COA Resolution No. 80-122 dated April 23, 1980. c. Advise the Municipal Accountant to enforce strictly the prescribed rules and regulations in the handling of cash advances and the rendering of accounts and make journal entries to adjust the credit balances of the officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Demand letters were issued to officers and employees. The Municipal Accountant enforces rules and a journal entry has been made to adjust the credit balances of the officers and employees.
observations.reason: Several employees were no longer connected with the LGU. There is still an on-going payroll deduction for those with outstanding cash advances; need to determine which long outstanding cash advances are uncollectible/dormant to qualify for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant summarize all payrolls attributable to the constructed assets in the current year and prepare adjusting entries to capitalize these labor costs. Previous years’ projects should be traced and adjusted accordingly.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The Accounting Office capitalized labor costs attributable to the construction of assets for CY 2014; needs time to trace prior years’ transactions.
observations.reason: It will take time to trace prior years’ transactions for the construction of assets; the Accounting Office capitalized labour costs attributable to projects for CY 2014 only.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the appropriate asset account for the supplies, equipment and materials purchased out of the trust fund in order to recognize the asset funded by other government agency for use by the LGU. Upon liquidation to the grantor, the assets should be removed in the Trust Fund books and transfer them in the General Fund by debiting the appropriate asset account and credit to government equity.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 No management action. The designated Accountant is following the procedure of the previous Accountant.
observations.reason: Not implemented; the designated accountant is continuing the predecessor's practice.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict adherence to Section 4 of EO 298 dated March 23, 2004 specifically on the payment of lodging relating to itemized rates as required to conserve the meager funds of the LGU. Itemized rates as required should be complied to conserve the meager funds of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 There is a strict adherence to Section 4 EO 298 dated March 23, 2004. Travel claims are based on travel orders issued by the authorized signatories.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly and facilitate transfers and the liquidation of fund transfers from source agency specially those long outstanding/ dormant accounts. It is further recommended that adjusting entries be made on errors and misclassification discussed in the foregoing paragraphs. The municipal accountant should also monitor, reconcile and adjust the balances of the provincial and barangays share of RPT and CTC.
observations.managementAction: AAR Submitted explanations. Reconciliations and adjustments are on-going.
observations.reason: Submitted liquidation of some items; some long outstanding accounts still need reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Furnish the municipal accountant and the municipal treasurer with the copy of the amortization schedule. b. Take note of the due dates of the amortization payments for the CBRMP and LOGOFIND loans. c. Remit the amount due on or before the due dates to avoid the necessary penalties and require the concerned municipal officials to be vigilant in the payment of maturing obligations. d. Demand the immediate refund of the amount of penalties from the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 A final amortization schedule was furnished to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer. The LGU prepared vouchers before the due date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all collectors remit their collections intact daily to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Sections 15 and 29 of the NGAS. b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the use of accountable forms strictly by the person to whom it was issued. c. Transfer the collector of water fees under the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for the proper monitoring of his collections and prompt capture of the collection data.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 All collectors are directed to remit their collections daily. The Municipal Treasurer monitors the accountable forms; collection of water fees is already in MTO. An AF officer is designated in-charge in approving AF requested by the requesting officer and is recorded in the logbook.
observations.reason: There are still instances of delayed remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to viable and sustainable programs, projects and activities that directly generate jobs and livelihood opportunities to its constituents in accordance with Department of Budget and Management and Department of Interior and Local Government Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The new administration limits the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to viable and sustainable projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Ensure that only reasonable expenditures are incurred and prevent those that are excessive as contemplated under COA Circular No. 85-55-A. b. Adhere to the ongoing directive of adopting austerity measures in the government pursuant to Administrative Order No. 103.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The current administration exercise prudence in the disbursements of government funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to speed up the titling of these lands in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants. We further recommend that management conduct immediate follow-ups to facilitate the issuance of title in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants. If not possible, cancel the transaction and request for the return of the payment from the lot owner.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 With the Land Titling Program of the DENR, the Municipal Assessor has requested for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.
observations.reason: High cost in processing the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Develop a GAD Plan/Program designed to address gender issues within their concerned sectors or mandate and implement applicable provisions in the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women. b. Provide an allocation of at least 5% of the total budget for GAD related activities pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and the National Commission on the Role of the Filipino Women.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 A GAD Plan/Program was developed addressing gender issues within concerned sectors. For CY 2016, the GAD Plan includes funds provided by other government agencies.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, the GAD Budget is only 1% of the total annual budget. (Reiterated)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to replenish the General Fund with the amount of the SEF checks which were not cleared/re-deposited amounting to P52,687.00. b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry setting up the receivable (Due from Other Funds) and payable accounts (Due to Other Funds) in the General Fund and Special Education Fund respectively. c. Stop the practice of encashing checks out of its collections. d. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare regularly the Bank Reconciliation Statements. e. Provide COA copies of the pertinent documents relating to the re-deposit such as validated deposit slip and bank statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 No replenishment was made. The Accountant insisted that the check was already cleared by the bank but taken up as another deposit, resulting in understatement of Cash in Vault.
observations.reason: No replenishment was made; adjusted beginning balances for some accounts were established but other bank accounts were not reconciled; items were carried over from previous officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records at least quarterly. The Municipal Treasurer to see to it that funds are really available when he signs the certification. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly and provide COA copies of the pertinent documents relating to re-deposits.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Monthly transactions were reconciled for all bank accounts. Submitted reconciliations; beginning balances of certain accounts were adjusted.
observations.reason: Beginning balances were not reconciled since these were carried over from previous officers. The adjusted beginning balance of FCB-SEF has been established. However, other bank accounts were not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Stop from involving any employee in the collecting activities of the local government unit if he/she is not regularly appointed and officially designated agency personnel. b. Require all accountable officers to apply for the proper amount of fidelity bond in relation to the cash accountabilities so as to safeguard government funds and protect the interest of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Regular accountable officers are bonded. Some accountable officers are bonded; an on-going enforcement of requirements.
observations.reason: Some job order/casual employees are still allowed to perform collecting functions but under the supervision of a bonded employee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Conduct immediate follow-ups to facilitate the issuance of title in the name of the municipality to safeguard them from squatters and unlawful occupants. Otherwise, cancel the transaction and request for the return of the payment from the lot owner. b. Request for an SB Resolution confirming the purchase made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 The Municipal Assessor has requested the DENR for a survey of these properties to be granted with special patents.
observations.reason: No appropriation yet for the processing of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management facilitate the immediate registration of the unregistered vehicles and those with expired registration status with the Land Transportation Office including the ambulance and secure the plate number of the latter.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 All functional vehicles were registered with the LTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the accountant: a. To immediately prepare adjusting entries to show the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan accounts. b. In the succeeding amortization payments, review amounts in the billing statements before effecting payments and see to it that there is proper recording of the charges made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Needs further verification from MDFO to reflect the correct balance of the LOGOFIND and CBRMP loan. A final amortization schedule was furnished to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Further verification from MDFO is required; the LGU prepared the voucher before the due date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality comply all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment by proper management of solid wastes. The LGU officials should act for the closure of the open dumpsite and implement proper handling of solid wastes as authorized under the law.
observations.managementAction: The old dumpsite was already closed.  Recycling is encouraged by the municipality to minimize solid waste.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Special Education Fund be utilized for expenses that are authorized in accordance with the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 of the Department of Education, Culture and Sports, Department of Budget and Management and the Department of Interior and Local Government.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management through the GAD Focal Point System prepare the GAD Plan and Budget that support gender mainstreaming and submit the same to the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) for review and endorsement to DBM and ensure implementation of the programs, activities and projects that are gender responsive for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget is already indorsed by DILG.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned Municipal Mayor direct the persons responsible to settle immediately the amount suspended in audit amounting to P68,143.00 for the reimbursement of traveling expenses of the DCW and CDW during their learning development visit.
observations.managementAction: Documents were already submitted to support the amount suspended in audit.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the persons liable to settle immediately the amount disallowed in audit amounting to P5,250.00 for the payment of RATA to the Medical Doctor for the whole month of December 2015.
observations.managementAction: The amount disallowed has been settled by the person liable.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that contract documents for all procurement of infrastructure projects contain all the general and special conditions required of a contract and to provide protection to the rights of the LGU when matters relating to the poor or non-performance by the contractor occurs. 

We recommend further, that the municipality pursue forfeiture of performance bond in favor of the LGU in case the contractor falls short in performing its contractual obligations to the LGU for the construction of these projects.
observations.managementAction: At present, the municipality has adopted the proper form which includes the general and special conditions required of a contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management return to the National Treasury the unutilized / unexpended balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the same as unconstitutional.
observations.reason: There are still remaining PDAF balances not returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality strictly observe the pertinent provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG-DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual IRA for development projects and ensure that said fund is optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: The fund was utilized for various projects in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to reconcile the result of the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments thereof, preferably before the year ends so as not to reiterate this observation.  Further, cause the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers / cards which shall be kept for each class of PPE to record the description, acquisition, transfer/disposal and other information about the asset.
observations.managementAction: Physical count of PPE was conducted during assumption of new LCE by department but they have not yet started the reconciliation with the amount per books.
observations.reason: Need to consolidate the physical count of PPE, group by type of PPE, reconcile balances with books, and the maintenance of the subsidiary ledgers / cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to adopt measures to strengthen the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan.  Require the Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation of the merchandise inventory recorded in the books as against the actual and ensure the proper recording of the transactions / the receipt and disposition of the funds as well as the preparation of the periodic report on the financial status of the economic enterprise.
observations.managementAction: Management had stopped the operation of the bagsakan. Collection of receivables was deposited to Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: There is still merchandise inventory left in the books as of Dec. 31, 2016 in the total amount of P95,512.93.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to make representation to the Provincial Treasurer to confirm the validity and existence of the amount deposited and request available records as basis for the action to be taken by the agency in connection with the said deposit.
observations.managementAction: Management had made representation to the Provincial Treasurer inquiring the status of the amount.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer had already received the letter reply from the Provincial Treasurer and was informed that the account did not exist.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend that management should review and evaluate the list of KKK Fund loan grantees in the attached Annex A if it is still valid and collection is still possible. If found to be futile, request for the write off of the accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Since the loan was granted a long time ago, management had a difficulty in collecting the payments for the loans since most of grantees were not anymore living in municipality and some were already deceased.

Request for write-off of the said account.
observations.reason: Management requested for the write-off of the account since the possibility of collecting the loan payments is very remote considering that it was granted for more than 46 years ago.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should record cash advances for Intelligence Fund under the Due From Officers and Employees (account 123) pursuant to the above-cited provision. Likewise, monitor the liquidation of cash advances through constant follow-ups so that these could be immediately recorded in the agency books. Lastly, furnish the COA Auditor with a copy of all transmittal letters as required under Section 6 of COA Circular No. 2003-003.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation reports were already submitted to COA Central Office.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, total cash advance was P1,060,965.00.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should strictly follow the provisions of Sections 174 and 258 of R.A 7160 to enforce the collection of the delinquent real property tax receivables totaling P1,071,895.86.
observations.reason: Management informed that they had still difficulty in monitoring the delinquent taxpayers because of their manual system.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Sangguniang Bayan impose and implement the required taxes, fees and permits as mandated in the Revised Revenue Code and whenever applicable, consider the revision of the said code in order to impose rates which are responsive to the present economic situation considering that the same was enacted way back in CY 2006.
observations.reason: Ongoing revision of Local Revenue Code

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 7160 in the computation of PS limitation to avoid excess expenditures for Personal Services.
observations.reason: Excess in PS limitation for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections in her possession to the authorized government depository bank to safeguard government funds from risks of loss.
observations.managementAction: Collections were deposited but not regularly and intact.
observations.reason: Considering the location of the municipality and changing weather conditions, they explained that it is impossible to deposit their collection in accordance to the regulations which is daily.  This same observation is reiterated in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

 Notify the public through signboards of their projects at the project location and adhere to the other provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

 Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the undersigned the list of ongoing projects/programs/activities for the 4th quarter of CY 2013.
observations.managementAction: Completed quarterly report required was not submitted for CY 2016.
observations.reason: Despite quarterly follow ups, report with complete data was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should require the Municipal Livestock Coordinator, who is in – charge of the dispersal program of the municipality, to monitor the project, prepare and submit a report regularly to the Accounting Division any increase in the livestock inventory due to returns from recipients as well as the mortality duly supported with an affidavit and witnessed by any of the barangay officials to ensure the reliability, accuracy and validity of  the recorded amount in the financial statement.
observations.reason: No physical inventory conducted and no report submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management the following:

1. Require the Accountant to book up the receivable of P264,201.00 to correct the understatement of income account for waterworks. 
2. Intensify collection of receivable by requiring the collectors to meet certain targets.  
3. Require the collectors to submit monthly report of receivables to be used as basis for the recording of receivables in the books.
4. Review and investigate causes for the non collection of monthly dues and adopt measures to correct them.
5. Enforce disconnection of delinquent consumers if warranted as provided in the Municipal Ordinance 2013-05.
6. Ensure safekeeping of the subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The LCE has directed to intensify collection of receivables and to secure the subsidiary ledgers of water consumers.
observations.reason: Per verification as of December 2016, the receivable from waterworks operations still remained unrecorded in the books. Subsidiary ledgers remained not securely placed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents with covering transmittal letter pertaining to the cash advances granted for confidential fund in a manner prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001 and strictly adhere to the intent of the provisions enshrined in the said Joint Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Management had stopped granting cash advances for Confidential Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed not to appropriate IF in their annual budget. LGUs are entitled only to CF. Support the annual budget with the detailed Peace and Order Program and present it in the budget as a non-office expense alongside with the 30% confidential fund; appropriate confidential fund which does not exceed 30% of the LGU’s total POP. Ensure that cash advances granted for CF shall not exceed the budgeted amount; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 to ensure proper budgeting, utilization and reporting of the confidential fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management had stopped granting cash advances for Confidential Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to approve payments only for expenses authorized under Section 272 of R.A. 7160, DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01 and 01-A and other governing rules and regulation on SEF. Only the allowed expenses under the law shall be charged against the SEF. Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation for non-compliance; submit to the Auditor documents as proof that salaries paid to the JOs for handling classes were for teaching extension, non-formal, remedial and summer classes duly approved by the DepEd authorities; ensure that all financial assistance granted to various schools shall be supported with complete documentations like the POW, program/activity design or breakdown of expenses, official receipts, etc. Instruct all concerned school principals/heads to make a representation before their higher officers/officials to provide the former with official receipts to support the amount received; advise the Municipal Treasurer to issue the check in the name of the school or the designation/position of the agency head; submit to the Auditor the required/lacking documents mentioned in the audit recommendation numbers 1, 2 and 3, as well as the lacking documents mentioned in the attached schedule marked as Annex A. Failure to comply may result to suspension or disallowance of the corresponding transactions; and faithfully adhere to the provisions stated above to guarantee legit and efficient use of the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The Management had stopped granting cash advances for Confidential Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply and adhere to the terms and conditions embodied in an agreement covering a project to ensure its total implementation. This assures that there will be no losses of government resources and the people will benefit therefrom; conduct competitive bidding to all procurement of the Agency as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184. Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; and submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the above discussion as well as the justification on the occurrence of the irregularities. Non-compliance thereof may cause suspension or disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Management already stopped charging transactions from prior year’s to current year’s appropriation
observations.reason: Charging of prior year’s transactions to current year’s appropriations already minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to immediately return the restituted PDAF amounting to P3,000,000.00 to the coffer of the national government through its deposit in the account of the BTr.
observations.managementAction: The amount disallowed was already returned and remitted to the Bureau of Treasury

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management conduct competitive bidding in all its procurement as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184. Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; attach the required documents to the payments where alternative mode of procurement was used, instead of competitive bidding, due to failure of bidding; and submit the lacking documents to the Auditor. Failure to comply may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Management already conducted competitive bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop the practice of charging against the 20% development fund the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures; refrain from charging against the fund the expenditures which do not have corresponding appropriation to comply with Section 4.1 of P.D. No. 1445; provide the Auditor with computation as basis for the amount contributed to KALAHI-CIDDS-NCDDP amounting to P1,700,000.00; submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the irregularities noted in Nos. 1 and 2. Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal use.
observations.managementAction: The Management already stopped charging transactions from prior year’s to current year’s appropriation
observations.reason: Required documents were not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the accountable officers to immediately submit to the Accounting Office liquidation of their cash advances. Failure to comply may cause the filing of appropriate charges against the accountable officer pursuant to Sections 9.3.2 and 9.3.3 of the above-mentioned COA Circular; prohibit the granting of additional cash advance to the accountable officer if his/her previous accountabilities had not been liquidated or a proper accounting thereof had been made; remind the accountable officers to always settle their cash advances within the required period or at the end of each year; and strictly observe the rules, laws and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Liquidations were already submitted to the Office of the Accountant and a memorandum was issued requiring the Accountable Officers to settle their obligations.
observations.reason: There are still unliquidated cash advances granted from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the appropriation for LDRRMF have full cash back-up to have ample fund to defray disaster-related expenses in time of emergencies; allocate 30% of the LDRRMF to QRF as stand-by fund for relief and recovery programs; accrue and transfer the unexpended portion of the LDRRMF to a special trust fund in compliance with Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121; post the financial reports on the utilization of the LDRRMF in the bulletin boards of the municipal building and in other conspicuous places reserved for the purpose per requirement of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-141; prepare and submit to the Auditor, as well as to other oversight government agencies, the utilization report of the fund in a manner prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002; utilize properly and efficiently the assets of the government in accordance with plans and programs; and strictly adhere to R.A. No. 10121 and other related rules and regulations in the creation, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Postings was made to the bulletin of the Municipality
observations.reason: Postings made were not updated, unexpended balance was still not transferred to a special trust fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from contracting loans for general purposes. Proceeds from loans should be utilized for infrastructure and other socio-economic development projects and to stabilize local finances as stated in Sections 296 of R.A. No. 7160; submit to the Auditor justification/comment on the occurrence of the above irregularities. Failure to do so may cause suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to Sections 296 and 3 of R.A. No. 7160 and E.O. No. 723, respectively, in entering into credit finances.
observations.managementAction: The loan was already fully paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant- Designate to stop receiving collections from the job order. Such function should be given either to the revenue collection officer, assistant municipal treasurer or to the municipal treasurer; advise the Municipal Accountant- Designate to personally prepare and maintain the BRS and financial statements. Assistance from the treasury and budget departments should be limited to tutorial and oral instructions only; and adhere to the provision on internal control especially on the segregation of duties and responsibilities between budget, accounting and treasury.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were already implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to formulate and include only GAD PPAs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB and GAR in a manner and in prescribed forms to comply Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC; submit to the Auditor justification/explanation why the management incurred expenses that are not in accordance with the objectives of the GAD fund, as well as documents to prove that the PPAs indeed addressed emerging gender issues and concerns. Failure to comply may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.
observations.managementAction: PPAs included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive.
observations.reason: Most of the PPAs included in the GAD Plan are still not supportive of the government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to advise the contractors to fast track the implementation of the projects, programs and activities to facilitate speedy completion thereof and improve performance rates. As to PPAs administered by the Municipality, effect implementation timeliness to serve as guide in monitoring the status of project and enhance monitoring activities in order to effect prompt attainment of the program.
observations.managementAction: PAPs under 20% Development Fund were implemented.
observations.reason: Not all PAPs identified in the Annual Investment Plan were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS by reconciling balances in the bank statements with the balances in the general or subsidiary ledgers as required by Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011; instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit BRS, including the paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memos, to the Audit Team within 10 days after the end of each month; advise the Municipal Accountant to always draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and corrections in the general or subsidiary ledgers; and adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the preparation and submission of BRS.
observations.managementAction: BRS were already prepared and updated as of December 31, 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to liquidate immediately with the Accounting Section the unsettled cash advances; revert the accounting entry made in booking up the CIF liquidation amounting to P869,000.00; and strictly observe the provisions set forth in COA Circular Nos. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Agency directed the Municipal Accountant and to effect the required liquidation/settlement of cash advances.
observations.reason: Some AOs already settled and liquidated their accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to prepare and maintain general and subsidiary ledgers pursuant to the provisions enshrined in P.D. 1445 to facilitate verification of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: GL and SL prepared and updated as of December 31, 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed to ensure that service contracts must contain the attestation clause and the duties and responsibilities to be performed, duly signed by two instrumental witnesses, before a hired employee under job order could render services. A copy of the service contracts must also be issued to and received by the employee concerned; seek SB authority/resolution allowing the Municipal Mayor to incur obligation in paying the wages of the job orders; and revised the rates given to the job orders, from fixed monthly rate to hourly, daily or piecework rate basis.
observations.managementAction: Job Order prepared.
observations.reason: Recommendation of the Auditor not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required financial statements and reports to the Audit Team in a manner and frequency required in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and Chapter 4 of NGAS Manual for LGU.
observations.managementAction: Reports were prepared monthly.
observations.reason: Accounts submitted to the Office of the Auditor but with no FS for the period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that Agency, through the Local Government Unit GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), formulate and include programs, projects and activities that would only address women’s human rights and gender discrimination in the government policies, programs and activities, in a manner prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: PAPs prepared related to GAD
observations.reason: Most of the PAPs prepared are not gender responsive.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to utilize the programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development and Trust Funds through proper planning and timing of execution and coordination among LGU officials and employees to ensure implementation of the same; fast track the implementation of the PPAs to facilitate speedy completion thereof and improve performance rates; and effect implementation timeliness to serve as guide in monitoring the status of project and enhance monitoring activities in order to effect prompt attainment of the program.
observations.managementAction: PPAs are implemented for 20% Development and Trust Funds.
observations.reason: PPAs were implemented but delayed

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of charging expenses to the 20% Development Fund which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The Municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The management only charge authorized expenses to 20% Development Fund and within the annual school board budget.
observations.reason: ineligible charges to 20% Development Fund were already minimized.
